LINKEDIN OUTBOUND (30-day sequence): - ICP definition and search filters (Sales Navigator boolean strings) - Connection request message (under 300 chars, 3 variations) - Day 1: Connection accepted → value-first message (no pitch) - Day 3: Share relevant content/case study - Day 7: Soft problem-awareness question - Day 14: Direct pitch with social proof - Day 21: Breakup message with value leave-behind - Daily activity targets (connections, messages, content posts) - Response rate benchmarks and optimization triggers - Tools: Sales Navigator, Dripify/Expandi automation rules 2. CONTENT MARKETING (weekly cadence): - Content pillars (4-5 themes mapped to buyer journey) - Weekly calendar: Monday (blog post), Wednesday (LinkedIn article), Friday (newsletter) - SEO content template (H1/H2 structure, internal linking, CTA placement) - Content repurposing workflow (blog → social → email → video) - Distribution checklist per piece (social, communities, syndication) - KPIs: organic traffic, time on page, conversion rate by content type - 90-day content ramp plan with specific topic ideas 3. GOOGLE ADS (account structure): - Campaign structure (brand, competitor, category, long-tail) - Keyword research framework with example keywords for this business - Ad copy templates (3 RSAs per ad group, pinned headlines) - Landing page requirements (message match, single CTA, trust signals) - Budget allocation by campaign type (% split) - Bidding strategy progression (manual CPC → target CPA → target ROAS) - Negative keyword seed list, quality score optimization 4. EMAIL COLD OUTREACH (5-email sequence): - Email 1 (Day 0): Pattern interrupt subject line, personalized opening, one-line value prop, soft CTA - Email 2 (Day 3): Reply to thread, case study/social proof, specific metric - Email 3 (Day 7): New thread, different angle (pain point focus), video embed - Email 4 (Day 12): Competitor comparison or industry insight, harder CTA - Email 5 (Day 18): Breakup email, summary of value, open door - Subject line formulas (5 templates per email) - Deliverability setup (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, warm-up schedule) - Tools and sending limits 5. PRODUCT HUNT LAUNCH (day-by-day timeline): - T-30: Build hunter relationship, collect testimonials, prepare assets - T-14: Create teaser landing page, build launch day email list - T-7: Final asset prep (logo, gallery images, video, description) - T-1: Schedule social posts, brief supporters, final check - Launch Day hour-by-hour: midnight posting, morning push, afternoon engagement, evening gratitude - Post-launch: follow-up with upvoters, blog post recap, press outreach - Asset specifications (thumbnail 240x240, gallery 1270x760, video <2min) Include specific metrics, benchmarks, and budget requirements for each channel.`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['CHANNEL-PLAYBOOKS.md'] = `# Channel Playbooks — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('channels', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('channels', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('channels', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Design a complete referral program for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Revenue model: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}. Cover: 1. INCENTIVE STRUCTURE (dual-sided): - Referrer reward options: credit, cash, extended subscription, feature unlock, tiered rewards (1 referral = X, 5 = Y, 10 = Z) - Referee reward: discount on first purchase/month, extended trial, bonus feature - Cost-per-acquisition analysis vs other channels - Break-even calculation for incentive amounts - Tiered/milestone rewards (Bronze 1-2, Silver 3-5, Gold 6-10, Platinum 11+) 2. VIRAL COEFFICIENT CALCULATION: - Formula: K = invites_per_user × conversion_rate - Current baseline assumptions - Target K-factor (>1 for viral growth) - Sensitivity analysis: how changes in invite rate and conversion rate affect K - Time-to-virality calculation (viral cycle time) - Realistic benchmarks by industry 3. REFERRAL TRACKING IMPLEMENTATION: - Unique referral link generation (format: ${inf.name.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g,'-')}.com/ref/USERNAME) - Cookie duration (90 days recommended) - Attribution logic (first-touch vs last-touch) - Multi-channel tracking (link, code, email) - Database schema for referral tracking (referrer_id, referee_id, status, reward_status, created_at, converted_at) - Anti-fraud measures (IP dedup, email domain checks, minimum activity threshold) - Webhook integration for reward fulfillment 4. EMAIL TEMPLATES (5 templates): - Invitation email (from referrer to friend) - Referral accepted notification (to referrer) - Reward earned notification (to referrer) - Welcome email to referred user (highlighting special offer) - Milestone reached congratulation (tier upgrade) - Subject lines, preview text, and full body copy for each 5. LANDING PAGE COPY: - Headline and subheadline variations (3 each) - How it works section (3 steps with icons) - Reward explanation with visuals - FAQ section (6-8 common questions) - Social proof section (referral stats, testimonials) - CTA button copy variations 6. A/B TEST PLAN: - Test 1: Incentive amount (low vs medium vs high) - Test 2: Incentive type (credit vs cash vs feature) - Test 3: Dual-sided vs referrer-only rewards - Test 4: Landing page headline variations - Test 5: Email subject line variations - Sample size calculator, statistical significance threshold (95%), test duration estimates Include implementation timeline (4 weeks) and success metrics.`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['REFERRAL-PROGRAM.md'] = `# Referral Program Design — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('referral', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('referral', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('referral', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive launch checklist for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Revenue model: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}. Structure as three phases: **PRE-LAUNCH (T-30 to T-1):** T-30 to T-21: - [ ] Finalize MVP feature set and freeze scope - [ ] Set up analytics (PostHog/Plausible events, conversion funnels) - [ ] Create waitlist landing page with email capture - [ ] Begin private beta with 10-20 users, collect feedback - [ ] Draft press kit (company backgrounder, founder bios, product screenshots, logo pack) - [ ] Identify 50 journalists/bloggers covering ${inf.type_label} space - [ ] Set up social media accounts (Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram) - [ ] Create Product Hunt upcoming page T-20 to T-11: - [ ] Incorporate beta feedback into product - [ ] Write launch blog post (origin story + what we built + who it's for) - [ ] Record product demo video (2 min max) - [ ] Create social media content calendar for launch week (15+ posts) - [ ] Prepare email sequences: waitlist announcement, launch day, follow-up - [ ] Build relationships with Product Hunt hunters - [ ] Set up customer support (help docs, chat widget, support email) - [ ] Create onboarding flow for new users T-10 to T-1: - [ ] Final QA pass (all critical flows, mobile responsive, cross-browser) - [ ] Load testing (handle 10x expected launch traffic) - [ ] Prepare Product Hunt assets (thumbnail, gallery, tagline, description, first comment) - [ ] Schedule all social media posts - [ ] Brief launch team on roles and timeline - [ ] Send "launching soon" email to waitlist - [ ] Set up real-time monitoring dashboard (uptime, errors, signups) - [ ] Prepare incident response plan (who to call if things break) **LAUNCH DAY (hour-by-hour):** - 00:01 — Submit to Product Hunt (or hunter posts) - 06:00 — Send launch email to full waitlist - 07:00 — Post on Twitter/X with product demo GIF - 08:00 — Share on LinkedIn (personal + company page) - 09:00 — Post in relevant communities (Reddit, Indie Hackers, HN) - 10:00 — Respond to all Product Hunt comments - 12:00 — Midday social media push (results so far, social proof) - 14:00 — Send personalized messages to journalists/bloggers - 16:00 — Engagement push (reply to every comment, thank supporters) - 18:00 — Share milestone update (signups, upvotes, feedback highlights) - 20:00 — Evening gratitude post, behind-the-scenes content - 22:00 — Final metrics check, team debrief, plan for Day 2 **POST-LAUNCH WEEK 1:** Day 1-2: - [ ] Respond to ALL feedback (Product Hunt, social, email) within 4 hours - [ ] Track key metrics: signups, activation rate, first-session duration, NPS - [ ] Fix any critical bugs discovered during launch - [ ] Send follow-up email to non-converting signups Day 3-5: - [ ] Publish launch retrospective blog post (metrics, lessons, what's next) - [ ] Follow up with journalists who showed interest - [ ] Analyze acquisition channel performance (which drove most signups) - [ ] Begin user interviews with first 20 paying customers - [ ] Submit to startup directories (20+ directories listed) Day 6-7: - [ ] Week 1 metrics report (vs. targets) - [ ] Prioritize feature requests from launch feedback - [ ] Plan week 2 content calendar - [ ] Set up automated onboarding email sequence based on user behavior - [ ] Team retrospective: what worked, what didn't, what to do differently Include target metrics for each phase and contingency plans.`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['LAUNCH-CHECKLIST.md'] = `# Launch Checklist — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('launch', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('launch', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('launch', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive churn reduction playbook for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Revenue model: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}. Cover: 1. CHURN TAXONOMY: Voluntary churn reasons: - Price sensitivity — perceive cost too high relative to value received - Value gap — not achieving expected outcomes, underusing features - Competitor switch — found alternative that better fits needs - Need dissolved — business pivot, project ended, role changed - Poor experience — bugs, slow support, UX friction Involuntary churn reasons: - Card decline — expired card, insufficient funds, fraud block - Billing error — incorrect charge, duplicate billing - Payment method issues — bank policy, international card restrictions 2. SAVE FLOW SCRIPTS (for each voluntary reason): Price objection script: acknowledge → reframe value → offer annual discount → offer downgrade → last resort offer Value gap script: acknowledge → diagnose usage → offer onboarding session → create success plan → 30-day check-in Competitor script: acknowledge → ask what's better → match/beat feature → highlight switching costs → offer migration help Need dissolved script: acknowledge → offer pause (up to 3 months) → offer downgrade to minimal plan → graceful exit with reactivation offer Poor experience script: acknowledge + apologize → escalate specific issue → offer credit → assign dedicated support → follow-up timeline Each script should include exact phrases, tone guidance, and escalation paths. 3. CANCELLATION SURVEY: - Primary reason (single select, 6 options matching taxonomy) - Secondary factors (multi-select, 10 options) - Feature most valued (open text) - Feature most missing (open text) - Would you return if [condition]? (single select) - NPS score at cancellation - Permission to follow up (yes/no) - Implementation: in-app modal before confirming cancellation 4. WIN-BACK CAMPAIGN (3-email sequence): Email 1 (Day 3 post-cancel): "We miss you" — acknowledge departure, share what's new/improved, no hard sell Email 2 (Day 14 post-cancel): "Here's what you're missing" — new features since cancellation, customer success story, special return offer (20% off 3 months) Email 3 (Day 30 post-cancel): "Last chance" — final offer (30% off or free month), deadline urgency, one-click reactivation link Include subject lines (3 variations each), preview text, full body copy, CTA buttons. 5. INVOLUNTARY CHURN RECOVERY (dunning sequence): - Pre-dunning: notify 7 days before card expiry - Failed payment Day 0: immediate retry + email notification (update payment method link) - Day 3: second retry + email with urgency (service will be limited) - Day 7: third retry + email warning (account will be paused) - Day 10: final retry + SMS notification if available - Day 14: account paused (not deleted), final email with 30-day reactivation window - Day 44: account data archived, notification of deletion in 30 days - Smart retry logic: retry on different days of week, retry at different times, retry after paycheck dates (1st and 15th) 6. CHURN METRICS DASHBOARD: - Monthly churn rate (gross and net) - Churn by reason (pie chart) - Revenue impact by churn reason - Save rate by script/offer - Win-back conversion rate - Involuntary recovery rate - Cohort retention curves - Leading indicators (usage drop, support tickets, login frequency decline) Include churn rate benchmarks for ${inf.type_label} businesses and reduction targets.`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['CHURN-REDUCTION.md'] = `# Churn Reduction Playbook — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('churn', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('churn', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('churn', 'sk', e.message); } })() ]); // ── Group G — People & Culture (4 docs) ──────────── addP('culture', 'Culture Document', '', '⬡ People Docs'); setP('culture', 'run'); addP('equity', 'Equity Guide', '', '⬡ People Docs'); setP('equity', 'run'); addP('perf', 'Performance Framework', '', '⬡ People Docs'); setP('perf', 'run'); addP('remote', 'Remote Work Policy', '', '⬡ People Docs'); setP('remote', 'run'); const [cultureR, equityR, perfR, remoteR] = await Promise.allSettled([ (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive company culture document for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Cover: 1. COMPANY VALUES (5-7 values): For each value provide: - Value name (concise, memorable) - One-sentence definition - "This means we..." (3 behavioral examples of living the value) - "This does NOT mean..." (2 anti-patterns to avoid) - Interview question to assess for this value - Recognition example (how to celebrate someone demonstrating this value) 2. CULTURE DOCUMENT TEMPLATE: - Our mission (why we exist beyond making money) - Our vision (what the world looks like if we succeed) - Who we are (team identity, what makes us different) - How we work (operating principles) - What we believe about our customers - What we believe about our craft 3. DECISION-MAKING PRINCIPLES: - Decision-making framework (RAPID: Recommend, Agree, Perform, Input, Decide) - When to decide alone vs. consult vs. consensus - Reversible vs. irreversible decisions (Type 1 vs Type 2) - Disagree and commit principle - Decision documentation template (context, options considered, decision, rationale) 4. COMMUNICATION NORMS: - Async-first communication (default to written, meetings are escalation) - Channel guide: what goes in Slack vs. email vs. docs vs. meetings - Response time expectations by channel (Slack: 4hrs, email: 24hrs, urgent: 1hr) - Writing norms (clear, concise, provide context, bottom-line-up-front) - No-surprise rule (bad news travels fast, share early) 5. MEETING CADENCE: - Daily standup (15 min, async option via Slack bot) - Weekly team sync (30 min, agenda required) - Monthly all-hands (45 min, metrics + wins + Q&A) - Quarterly planning (half-day, OKR review and setting) - Annual offsite (2-3 days, strategy + team building) - Meeting hygiene rules (agenda required, notes published, default 25/50 min) 6. FEEDBACK CULTURE: - Radical candor framework (care personally + challenge directly) - Feedback format: SBI (Situation, Behavior, Impact) - Regular 1:1s (weekly, 30 min, employee-driven agenda) - Skip-level 1:1s (monthly) - Anonymous feedback channel (quarterly pulse surveys) - How to give feedback up (to managers and leadership) 7. DIVERSITY, EQUITY & INCLUSION COMMITMENT: - Statement of commitment - Inclusive hiring practices (structured interviews, diverse panels, blind resume review) - ERG (Employee Resource Group) support - Pay equity review process (annual audit) - Inclusive language guide - Accommodation policy - Reporting and accountability mechanisms Make this feel authentic to a ${inf.type_label} startup, not generic corporate boilerplate.`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['CULTURE.md'] = `# Culture & Values — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('culture', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('culture', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('culture', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate an employee equity education guide for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Revenue model: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}. Cover: 1. STOCK OPTIONS vs RSUs: - Stock options: right to buy shares at a set price (strike/exercise price) - You profit only if company value exceeds strike price - Require action (exercise) and cost money upfront - Common at early-stage startups - RSUs (Restricted Stock Units): promise of shares at no cost once vested - Always have value (as long as shares have value) - No action or cost required — shares delivered automatically - More common at later-stage/public companies - Side-by-side comparison table (cost, risk, tax timing, employee preference) - When each type makes sense for the company and employee 2. VESTING EXPLAINED: - Standard 4-year vesting with 1-year cliff - Month-by-month vesting after cliff - Example timeline: 10,000 shares granted - Month 0-11: 0 shares vested (cliff period) - Month 12: 2,500 shares vest (25% cliff) - Month 13-48: ~208 shares/month - Month 48: fully vested - Acceleration clauses: single-trigger (change of control) vs double-trigger (CoC + termination) - What happens when you leave (vested vs unvested, exercise window) 3. EXERCISE WINDOWS: - Standard 90-day post-termination exercise period (and why it's a problem) - Extended exercise windows (trending: 7-10 years post-departure) - Early exercise (83(b) election): what it is, when it makes sense, 30-day filing deadline - Cashless exercise at IPO/acquisition - Exercise strategies: exercise and hold vs. exercise and sell vs. wait 4. TAX IMPLICATIONS: - ISOs (Incentive Stock Options): - No tax at grant or exercise (for regular tax) - AMT implications at exercise (spread is AMT preference item) - Long-term capital gains if held 2 years from grant + 1 year from exercise - Disqualifying disposition rules - NSOs (Non-Qualified Stock Options): - No tax at grant - Ordinary income tax at exercise (on spread) - Employer withholding at exercise - Capital gains/losses on subsequent sale - RSU taxation: - Taxed as ordinary income at vesting (FMV at vest date) - Employer withholds taxes at vesting - Capital gains on appreciation after vesting - Tax comparison table with examples at different valuations - When to consult a tax advisor (always, but especially when...) 5. 409A VALUATION: - What it is (IRS-required fair market value determination) - Why it matters (sets your strike price) - How it's determined (independent appraisal, typically annually or after material events) - Why the 409A is usually lower than the preferred price investors paid - Common share vs preferred share price difference explained - Impact on your equity value (lower 409A = lower strike = better for employees) 6. HOW TO THINK ABOUT EQUITY VALUE: - Never count on equity as guaranteed compensation - Equity is a lottery ticket with asymmetric upside - Framework: expected value = probability of outcome × value at outcome - Scenario modeling: base case, bull case, bear case - Dilution: what it is, how future funding rounds affect your ownership % - Liquidation preferences: why common shareholders get paid last - Fully-diluted share count: why your % matters more than share count - Rule of thumb: equity should be a bonus, not a substitute for fair salary 7. COMMON EMPLOYEE QUESTIONS ANSWERED: - "How much is my equity worth?" (it depends — here's how to think about it) - "Should I exercise my options before I leave?" (decision framework) - "What happens to my equity if the company gets acquired?" (scenarios) - "Can I sell my shares before an IPO?" (secondary markets, ROFR) - "What if the company never goes public?" (tender offers, buybacks, staying private) - "Should I negotiate for more equity or more salary?" (depends on risk tolerance and stage) - "What's the difference between my option grant and actual ownership?" (fully diluted) - "Do I need a financial advisor?" (yes if exercising >$10K or AMT exposure) Include practical examples with specific dollar amounts throughout.`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['EQUITY-GUIDE.md'] = `# Employee Equity Guide — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('equity', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('equity', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('equity', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive performance management framework for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Cover: 1. REVIEW CADENCE: - Quarterly performance reviews (full cycle) - Monthly 1:1 check-ins (lightweight, employee-driven) - Weekly standup updates (team visibility) - Annual compensation review (tied to Q4 performance review) - Timeline: Q1 review (Apr), Q2 review (Jul), Q3 review (Oct), Q4 review + annual comp (Jan) - Who reviews whom: direct manager primary, skip-level input, peer feedback (360) 2. EVALUATION CRITERIA TEMPLATE: - Core competencies (applicable to all roles): - Execution & delivery (quality, speed, reliability) - Collaboration & communication - Problem-solving & initiative - Growth mindset & learning - Alignment with company values - Role-specific competencies (examples for engineering, sales, marketing, ops) - Rating scale: 1-5 (Does Not Meet, Partially Meets, Meets, Exceeds, Exceptional) - Rating descriptions with behavioral anchors for each level - Distribution guidance (not forced ranking, but calibration reference) 3. SELF-ASSESSMENT FORM: - Key accomplishments this quarter (3-5, with measurable impact) - Goals from last quarter — status and learnings - Challenges faced and how you addressed them - Areas where you want to grow - Feedback for your manager (what's helping, what could change) - Goals for next quarter (3-5, SMART format) - Career development interests (skills, roles, projects) - Overall self-rating with justification 4. MANAGER ASSESSMENT FORM: - Performance against goals (rated 1-5 per goal) - Competency ratings (1-5 per competency with written justification) - Key strengths demonstrated this quarter (specific examples) - Development areas with actionable suggestions - Overall performance rating (1-5 with calibration note) - Promotion readiness assessment (not ready / developing / ready now) - Compensation recommendation (if applicable) - Specific goals and expectations for next quarter 5. CALIBRATION PROCESS: - Purpose: ensure consistency across teams and managers - Participants: all managers at same level + their skip-level - Preparation: managers bring ratings and justification for all direct reports - Process: review by rating bucket, discuss edge cases, adjust for consistency - Rules: no discussing personality, only behaviors and outcomes - Output: calibrated ratings, identified high-performers, identified needs-support - Timeline: 1 week after manager assessments due, before delivery to employees - Bias checks: gender, tenure, team, recency bias mitigation 6. PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN (PIP) TEMPLATE: - When to initiate (sustained "Does Not Meet" rating, specific incident, pattern of issues) - PIP document sections: - Current performance concerns (specific, documented examples) - Expected performance standards (measurable, achievable) - Support and resources provided (training, mentoring, reduced workload) - Measurable milestones with specific due dates (30/60/90 day) - Check-in cadence (weekly during PIP) - Consequences of not meeting expectations - Employee acknowledgment and response - PIP best practices: document everything, be direct but respectful, involve HR, give genuine path to success - PIP outcome options: successful completion, extension, role change, separation 7. PROMOTION CRITERIA: - Already performing at next level for 1+ quarter - Demonstrates scope and impact beyond current role - Peer and cross-functional recognition - Skills matrix: what's required at each level (IC1-IC6, M1-M3) - Promotion packet template (evidence of readiness, manager recommendation, peer endorsements) - Promotion committee process (who decides, what they review) 8. COMPENSATION REVIEW: - Annual review tied to Q4 performance cycle - Market data comparison (using Levels.fyi, Pave, Radford) - Merit increase bands by rating (Meets: 3-5%, Exceeds: 6-8%, Exceptional: 9-12%) - Equity refresh grants (criteria and typical amounts by level) - Off-cycle adjustment criteria (promotion, market correction, retention risk) - Total compensation communication template`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['PERFORMANCE-FRAMEWORK.md'] = `# Performance Management Framework — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('perf', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('perf', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('perf', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive remote work policy for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Cover: 1. REMOTE/HYBRID/IN-PERSON DECISION FRAMEWORK: - Decision criteria matrix: role type, collaboration needs, client requirements, employee preference - Three modes defined: - Fully remote: no office requirement, work from anywhere - Hybrid: X days in office per week (flexible which days) - In-person: role requires physical presence (with justification) - Per-role designation process (manager + HR determine, employee can request change) - Relocation policy: approved states/countries, tax implications notice, cost-of-living adjustment policy - International remote work: visa/tax considerations, maximum days per country 2. COMMUNICATION NORMS (async-first): - Default to async: write it down before scheduling a meeting - Tool hierarchy: - Slack: quick questions, social, real-time when needed (not urgent by default) - Email: external communication, formal decisions, long-form - Notion/Docs: specifications, decisions, knowledge base - Video calls: brainstorming, relationship building, complex discussions - Response time expectations: - Slack: within 4 business hours (not instant) - Email: within 24 business hours - @urgent tag: within 1 hour during business hours - After hours: no expectation of response until next business day - Over-communication encouraged: share context, explain decisions, document everything - Status indicators: use Slack status to show availability (focus time, OOO, in meetings) 3. MEETING POLICY: - Maximum meeting hours: 15 hours/week per person - No-meeting days: Tuesday and Thursday (company-wide focus days) - Meeting hygiene: - Every meeting needs an agenda (shared 24h in advance) - Default to 25 min or 50 min (not 30/60) - Notes and action items documented and shared within 24 hours - Optional attendees clearly marked (they should decline if possible) - Recurring meetings reviewed monthly (cancel if not valuable) - Camera policy: on for 1:1s and small groups, optional for all-hands - Meeting-free weeks: one week per quarter (no internal meetings) 4. TIME ZONE MANAGEMENT: - Core collaboration hours: 10am-2pm ET (4 hours of overlap) - Meetings scheduled only during core hours unless all parties agree - Team time zone map maintained and visible - Calendar etiquette: display working hours, block personal time - Handoff practices for distributed teams (async standup, written updates) - Fair rotation of inconvenient meeting times across time zones 5. HOME OFFICE STIPEND: - One-time setup stipend: $1,000 for desk, chair, monitor, peripherals - Monthly internet/utilities stipend: $100/month - Approved equipment list (ergonomic recommendations) - Reimbursement process: submit receipts, approved within 1 week - Equipment ownership: company-funded items returned if leaving within 1 year - Refresh cycle: additional $500 annually for upgrades/replacements 6. CO-WORKING BUDGET: - Monthly co-working allowance: $200/month (WeWork, local spaces) - Booking process: use preferred vendors or submit for reimbursement - Team co-working days encouraged (team lead coordinates, company pays) - Coffee shop work: $50/month food & beverage allowance when working from cafes 7. IN-PERSON GATHERING CADENCE: - Quarterly team gatherings (2-3 days, company-funded travel + lodging) - Annual company offsite (3-5 days, all employees, strategy + team building) - Budget per gathering: $2,000-3,000 per person (travel, hotel, activities, meals) - Planning lead time: 8 weeks minimum, 12 weeks for annual offsite - Gathering format: 50% work sessions, 30% team building, 20% unstructured - Virtual inclusion: if someone can't travel, provide high-quality virtual participation 8. WELLNESS & BOUNDARIES: - Right to disconnect: no work communications expected outside business hours - Flexible hours within core collaboration window - Mental health days (no questions asked, 2 per quarter) - Annual wellness stipend: $500 (gym, meditation app, therapy copay) - Burnout prevention: manager training, workload monitoring, mandatory PTO minimums`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['REMOTE-WORK-POLICY.md'] = `# Remote Work Policy — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('remote', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('remote', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('remote', 'sk', e.message); } })() ]); // ── Group H — Product (2 docs) ──────────── addP('analytics', 'Analytics Instrumentation', '', '⬡ Product Docs'); setP('analytics', 'run'); addP('a11y', 'Accessibility Plan', '', '⬡ Product Docs'); setP('a11y', 'run'); const [analyticsR, a11yR] = await Promise.allSettled([ (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive analytics instrumentation plan for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Revenue model: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}. Cover: 1. EVENT TAXONOMY: Organize events into four categories with naming convention (object_action format): Page Views: - page_viewed (properties: path, referrer, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign) - page_scrolled (properties: path, scroll_depth_percent, time_on_page) Feature Usage Events: - feature_used (properties: feature_name, feature_category, duration_seconds) - search_performed (properties: query, results_count, result_clicked) - filter_applied (properties: filter_type, filter_value, results_count) - export_initiated (properties: export_format, item_count) - settings_changed (properties: setting_name, old_value, new_value) Funnel Events: - signup_started (properties: source, referrer) - signup_completed (properties: method: email/google/github, time_to_complete) - onboarding_step_completed (properties: step_number, step_name, time_spent) - onboarding_completed (properties: total_time, steps_skipped) - trial_started (properties: plan_type, source) - upgrade_initiated (properties: from_plan, to_plan, trigger) - checkout_started (properties: plan, price, coupon_applied) - payment_completed (properties: plan, amount, payment_method, is_first_payment) - subscription_cancelled (properties: plan, reason, tenure_days) Business Events: - revenue_recognized (properties: amount, plan, mrr_change_type: new/expansion/contraction/churn) - invitation_sent (properties: invite_method, inviter_plan) - invitation_accepted (properties: time_to_accept) - support_ticket_created (properties: category, priority) - nps_submitted (properties: score, comment_provided) 2. POSTHOG TRACKING IMPLEMENTATION: - Installation (npm package + script tag options) - Initialization with project API key - User identification (posthog.identify with user properties) - Group analytics (company/workspace level tracking) - Event tracking code examples for each category above - Feature flags integration (linking flags to events) - Session recording setup (privacy controls, sampling rate) - Autocapture configuration (what to enable/disable) 3. DATA LAYER SPECIFICATION: - dataLayer structure for Google Tag Manager compatibility - User properties schema (plan, role, company_size, signup_date, lifecycle_stage) - Group/company properties schema (industry, employee_count, plan, mrr) - Event property standards (naming convention, required vs optional, data types) - PII handling rules (what to track, what to hash, what to never capture) - Consent-aware tracking (check consent before firing events) 4. DASHBOARD WIREFRAMES (5 dashboards): Dashboard 1 — Executive Overview: - MRR (current, trend, vs target) - Active users (DAU, WAU, MAU with trends) - Trial-to-paid conversion rate (funnel visualization) - Net revenue retention (monthly) - NPS score (current + trend) - Top acquisition sources (pie chart) Dashboard 2 — Acquisition: - Visitors by source/medium (stacked bar, weekly) - Signup funnel (visitors → signup started → signup completed → activated) - Cost per acquisition by channel - Landing page conversion rates (table with trends) - UTM campaign performance Dashboard 3 — Activation & Onboarding: - Onboarding funnel (step-by-step drop-off) - Time to first value action (distribution) - Activation rate by cohort (weekly cohorts) - Feature adoption in first 7 days (heatmap) - Onboarding completion rate (with/without each step) Dashboard 4 — Engagement & Retention: - DAU/MAU ratio (stickiness, trend) - Feature usage frequency (top 10 features, bar chart) - Retention curves (weekly cohort, 8-week view) - Power users vs casual users (segmentation) - Session duration distribution - Churn risk score distribution Dashboard 5 — Revenue: - MRR waterfall (new, expansion, contraction, churn) - ARPU trend (by plan, by cohort) - Plan distribution (pie chart) - Upgrade/downgrade rates - Lifetime value by acquisition source - Revenue churn vs logo churn 5. ALERT THRESHOLDS: - Error rate spike: >1% of requests return 5xx → Slack alert - Signup drop: <50% of 7-day average daily signups → Slack + email alert - Payment failure spike: >5% failure rate in 1 hour → urgent Slack + SMS - Conversion rate drop: trial-to-paid below 2% (7-day rolling) → weekly report flag - Feature adoption anomaly: any core feature usage drops >30% week-over-week → Slack alert - Uptime: any 5-minute period with >10% error rate → PagerDuty - Revenue alert: MRR drops >5% in a day → urgent notification to founders`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['ANALYTICS-INSTRUMENTATION.md'] = `# Analytics Instrumentation Plan — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('analytics', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('analytics', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('analytics', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive accessibility plan for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). ${desc} Cover: 1. WCAG 2.1 AA CHECKLIST organized by principle: PERCEIVABLE: - [ ] 1.1.1 Non-text content: all images have alt text (descriptive for meaningful, empty for decorative) - [ ] 1.2.1 Audio/video: captions for pre-recorded media - [ ] 1.2.2 Captions: synchronized captions for all video content - [ ] 1.3.1 Info and relationships: proper heading hierarchy (H1→H2→H3), semantic HTML (nav, main, aside, footer) - [ ] 1.3.2 Meaningful sequence: DOM order matches visual order - [ ] 1.3.3 Sensory characteristics: instructions don't rely solely on shape, color, size, location - [ ] 1.4.1 Use of color: color is not the only means of conveying information - [ ] 1.4.2 Audio control: mechanism to pause/stop audio that plays >3 seconds - [ ] 1.4.3 Contrast minimum: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text (18px+ bold or 24px+) - [ ] 1.4.4 Resize text: page readable at 200% zoom without horizontal scrolling - [ ] 1.4.5 Images of text: use real text instead of images of text - [ ] 1.4.10 Reflow: content reflows at 320px width without horizontal scroll - [ ] 1.4.11 Non-text contrast: 3:1 for UI components and graphical objects OPERABLE: - [ ] 2.1.1 Keyboard: all functionality available via keyboard - [ ] 2.1.2 No keyboard trap: focus can always be moved away from any component - [ ] 2.2.1 Timing adjustable: time limits can be extended or turned off - [ ] 2.3.1 Three flashes: no content flashes more than 3 times per second - [ ] 2.4.1 Skip navigation: "skip to main content" link as first focusable element - [ ] 2.4.2 Page titled: descriptive, unique page titles - [ ] 2.4.3 Focus order: logical tab order matching visual layout - [ ] 2.4.4 Link purpose: link text is descriptive (no "click here") - [ ] 2.4.6 Headings and labels: descriptive headings and form labels - [ ] 2.4.7 Focus visible: clear visible focus indicator on all interactive elements - [ ] 2.5.1 Pointer gestures: multipoint/path-based gestures have single-pointer alternatives UNDERSTANDABLE: - [ ] 3.1.1 Language of page: lang attribute on HTML element - [ ] 3.1.2 Language of parts: lang attribute for content in different language - [ ] 3.2.1 On focus: no unexpected context changes on focus - [ ] 3.2.2 On input: no unexpected context changes on input (unless user informed) - [ ] 3.3.1 Error identification: errors clearly described in text - [ ] 3.3.2 Labels or instructions: form fields have visible labels and instructions - [ ] 3.3.3 Error suggestion: specific correction suggestions when input errors detected - [ ] 3.3.4 Error prevention: confirmation, reversibility, or review for legal/financial submissions ROBUST: - [ ] 4.1.1 Parsing: valid HTML (no duplicate IDs, proper nesting) - [ ] 4.1.2 Name, role, value: custom components expose correct ARIA roles and states - [ ] 4.1.3 Status messages: dynamic content updates announced via ARIA live regions 2. AUTOMATED TESTING SETUP (axe-core): - Installation: npm install @axe-core/react axe-core - Development integration: axe-core React integration (console warnings in dev) - CI/CD integration: axe-core CLI or Playwright axe plugin in test pipeline - Configuration: custom rules (disable false positives, add project-specific rules) - Pre-commit hook: run axe on changed components - Example test code for Playwright + axe integration - Reporting: generate HTML accessibility reports per build - Severity classification: critical (blocks usage), serious (major barrier), moderate (degraded experience), minor (annoyance) 3. MANUAL TESTING GUIDE: - Keyboard-only testing protocol (30-minute test script): - Navigate entire site using only Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space, Escape, Arrow keys - Verify all interactive elements are reachable and operable - Check focus visibility throughout - Test modal dialogs (focus trap, Escape to close) - Test dropdown menus, tooltips, accordions - Screen reader testing: - Test with NVDA (Windows, free) and VoiceOver (macOS, built-in) - Test critical user flows: signup, login, main feature, checkout - Verify: page landmarks announced, headings navigable, form labels read, error messages announced, dynamic content updates announced - Document screen reader behavior for each component - Color and contrast testing: - Use browser high contrast mode - Test with color blindness simulators (protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia) - Verify contrast ratios with WebAIM Contrast Checker - Zoom and reflow testing: - Test at 200% and 400% zoom - Test on 320px viewport width - Verify no content is cut off or overlapping - Motion sensitivity testing: - Verify prefers-reduced-motion media query is respected - Test with animations disabled 4. REMEDIATION PRIORITY MATRIX: | Priority | Impact | Effort | Examples | Timeline | |----------|--------|--------|----------|----------| | P0 Critical | Blocks access | Any | Missing form labels, keyboard traps, no alt text on key images | Fix immediately | | P1 High | Major barrier | Low-Med | Poor contrast, missing skip nav, focus not visible | Fix within 2 weeks | | P2 Medium | Degraded experience | Medium | Missing ARIA landmarks, decorative images with alt text | Fix within 1 month | | P3 Low | Minor annoyance | High | Suboptimal heading hierarchy, verbose alt text | Fix within 1 quarter | 5. ACCESSIBILITY STATEMENT TEMPLATE: - Commitment statement - Standard targeted (WCAG 2.1 Level AA) - Current conformance status (partial/full) - Known limitations with planned remediation dates - Feedback mechanism (email, form, phone) - Response commitment (acknowledge within 2 business days, resolve within 10) - Enforcement procedure reference - Date of last assessment - Third-party audit information (if applicable) Include links to tools, resources, and training materials.`, 5000); if (raw?.length > 500) { files['ACCESSIBILITY-PLAN.md'] = `# Accessibility Plan — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('a11y', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('a11y', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('a11y', 'sk', e.message); } })() ]); // ── END: Legal + GTM + People + Product docs ──────────── // ── MEDIUM-PRIORITY: Remaining 30 documents (30/78) ──────────── // Batch 1: Financial & Strategic Planning (6 docs) addP('valuation_model', 'Valuation Model', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('scenario_plan', 'Scenario Planning', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('partnership_strat', 'Partnership Strategy', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('negotiation_guide', 'Negotiation Guide', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('governance', 'Governance Framework', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('investor_update', 'Investor Update Template', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); setP('valuation_model', 'run'); setP('scenario_plan', 'run'); setP('partnership_strat', 'run'); setP('negotiation_guide', 'run'); setP('governance', 'run'); setP('investor_update', 'run'); await Promise.allSettled([ (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Generate a DCF valuation model framework for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}, ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}). Include: 5-year revenue projections with growth assumptions, COGS and operating expense forecasts, free cash flow calculation, WACC computation (cost of equity via CAPM, cost of debt, capital structure), terminal value using both perpetuity growth and exit multiple methods, comparable company analysis with 5 relevant public comps and multiples (EV/Revenue, EV/EBITDA), sensitivity tables for discount rate vs growth rate, and pre-money valuation range. Use realistic assumptions for a ${inf.type_label} business. Format as actionable spreadsheet-ready tables.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['VALUATION-MODEL.md'] = `# Valuation Model — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('valuation_model', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('valuation_model', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('valuation_model', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create scenario planning for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Define three scenarios: BULL (everything works, market tailwinds), BASE (realistic execution), BEAR (headwinds, slow adoption). For each scenario include: narrative description, trigger events that signal this scenario is unfolding, key metrics and thresholds, revenue impact at 12/24/36 months, required team size, cash runway implications, and strategic response playbook. Add a monitoring dashboard of 5 leading indicators with red/yellow/green thresholds. Include contingency pivots for the bear case and acceleration tactics for the bull case.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['SCENARIO-PLANNING.md'] = `# Scenario Planning — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('scenario_plan', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('scenario_plan', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('scenario_plan', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Write a partnership strategy for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Cover: partner identification methodology (adjacent markets, complementary products, distribution channels), evaluation criteria matrix (reach, brand alignment, technical fit, revenue potential scored 1-5), partnership tiers (referral, integration, co-sell, strategic), deal structure templates for each tier (rev share %, exclusivity, term length), outreach sequence (intro, value prop, proposal, negotiation), partnership onboarding checklist, joint KPIs and reporting cadence, and exit/termination clauses. List 5 specific partner categories relevant to this business type.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['PARTNERSHIP-STRATEGY.md'] = `# Partnership Strategy — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('partnership_strat', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('partnership_strat', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('partnership_strat', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a negotiation guide for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Cover BATNA framework (how to identify and strengthen your best alternative), ZOPA identification, and anchoring techniques. Include specific playbooks for: vendor negotiations (SaaS tools, contractors, office), investor negotiations (valuation, terms, board seats, pro-rata), enterprise sales (procurement, legal, security review, pricing), and hiring (offer structuring, equity negotiation). For each playbook: opening position, concession strategy, walk-away points, and common traps. Add a preparation checklist and post-negotiation review template.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['NEGOTIATION-GUIDE.md'] = `# Negotiation Guide — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('negotiation_guide', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('negotiation_guide', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('negotiation_guide', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a governance framework for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Include: recommended board composition by stage (pre-seed through Series B — number of seats, independent vs investor vs founder), meeting cadence and agenda templates, committee structure (audit, compensation, nominating), fiduciary duties explained plainly, board information rights and reporting packages, observer rights, consent matters requiring board approval, D&O insurance requirements, advisory board structure and compensation (0.1-0.5% equity), and governance evolution timeline as the company scales.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['GOVERNANCE.md'] = `# Governance Framework — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('governance', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('governance', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('governance', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a monthly investor update template for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}, ${inf.revenue_model}). Structure: TL;DR (3 bullets), Highlights (top 3 wins), Lowlights (top 3 challenges with mitigation), KPI dashboard (MRR, growth rate, CAC, LTV, churn, runway, burn, NPS — with month-over-month deltas), Product update (shipped, in-progress, next), Team update (hires, open roles, org changes), Financial summary (revenue, expenses, cash position, months of runway), The Ask (specific help needed from investors — intros, advice, hiring), and a closing note. Keep format scannable — investors spend 2 minutes reading.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['INVESTOR-UPDATE-TEMPLATE.md'] = `# Investor Update Template — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('investor_update', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('investor_update', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('investor_update', 'sk', e.message); } })() ]); // Batch 2: Reporting, Pitch & Growth Models (6 docs) addP('board_deck', 'Board Deck Template', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('weekly_metrics', 'Weekly Metrics', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('pitch_script', 'Pitch Script', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('network_fx', 'Network Effects', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('marketplace_strat', 'Marketplace Strategy', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('fin_controls', 'Financial Controls', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); setP('board_deck', 'run'); setP('weekly_metrics', 'run'); setP('pitch_script', 'run'); setP('network_fx', 'run'); setP('marketplace_strat', 'run'); setP('fin_controls', 'run'); await Promise.allSettled([ (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a quarterly board deck template for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Slides: 1) Agenda & key decisions needed, 2) Executive summary (3 bullets), 3) Financial overview (revenue, burn, runway with charts), 4) KPI dashboard (core metrics vs targets), 5) Product roadmap (shipped, building, planned), 6) Go-to-market update (pipeline, conversion, CAC trends), 7) Team & hiring (org chart, open roles, culture), 8) Competitive landscape (market shifts), 9) Risk register (top 5 risks with mitigation), 10) Strategic decisions for board vote, 11) Appendix. For each slide specify: data to include, visualization type, and talking points. Keep deck to 20 minutes presentation time.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['BOARD-DECK-TEMPLATE.md'] = `# Board Deck Template — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('board_deck', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('board_deck', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('board_deck', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a weekly metrics dashboard for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}, ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}). Define: Tier 1 metrics (check daily — revenue, signups, activation rate), Tier 2 metrics (check weekly — retention, NPS, support tickets, feature adoption), Tier 3 metrics (check monthly — LTV, CAC, payback period). For each metric: definition, data source, target range, alert threshold. Include a weekly standup reporting format (Slack-friendly), a Monday metrics email template, and a weekly review meeting agenda (30 min). Add a scorecard template with red/yellow/green status for each metric and week-over-week trend arrows.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['WEEKLY-METRICS.md'] = `# Weekly Metrics Dashboard — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('weekly_metrics', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('weekly_metrics', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('weekly_metrics', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Write a 3-minute scripted pitch for "${inf.name}": ${desc}. Type: ${inf.type_label}, revenue model: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}. Include timing marks [0:00-0:30] Hook — attention-grabbing problem statement, [0:30-1:00] Problem — pain points with data, [1:00-1:30] Solution — product demo moment, [1:30-2:00] Traction — metrics and social proof, [2:00-2:30] Business model — unit economics, [2:30-3:00] Ask — specific amount, use of funds, closing. Add [PAUSE] marks for emphasis, [SLIDE] cues, audience adaptation notes for investors vs customers vs partners, and common Q&A responses for 5 likely questions.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['PITCH-SCRIPT.md'] = `# Pitch Script — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('pitch_script', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('pitch_script', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('pitch_script', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Analyze network effects for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Classify which types apply: direct (more users = more value per user), indirect/cross-side (two-sided marketplace effects), data (more usage = better product via ML/personalization), platform (third-party integrations/ecosystem). For each applicable type: mechanism description, critical mass estimate, metrics to track (e.g., liquidity, DAU/MAU ratio, cross-side ratio), strategies to reach tipping point, defensibility once achieved. Include viral coefficient calculation framework (invites sent × conversion rate), network effect mapping diagram description, and comparison to analogous companies that achieved strong network effects.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['NETWORK-EFFECTS.md'] = `# Network Effects Analysis — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('network_fx', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('network_fx', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('network_fx', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a marketplace strategy for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Cover the chicken-and-egg problem: which side to seed first and why, supply-side acquisition tactics (concierge, scraping, partnerships), demand-side acquisition tactics (SEO, content, paid). Define liquidity metrics: search-to-fill rate, time-to-match, utilization rate. Include geographic density strategy (single city → metro expansion → national), pricing strategy (subsidize which side, take rate evolution), quality curation (vetting, reviews, guarantees), disintermediation prevention, and marketplace maturity stages with different strategies for each stage. Add competitive moat analysis specific to marketplace dynamics.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['MARKETPLACE-STRATEGY.md'] = `# Marketplace Strategy — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('marketplace_strat', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('marketplace_strat', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('marketplace_strat', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create financial controls for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Cover: separation of duties (who initiates, approves, records transactions), approval authority matrix by amount ($0-500, $500-5K, $5K-25K, $25K+), expense policy (categories, per diems, reimbursement process, prohibited expenses), corporate card controls (per-transaction limits, MCC restrictions), bank account controls (dual signatures above threshold, reconciliation cadence), payroll controls, vendor onboarding and payment process, month-end close checklist, audit preparation guide, and document retention schedule. Include templates for expense reports, purchase orders, and check requests. Scale guidance from startup to 50-person company.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['FINANCIAL-CONTROLS.md'] = `# Financial Controls — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('fin_controls', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('fin_controls', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('fin_controls', 'sk', e.message); } })() ]); // Batch 3: Finance Models, Community & Content (6 docs) addP('dilution_model', 'Dilution Model', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('three_stmt', 'Three-Statement Model', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('community_strat', 'Community Strategy', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('content_mktg', 'Content Marketing Plan', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('jtbd_guide', 'JTBD Interview Guide', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('product_strat', 'Product Strategy', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); setP('dilution_model', 'run'); setP('three_stmt', 'run'); setP('community_strat', 'run'); setP('content_mktg', 'run'); setP('jtbd_guide', 'run'); setP('product_strat', 'run'); await Promise.allSettled([ (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a dilution model for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Model ownership across funding rounds: Founding (100%), Pre-seed ($500K at $3M), Seed ($2M at $10M), Series A ($8M at $40M), Series B ($25M at $150M). For each round show: pre-money valuation, amount raised, post-money, new shares issued, founder ownership %, employee pool %, investor ownership % per round. Include option pool creation (10-20%) and its dilutive effect, anti-dilution provisions (weighted average vs full ratchet) with worked examples, and pro-rata rights impact. Add a cap table template and SAFE/convertible note conversion scenarios.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['DILUTION-MODEL.md'] = `# Dilution Model — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('dilution_model', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('dilution_model', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('dilution_model', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a three-statement financial model for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}, ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}). BALANCE SHEET — cash, AR, prepaid, fixed assets, total assets; AP, accrued liabilities, deferred revenue, debt, equity, total L&E. CASH FLOW STATEMENT — operating (net income + adjustments), investing (capex, acquisitions), financing (equity raises, debt). Show quarterly for Year 1, annual for Years 2-3. Include key assumptions table and unit economics tie-in.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['THREE-STATEMENT-MODEL.md'] = `# Three-Statement Financial Model — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('three_stmt', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('three_stmt', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('three_stmt', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a community strategy for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Include specific topic ideas for the first 3 months.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['CONTENT-MARKETING-PLAN.md'] = `# Content Marketing Plan — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('content_mktg', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('content_mktg', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('content_mktg', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a Jobs-to-be-Done interview guide for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Budget: $500-2000/quarter.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['USER-RESEARCH-PLAN.md'] = `# User Research Plan — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('user_research', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('user_research', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('user_research', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a tech debt management framework for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Define debt categories: code debt (copy-paste, dead code, complex functions), architecture debt (monolith coupling, missing abstractions), infrastructure debt (manual deploys, no monitoring), testing debt (low coverage, flaky tests), documentation debt. Severity scoring matrix: impact (1-5) × likelihood of causing incident (1-5) = priority score. Sprint allocation policy (20% of capacity for debt reduction). Debt register template with columns: ID, description, category, severity, estimated effort, business impact, date identified, owner, status. Include a quarterly debt audit process, definition of "acceptable debt," and decision framework for when to incur intentional debt (shipping deadline tradeoffs).`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['TECH-DEBT-FRAMEWORK.md'] = `# Tech Debt Framework — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('tech_debt', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('tech_debt', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('tech_debt', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create an internationalization strategy for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). GDPR Article 28 compliant format.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['docs/DPA-TEMPLATE.md'] = `# Data Processing Agreement — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('dpa_template', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('dpa_template', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('dpa_template', 'sk', e.message); } })() ]); // Batch 5: HR, Operations & Strategic Assessment (6 docs) addP('onboarding_ck', 'Onboarding Checklist', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('comp_bench', 'Compensation Benchmarks', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('supply_chain', 'Supply Chain', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('rev_recog', 'Revenue Recognition', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('cust_journey', 'Customer Journey Map', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); addP('zero_to_one', 'Zero-to-One Assessment', '', '⬡ Phase 7b'); setP('onboarding_ck', 'run'); setP('comp_bench', 'run'); setP('supply_chain', 'run'); setP('rev_recog', 'run'); setP('cust_journey', 'run'); setP('zero_to_one', 'run'); await Promise.allSettled([ (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create an employee onboarding checklist for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Include systems access checklist (email, Slack, GitHub, Notion, HRIS, etc.), equipment list, and onboarding feedback survey.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['ONBOARDING-CHECKLIST.md'] = `# Onboarding Checklist — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('onboarding_ck', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('onboarding_ck', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('onboarding_ck', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create compensation benchmarks for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). For each role: base salary range (25th/50th/75th percentile), equity range (% for early stage, # shares for later), total compensation calculation methodology. Reference 3 data sources (Levels.fyi, Pave, Carta). Include geographic adjustment factors (SF=1.0, NYC=0.95, Austin=0.80, remote=0.85), stage adjustments (pre-seed through Series B), equity refresh policy, bonus structure, and benefits package comparison (health, 401k, PTO, parental leave). Add a compensation philosophy statement template.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['COMPENSATION-BENCHMARKS.md'] = `# Compensation Benchmarks — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('comp_bench', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('comp_bench', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('comp_bench', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a supply chain strategy for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). If physical products apply, include: supplier evaluation criteria (quality, lead time, MOQ, payment terms), logistics partners, inventory management (JIT vs safety stock), demand forecasting methodology, warehouse/fulfillment strategy (3PL vs in-house), shipping carrier comparison, returns process, and cost optimization. Include risk mitigation for supply disruptions and a vendor scorecard template.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['SUPPLY-CHAIN.md'] = `# Supply Chain Strategy — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('supply_chain', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('supply_chain', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('supply_chain', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a revenue recognition guide for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}, ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}). Cover: ASC 606 five-step model (identify contract, identify performance obligations, determine transaction price, allocate to obligations, recognize when satisfied), specific recognition rules for this business model — SaaS (ratably over subscription period), one-time sales (at delivery), professional services (over time or milestone), usage-based (as consumed). Include: deferred revenue accounting (liability until earned), contract modifications, refund reserves, multi-element arrangements, free trial and freemium treatment, annual vs monthly billing differences, and practical examples with journal entries. Add a monthly revenue recognition checklist and common audit findings to avoid.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['REVENUE-RECOGNITION.md'] = `# Revenue Recognition — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('rev_recog', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('rev_recog', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('rev_recog', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a customer journey map for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}, ${inf.revenue_model}). Map 7 stages with details for each: 1) AWARENESS — channels, first touchpoints, emotions, content that attracts; 2) CONSIDERATION — evaluation criteria, competitor comparison, content that educates; 3) PURCHASE — friction points, conversion triggers, checkout optimization; 4) ONBOARDING — first 7 days experience, activation milestones, welcome sequence; 5) ADOPTION — feature discovery, habit formation, time-to-value; 6) EXPANSION — upsell triggers, cross-sell opportunities, referral program; 7) ADVOCACY — review solicitation, case study creation, community participation. For each stage: customer actions, touchpoints, emotions (😊→😤), pain points, opportunities, KPIs, and responsible team. Include a journey map visualization template.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['CUSTOMER-JOURNEY-MAP.md'] = `# Customer Journey Map — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('cust_journey', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('cust_journey', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('cust_journey', 'sk', e.message); } })(), (async () => { try { const raw = await aiRaw(`Create a Zero-to-One assessment for "${inf.name}": ${desc}. Apply Peter Thiel's framework from "Zero to One": 1) CONTRARIAN THESIS — What important truth about this market do few people agree with you on? What conventional wisdom is wrong? 2) MONOPOLY THEORY — Can this achieve monopoly in a small market first? What is the small market to dominate (start niche, expand)? 3) LAST MOVER ADVANTAGE — proprietary technology (10x better?), network effects, economies of scale, branding — score each 1-5. 4) SECRETS — What secret about the world or human nature does this business exploit? (Natural secret or human secret?) 5) DEFINITE OPTIMISM — Specific plan to create the future vs indefinite hope? 6) DISTRIBUTION — How will you sell this? (viral, sales, marketing, complex sales) 7) DURABILITY — Will this be valuable in 10 years? Why? Provide honest, critical assessment with a final 1-10 Zero-to-One score.`, 3000); if (raw?.length > 300) { files['ZERO-TO-ONE-ASSESSMENT.md'] = `# Zero-to-One Assessment — ${inf.name}\n\n${raw}`; setP('zero_to_one', 'ok', 'Done'); } else { setP('zero_to_one', 'sk', 'Skipped'); } } catch(e) { setP('zero_to_one', 'sk', e.message); } })() ]); // ── END: All 78 business depth documents implemented ──────────── setP("depth-docs", "ok", `${depthDocsGenerated} business depth documents generated`); } // ── Apply white-label if configured ───────────────────────────────── if (typeof isWhiteLabel === 'function' && isWhiteLabel()) { // Strip Dynasty branding from generated files for (const [path, content] of Object.entries(files)) { if (typeof content === 'string') { files[path] = content .replace(/Dynasty Empire/g, ORG) .replace(/Your Deputy/g, 'Business Launcher') .replace(/dynasty-launcher\.vercel\.app/g, '') .replace(/Dynasty/g, ORG); } } } // ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ // ── FINAL BUILD VALIDATION GATE — runs before ANY files are pushed ────── // This catches ALL known Vercel build-breaking patterns permanently. // ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ await (async function validateFilesBeforePush() { const isNextJs = V2_DEPLOY_TARGET === 'fullstack' || (inf.stack||[]).some(s => s.toLowerCase().includes('next')); // ── 0. Guarantee a clean package.json exists (replaces template bloat) ── if (isNextJs && !files['package.json']) { files['package.json'] = JSON.stringify({ name: inf.repo || 'app', version: '0.1.0', private: true, scripts: { dev: 'next dev', build: 'next build', start: 'next start' }, dependencies: { 'next': '14.2.35', 'react': '^18.3.1', 'react-dom': '^18.3.1', 'lucide-react': '^0.400.0', 'tailwindcss': '^4.0.0' }, devDependencies: { 'typescript': '^5.3.3', '@types/react': '^18.2.0', '@types/node': '^20.11.0', 'postcss': '^8.4.33', 'autoprefixer': '^10.4.17' }, engines: { node: '20.x' } }, null, 2); } // ── 1. vercel.json: NEVER framework:null or wrong outputDirectory ── if (files['vercel.json']) { try { const vc = JSON.parse(files['vercel.json']); let changed = false; if (vc.framework === null || vc.framework === 'null') { delete vc.framework; changed = true; } if (isNextJs && vc.outputDirectory) { delete vc.outputDirectory; changed = true; } if (isNextJs && vc.buildCommand) { delete vc.buildCommand; changed = true; } if (changed) files['vercel.json'] = JSON.stringify(vc, null, 2); } catch {} } // ── 2. package.json: clean deps, required deps, no template bloat ── if (files['package.json']) { try { const pkg = JSON.parse(files['package.json']); if (!pkg.dependencies) pkg.dependencies = {}; if (!pkg.devDependencies) pkg.devDependencies = {}; // ── 2a. STRIP TEMPLATE BLOAT — Storybook, testing, and dev tools that break Vercel builds ── const bloatPrefixes = ['@storybook/', '@chromatic/', '@jest/', '@testing-library/', '@playwright/']; const bloatExact = [ 'storybook', 'chromatic', 'jest', 'ts-jest', 'vitest', '@vitest/coverage-v8', '@vitest/ui', 'playwright', '@playwright/test', 'msw', 'cypress', 'prettier', 'eslint', 'husky', 'lint-staged', 'commitlint', '@commitlint/cli', '@commitlint/config-conventional', 'concurrently', 'turbo', // Vite deps that conflict with Next.js ...(isNextJs ? ['vite', '@vitejs/plugin-react', '@vitejs/plugin-react-swc', 'vite-tsconfig-paths'] : []) ]; for (const section of [pkg.dependencies, pkg.devDependencies]) { for (const dep of Object.keys(section)) { if (bloatPrefixes.some(p => dep.startsWith(p)) || bloatExact.includes(dep)) { delete section[dep]; } } } // Remove storybook scripts if (pkg.scripts) { for (const [k] of Object.entries(pkg.scripts)) { if (k.includes('storybook') || k.includes('chromatic') || k === 'test' || k === 'test:watch' || k === 'test:coverage') { delete pkg.scripts[k]; } } } // ── 2b. Swap heroicons → lucide-react ── if (pkg.dependencies['@heroicons/react']) { delete pkg.dependencies['@heroicons/react']; pkg.dependencies['lucide-react'] = pkg.dependencies['lucide-react'] || '^0.400.0'; } // ── 2c. Ensure essential Next.js deps ── if (isNextJs) { const reqDeps = { 'next': '14.2.35', 'react': '^18.3.1', 'react-dom': '^18.3.1', 'lucide-react': '^0.400.0', 'tailwindcss': '^4.0.0' }; const reqDev = { 'typescript': '^5.3.3', '@types/react': '^18.2.0', '@types/node': '^20.11.0' }; for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(reqDeps)) { if (!pkg.dependencies[k]) pkg.dependencies[k] = v; } for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(reqDev)) { if (!pkg.devDependencies[k]) pkg.devDependencies[k] = v; } // Ensure build script exists if (!pkg.scripts) pkg.scripts = {}; if (!pkg.scripts.build) pkg.scripts.build = 'next build'; if (!pkg.scripts.dev) pkg.scripts.dev = 'next dev'; if (!pkg.scripts.start) pkg.scripts.start = 'next start'; } // ── 2d. Clean engine constraint to prevent Vercel warnings ── if (pkg.engines?.node === '>=20') { pkg.engines.node = '20.x'; } files['package.json'] = JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2); } catch {} } // ── 3. globals.css: OVERRIDE template's broken Tailwind v3 globals.css ── // Template globals.css uses @apply border-border which crashes Tailwind v4. // Push a clean Tailwind v4 compatible globals.css that REPLACES the template's. if (isNextJs) files['src/app/globals.css'] = `@import "tailwindcss";\n\n:root {\n --background: 0 0% 100%;\n --foreground: 0 0% 3.9%;\n --card: 0 0% 100%;\n --card-foreground: 0 0% 3.9%;\n --popover: 0 0% 100%;\n --popover-foreground: 0 0% 3.9%;\n --primary: 0 0% 9%;\n --primary-foreground: 0 0% 98%;\n --secondary: 0 0% 96.1%;\n --secondary-foreground: 0 0% 9%;\n --muted: 0 0% 96.1%;\n --muted-foreground: 0 0% 45.1%;\n --accent: 0 0% 96.1%;\n --accent-foreground: 0 0% 9%;\n --destructive: 0 84.2% 60.2%;\n --destructive-foreground: 0 0% 98%;\n --border: 0 0% 89.8%;\n --input: 0 0% 89.8%;\n --ring: 0 0% 3.9%;\n --radius: 0.5rem;\n}\n\n.dark {\n --background: 0 0% 3.9%;\n --foreground: 0 0% 98%;\n --card: 0 0% 3.9%;\n --card-foreground: 0 0% 98%;\n --popover: 0 0% 3.9%;\n --popover-foreground: 0 0% 98%;\n --primary: 0 0% 98%;\n --primary-foreground: 0 0% 9%;\n --secondary: 0 0% 14.9%;\n --secondary-foreground: 0 0% 98%;\n --muted: 0 0% 14.9%;\n --muted-foreground: 0 0% 63.9%;\n --accent: 0 0% 14.9%;\n --accent-foreground: 0 0% 98%;\n --destructive: 0 62.8% 30.6%;\n --destructive-foreground: 0 0% 98%;\n --border: 0 0% 14.9%;\n --input: 0 0% 14.9%;\n --ring: 0 0% 83.1%;\n}\n\nbody {\n font-family: var(--font-sans, system-ui, sans-serif);\n}\n`; // ── 3b. postcss.config.js: Tailwind v4 doesn't use PostCSS plugins ── // Remove any postcss.config that references tailwindcss as a plugin (v3 pattern) // Tailwind v4 uses @import "tailwindcss" in CSS instead if (isNextJs && files['postcss.config.js']?.includes('tailwindcss')) { delete files['postcss.config.js']; } // Also remove tailwind.config.ts / .js — Tailwind v4 configures via CSS @theme if (isNextJs) { for (const tcf of ['tailwind.config.ts', 'tailwind.config.js', 'tailwind.config.mjs']) { if (files[tcf]) delete files[tcf]; } } // ── 3c. next.config.js: ensure it's not just a comment placeholder ── if (isNextJs) { const nc = files['next.config.js'] || files['next.config.mjs'] || ''; if (!nc.includes('module.exports') && !nc.includes('export default') && !nc.includes('nextConfig')) { files['next.config.mjs'] = `/** @type {import('next').NextConfig} */\nconst nextConfig = {};\n\nexport default nextConfig;\n`; delete files['next.config.js']; // prefer .mjs for Next.js 14+ } } // ── 3d. tsconfig.json: ensure Next.js compatible settings ── if (isNextJs && files['tsconfig.json']) { try { const tsconfig = JSON.parse(files['tsconfig.json']); if (!tsconfig.compilerOptions) tsconfig.compilerOptions = {}; let changed = false; if (tsconfig.compilerOptions.jsx !== 'preserve') { tsconfig.compilerOptions.jsx = 'preserve'; changed = true; } if (!['bundler','nodenext','node16'].includes(tsconfig.compilerOptions.moduleResolution)) { tsconfig.compilerOptions.moduleResolution = 'bundler'; changed = true; } if (!tsconfig.compilerOptions.paths?.['@/*']) { tsconfig.compilerOptions.baseUrl = '.'; tsconfig.compilerOptions.paths = { '@/*': ['./src/*'] }; changed = true; } if (changed) files['tsconfig.json'] = JSON.stringify(tsconfig, null, 2); } catch {} } // ── 4. TSX files: replace @heroicons → lucide-react ── const iconMap = { 'ArrowRightIcon':'ArrowRight','CheckIcon':'Check','CheckCircleIcon':'CheckCircle', 'ShieldCheckIcon':'ShieldCheck','DatabaseIcon':'Database','LightningBoltIcon':'Zap', 'BoltIcon':'Zap','CircleStackIcon':'Database','PlusIcon':'Plus', 'DocumentTextIcon':'FileText','KeyIcon':'Key','ChartBarIcon':'BarChart3', 'ClockIcon':'Clock','ExclamationCircleIcon':'AlertCircle','XMarkIcon':'X', 'Bars3Icon':'Menu','UserIcon':'User','CogIcon':'Settings','HomeIcon':'Home', 'StarIcon':'Star','HeartIcon':'Heart','MagnifyingGlassIcon':'Search', 'EnvelopeIcon':'Mail','PhoneIcon':'Phone','GlobeAltIcon':'Globe', 'CreditCardIcon':'CreditCard','BuildingOfficeIcon':'Building2', 'RocketLaunchIcon':'Rocket','SparklesIcon':'Sparkles','WrenchIcon':'Wrench', 'EyeIcon':'Eye','TrashIcon':'Trash2','PencilIcon':'Pencil', 'ArrowLeftIcon':'ArrowLeft','ChevronDownIcon':'ChevronDown','ChevronRightIcon':'ChevronRight', 'InformationCircleIcon':'Info','BellIcon':'Bell','CalendarIcon':'Calendar', 'FolderIcon':'Folder','LinkIcon':'Link','PhotoIcon':'Image', 'CloudArrowUpIcon':'CloudUpload','ArrowDownTrayIcon':'Download', 'ShareIcon':'Share2','AdjustmentsHorizontalIcon':'SlidersHorizontal' }; for (const [fpath, content] of Object.entries(files)) { if (typeof content === 'string' && fpath.endsWith('.tsx') && content.includes('@heroicons/react')) { let code = content; const heroMatch = code.match(/import\s*\{([^}]+)\}\s*from\s*['"]@heroicons\/react[^'"]*['"]/g); if (heroMatch) { for (const imp of heroMatch) { const iconsInImport = imp.match(/\{([^}]+)\}/)?.[1]?.split(',').map(s=>s.trim()).filter(Boolean) || []; const mapped = iconsInImport.map(i => iconMap[i] || i.replace(/Icon$/, '')); const newImport = `import { ${mapped.join(', ')} } from 'lucide-react'`; code = code.replace(imp, newImport); // Replace usage in JSX iconsInImport.forEach((old, idx) => { code = code.replace(new RegExp(old.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g,'\\$&'), 'g'), mapped[idx]); }); } } files[fpath] = code; } } // ── 5. layout.tsx: ensure globals.css import exists ── const layoutPaths = ['src/app/layout.tsx', 'src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx']; for (const lp of layoutPaths) { if (files[lp] && !files[lp].includes("globals.css")) { files[lp] = `import './globals.css';\n\n${files[lp]}`; } } // ── 6. Remove unused imports from TSX (prevents TS strict mode errors) ── for (const [fpath, content] of Object.entries(files)) { if (typeof content === 'string' && fpath.endsWith('.tsx')) { let code = content; // Find all named imports from lucide-react const lucideMatch = code.match(/import\s*\{([^}]+)\}\s*from\s*['"]lucide-react['"]/); if (lucideMatch) { const imported = lucideMatch[1].split(',').map(s=>s.trim()).filter(Boolean); const bodyAfterImport = code.slice(code.indexOf(lucideMatch[0]) + lucideMatch[0].length); const used = imported.filter(icon => { const re = new RegExp('<\\s*' + icon.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g,'\\$&') + '[\\s/>]'); return re.test(bodyAfterImport); }); if (used.length < imported.length && used.length > 0) { code = code.replace(lucideMatch[0], `import { ${used.join(', ')} } from 'lucide-react'`); files[fpath] = code; } } } } // ── 7. COMPLETE TEMPLATE CLEANUP — one pass, everything fixed ────────── // Nullify ALL boilerplate components that cause build errors const nullComponents = { 'src/components/DemoBadge.tsx': 'export const DemoBadge = () => null;\n', 'src/components/CurrentCount.tsx': "export const CurrentCount = async () => null;\n", 'src/templates/DemoBanner.tsx': 'export const DemoBanner = () => null;\n', 'src/templates/Sponsors.tsx': 'export const Sponsors = () => null;\n', 'src/features/sponsors/SponsorLogos.tsx': 'export const SponsorLogos = () => null;\n', 'src/features/landing/LogoCloud.tsx': "import React from 'react';\nexport const LogoCloud = ({ children, text }: { children?: React.ReactNode; text?: string }) => null;\n", 'src/features/landing/StickyBanner.tsx': "import React from 'react';\nexport const StickyBanner = ({ children }: { children?: React.ReactNode }) => null;\n", }; for (const [fp, code] of Object.entries(nullComponents)) files[fp] = code; // ── 7b. Override template Schema.ts — prevent counterSchema export errors ── // Template's Schema.ts exports counterSchema but generated code may not use it. // Push a clean Schema.ts that re-exports everything the template expects. if (!files['src/models/Schema.ts']) { files['src/models/Schema.ts'] = `import { pgTable, serial, text, timestamp } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';\n\nexport const productsSchema = pgTable('products', {\n id: serial('id').primaryKey(),\n name: text('name').notNull(),\n description: text('description'),\n createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),\n});\n\n// Legacy template exports — kept for compatibility\nexport const counterSchema = productsSchema;\n`; } // ── 7c. Override counter page to prevent import errors from template demo app ── files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/counter/page.tsx'] = `export default function CounterPage() { return null; }\n`; // Fix AppConfig.ts files['src/utils/AppConfig.ts'] = (files['src/utils/AppConfig.ts'] || '').replace(/name:\s*['"]SaaS Template['"]/, `name: '${(inf.name||'App').replace(/'/g, "\\'")}'`) || files['src/utils/AppConfig.ts']; // Fix Logo component files['src/templates/Logo.tsx'] = `export const Logo = () => ${(inf.name||'App').replace(/'/g, "\\'")};\n`; // Comprehensive translation override — ALL 27 keys const projDesc = desc.slice(0, 200); const fullTranslation = { Index: { meta_title: `${inf.name} - ${inf.type_label||'Platform'}`, meta_description: projDesc }, Navbar: { sign_in: 'Sign In', sign_up: 'Get Started', product: 'Product', docs: 'Docs', blog: 'Blog', community: 'Support', company: 'About' }, Hero: { follow_twitter: `${inf.name} — ${inf.type_label||''}`, title: `The ${inf.name} — ${projDesc.slice(0, 80)}.`, description: projDesc, primary_button: 'Get Started', secondary_button: 'Learn More' }, Sponsors: { title: 'Powered By' }, Features: { section_subtitle: 'Features', section_title: `Why Choose ${inf.name}`, section_description: projDesc.slice(0, 150), feature1_title: 'Fast & Reliable', feature2_title: 'Secure', feature3_title: 'Scalable', feature4_title: 'TypeScript', feature5_title: 'Dashboard', feature6_title: 'API Access', feature_description: projDesc.slice(0, 120) }, Pricing: { section_subtitle: 'Pricing', section_title: 'Simple, Transparent Pricing', section_description: 'Start free, scale as you grow.', button_text: 'Get Started' }, PricingPlan: { free_plan_name: 'Free', premium_plan_name: 'Pro', enterprise_plan_name: 'Enterprise', free_plan_description: 'For individuals', premium_plan_description: 'For growing teams', enterprise_plan_description: 'For large organizations', feature_team_member: '{number} Team Members', feature_website: '{number} Projects', feature_storage: '{number} GB Storage', feature_transfer: '{number} TB Transfer', feature_email_support: 'Email Support', plan_interval_month: 'month', plan_interval_year: 'year', next_renew_date: 'Your subscription renews on {date}' }, FAQ: { question: `What does ${inf.name} do?`, answer: projDesc }, CTA: { title: 'Ready to get started?', description: `Start using ${inf.name} today.
