Deterministic route hardening (prevents vanilla/404 deployments) ── const _safeName = String(inf.name || 'Your Business').replace(/`/g, '\\`').replace(/'/g, "\\'"); const _safeDesc = String(desc || `${_safeName} platform`).slice(0, 280).replace(/`/g, '\\`').replace(/'/g, "\\'"); const _safePrice = String(inf.price || '$49/mo').replace(/`/g, '\\`').replace(/'/g, "\\'"); const _safeType = String(inf.type_label || 'Specialized service').replace(/`/g, '\\`').replace(/'/g, "\\'"); const _safeRevenue = String(inf.revenue_model || 'Subscription').replace(/`/g, '\\`').replace(/'/g, "\\'"); const _brandingLeakRe = /(SaaS Template|SaaS Boilerplate|Ixartz|nextjs-boilerplate)/i; const _mkMarketingPage = (title, subtitle, body, ctaHref = '/pricing', ctaText = 'Get Started') => `import Link from 'next/link'; export const metadata = { title: '${title} · ${_safeName}', description: '${subtitle}', openGraph: { title: '${title} · ${_safeName}', description: '${subtitle}', type: 'website' } }; export default function Page() { const painPoints = [ '${_safeType} teams lose momentum when workflows are fragmented', 'Buyers need clear proof, not vague promises', 'Operational handoff breaks when process is undocumented' ] const deliveryModel = [ 'Define outcome, scope, and conversion path', 'Implement pages, workflows, and automation hooks', 'Verify routes, content integrity, and launch readiness' ] const faq = [ { q: 'Who is this built for?', a: '${_safeType} buyers and operators who need predictable execution.' }, { q: 'What do we receive?', a: 'A complete delivery package with production-oriented assets and documentation.' }, { q: 'How do we get started?', a: 'Use the guided path in pricing, choose scope, and launch with a verified build flow.' } ] return (

${subtitle}

${title}

${body}

${ctaText} Read docs
{painPoints.map((item) => (
{item}
))}

How delivery works

{deliveryModel.map((step, idx) => (

Step {idx + 1}

{step}

))}

Common questions

{faq.map((item) => (
{item.q}

{item.a}

))}
); } `; const _mkHomePage = () => `import Link from 'next/link'; export const metadata = { title: '${_safeName}', description: '${_safeDesc}', openGraph: { title: '${_safeName}', description: '${_safeDesc}', type: 'website' } }; export default function HomePage() { const coreOutcomes = [ 'Niche-specific positioning and conversion architecture', 'Production-ready pages, offers, and launch assets', 'Operational clarity: delivery scope, checkpoints, and handoff documentation' ] const trustSignals = [ 'Outcome-first architecture aligned to ${_safeType}', 'Route and content verification before completion', 'Transparent scope across package and tier constraints' ] const faq = [ { q: 'What exactly is this?', a: '${_safeName} is a ${_safeType} execution system focused on clear business outcomes.' }, { q: 'Who should use it?', a: 'Operators and founders who need complete launch assets without ambiguous scope.' }, { q: 'How is value delivered?', a: 'Through prioritized outputs, validated route coverage, and conversion-focused page structure.' }, { q: 'How do we proceed?', a: 'Start from pricing, select the package depth, and launch with the guided build flow.' } ] return (

Built for ${_safeType}

${_safeName}

${_safeDesc}

See package options Speak with an operator
{coreOutcomes.map((item) => (

Outcome

{item}

))}

Why this converts in your niche

Audience fit is driven by direct, niche-specific messaging and offer clarity. Commercial model: ${_safeRevenue} · ${_safePrice}.

{trustSignals.map((signal) => (
{signal}
))}

FAQ

{faq.map((item) => (
{item.q}

{item.a}

))}

Ready to launch ${_safeName}?

Choose your package, lock scope, and move to execution with full transparency.

View pricing Read documentation
); } `; const _mkNotFoundPage = () => `import Link from 'next/link'; export default function NotFound() { return (

404

Page not found

The page you requested is unavailable right now. Continue with ${_safeName} using the links below.

Go home View pricing Documentation
); } `; if (isNextJs) { const _bpLocal = getBuildProfile(); const _existingHome = files['src/app/page.tsx'] || ''; const _needsHomeRepair = !_existingHome || _existingHome.length < 300 || _brandingLeakRe.test(_existingHome) || !_existingHome.includes(inf.name || ''); if (_needsHomeRepair) { const _home = _mkHomePage(); files['src/app/page.tsx'] = _home; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/page.tsx'] = _home; } const _routeDefs = [ { key: 'docs', title: 'Documentation', subtitle: `How ${_safeName} is delivered`, body: `Use this guide to understand deployment flow, route expectations, validation checks, and package-specific outputs for ${_safeType}.` }, { key: 'pricing', title: 'Pricing', subtitle: 'Scope-aligned pricing', body: `Each ${_safeName} package maps to execution depth and measurable outcomes. Commercial frame: ${_safeRevenue} · ${_safePrice}.` }, { key: 'about', title: 'About', subtitle: `Execution partner for ${_safeType}`, body: `${_safeName} exists to remove ambiguity and convert strategy into production-ready outcomes with transparent validation.` }, { key: 'services', title: 'Services', subtitle: 'Niche-specific deliverables', body: `Service delivery includes audience-fit messaging, conversion architecture, implementation scaffolds, and go-live verification for ${_safeType}.` }, { key: 'contact', title: 'Contact', subtitle: 'Direct operator support', body: `Get implementation guidance for your selected scope, timeline, and verification requirements.` } ]; const _alwaysRequired = new Set(['docs', 'pricing', 'about']); const _archetypeRequiresFive = ['starter_5p', 'growth', 'authority_site', 'enterprise_full'].includes(_bpLocal?.archetype || ''); for (const _r of _routeDefs) { if (!_alwaysRequired.has(_r.key) && !_archetypeRequiresFive) continue; const _primaryPath = `src/app/${_r.key}/page.tsx`; const _localePath = `src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/${_r.key}/page.tsx`; const _current = files[_primaryPath] || ''; const _needsRepair = !_current || _current.length < 220 || _brandingLeakRe.test(_current) || !_current.includes(inf.name || ''); if (_needsRepair) { const _code = _mkMarketingPage(_r.title, _r.subtitle, _r.body, _r.key === 'pricing' ? '/sign-up' : '/pricing', _r.key === 'pricing' ? 'Choose plan' : 'View pricing'); files[_primaryPath] = _code; files[_localePath] = _code; } else if (!files[_localePath]) { files[_localePath] = _current; } } const _nf = _mkNotFoundPage(); files['src/app/not-found.tsx'] = _nf; files['src/app/[locale]/not-found.tsx'] = _nf; // ── 8b. Strip AI prompt placeholders from backend/main.py ── if (files['backend/main.py']) { files['backend/main.py'] = files['backend/main.py'] .replace(/\[Define \d+-\d+ more[^\]]*\]/g, '') .replace(/\[Implement \d+ core[^\]]*\]/g, '') .replace(/\[Add \d+-\d+[^\]]*\]/g, '') .replace(/\[TODO[^\]]*\]/gi, '') .replace(/# ?\[.*?\]/g, '') .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n'); } // ── 9. Authority conversion quality gate (iterative; never blocks ship) ── if (!(_res && _resumeFlags.gateCompleted)) { // Defaults favor shipping. Optional localStorage `dynasty_quality_threshold` (60–88; values above 88 are clamped to 88 so customers are not stuck chasing model variance). // `dynasty_skip_authority_quality_gate=1` skips scoring entirely (emergency / QA). const _skipGate = localStorage.getItem('dynasty_skip_authority_quality_gate') === '1'; const _rawThresh = Number(localStorage.getItem('dynasty_quality_threshold') || '65'); const _qualityThreshold = Math.max(55, Math.min(88, Number.isFinite(_rawThresh) ? _rawThresh : 65)); const _dimensionFloors = { audience_fit: 58, clarity: 58, differentiation: 58, trust: 58, conversion: 58, seo_structure: 58, message_specificity: 58 }; const _floorSummary = Object.entries(_dimensionFloors).map(([k, v]) => `${k}>=${v}`).join(', '); const _qualityMaxPasses = 8; const _readPage = (p) => String(files[p] || '').slice(0, 3500); let _qualityScore = 0; let _qualityVerdict = 'unknown'; let _dimensionScores = {}; if (_skipGate) { addP('quality-gate', 'Authority quality gate', 'Skipped (dynasty_skip_authority_quality_gate=1)'); setP('quality-gate', 'ok', 'Scoring not run'); } else { addP('quality-gate', `Authority quality gate (target ${_qualityThreshold}/100)`, 'Scoring homepage and conversion routes'); setP('quality-gate', 'run', 'Pass 1 starting...'); for (let _pass = 1; _pass <= _qualityMaxPasses; _pass++) { setP('quality-gate', 'run', `Pass ${_pass}/${_qualityMaxPasses} — scoring`); const _auditPayload = { niche: inf.type_label || 'Specialized service', project: inf.name || 'Project', revenue_model: inf.revenue_model || 'Subscription', price_anchor: inf.price || '$49/mo', pages: { home: _readPage('src/app/page.tsx'), pricing: _readPage('src/app/pricing/page.tsx'), docs: _readPage('src/app/docs/page.tsx'), about: _readPage('src/app/about/page.tsx'), services: _readPage('src/app/services/page.tsx'), contact: _readPage('src/app/contact/page.tsx') } }; let _recommendations = ['Increase niche specificity', 'Strengthen trust proof', 'Tighten CTA clarity']; try { const _raw = await aiRaw(`You are a principal CRO + UX + SEO auditor. Score this website draft for launch quality in its specific niche. Return ONLY valid JSON: { "overall_score": 0-100, "verdict": "excellent|good|weak", "dimension_scores": { "audience_fit": 0-100, "clarity": 0-100, "differentiation": 0-100, "trust": 0-100, "conversion": 0-100, "seo_structure": 0-100, "message_specificity": 0-100 }, "critical_gaps": ["... up to 6"], "recommended_upgrades": ["... up to 8"] } Scoring guidance (draft Next.js TSX — be generous; mid-70s+ is normal when structure and copy exist): - Do NOT score as if this were a finished marketing site. Reward clear hero, CTAs, headings, and niche mentions. - overall_score should reflect weighted fit to niche + conversion clarity (most drafts should land 68–85 if usable) - verdict: excellent | good | weak - dimension_scores: each key required; if unsure, set each dimension within ±8 of overall_score (avoid leaving any at 0) - soft guidance (not hard fails): ${_floorSummary} Target audience and current page drafts: ${JSON.stringify(_auditPayload, null, 2)}`, 1800, 'standard'); const _m = (_raw || '').match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/); const _sc = _m ? safeJsonParse(_m[0]) : null; _qualityScore = Number(_sc?.overall_score || 0); if (!Number.isFinite(_qualityScore)) _qualityScore = 0; _qualityVerdict = String(_sc?.verdict || 'unknown'); _dimensionScores = _sc?.dimension_scores && typeof _sc.dimension_scores === 'object' ? { ..._sc.dimension_scores } : {}; const _dimKeys = ['audience_fit','clarity','differentiation','trust','conversion','seo_structure','message_specificity']; for (const _dk of _dimKeys) { let _v = Number(_dimensionScores[_dk]); if (!Number.isFinite(_v)) _v = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, Math.round(_qualityScore || 62))); _dimensionScores[_dk] = _v; } if (Array.isArray(_sc?.recommended_upgrades) && _sc.recommended_upgrades.length) { _recommendations = _sc.recommended_upgrades.slice(0, 8); } } catch {} const _recText = _recommendations.slice(0, 4).join(' · '); const _failedFloors = Object.entries(_dimensionFloors).filter(([k, min]) => Number(_dimensionScores?.[k] ?? 0) < min); const _failedFloorText = _failedFloors.length ? _failedFloors.map(([k, min]) => `${k}<${min}`).join(', ') : 'none'; setP(`quality-pass-${_pass}`, 'run', `Score ${_qualityScore || 0}/100 · ${_qualityVerdict || 'unknown'} · soft misses: ${_failedFloorText} · ${_recText}`); if (_qualityScore >= _qualityThreshold) { setP('quality-gate', 'ok', `Passed at ${_qualityScore}/100 on pass ${_pass} (${_qualityVerdict})${_failedFloors.length ? ' — minor dimension gaps noted; ship anyway' : ''}`); break; } setP('quality-gate', 'run', `Pass ${_pass}/${_qualityMaxPasses} scored ${_qualityScore}/100 (soft misses: ${_failedFloorText}) — regenerating pages`); try { const _rewrite = await ai(`Regenerate high-converting, niche-specific authority pages for a Next.js App Router site. Project: ${_safeName} Niche: ${_safeType} Description: ${_safeDesc} Revenue model: ${_safeRevenue} Price anchor: ${_safePrice} Must-haves: 1. Sound like a top-tier human copywriter, not AI boilerplate. 2. Specific to this niche audience and pain points. 3. Strong conversion architecture: intent-led hero, trust proof, offer clarity, objection handling, and precise CTAs. 4. Include concrete differentiation and outcome framing. 5. SEO-aware heading structure and metadata. 6. No generic "SaaS template" language. 7. Return production-quality TSX that compiles. 8. Meet quality floors: ${_floorSummary} Upgrades to enforce: ${_recommendations.map((r, i) => `${i + 1}. ${r}`).join('\n')} Return these files only using delimiters: ---BEGIN:home_page_tsx--- // src/app/page.tsx ---END:home_page_tsx--- ---BEGIN:docs_page_tsx--- // src/app/docs/page.tsx ---END:docs_page_tsx--- ---BEGIN:pricing_page_tsx--- // src/app/pricing/page.tsx ---END:pricing_page_tsx--- ---BEGIN:about_page_tsx--- // src/app/about/page.tsx ---END:about_page_tsx--- ---BEGIN:services_page_tsx--- // src/app/services/page.tsx ---END:services_page_tsx--- ---BEGIN:contact_page_tsx--- // src/app/contact/page.tsx ---END:contact_page_tsx---`, ['home_page_tsx', 'docs_page_tsx', 'pricing_page_tsx', 'about_page_tsx', 'services_page_tsx', 'contact_page_tsx'], 9000, 'frontend'); if (_rewrite.home_page_tsx) { files['src/app/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.home_page_tsx; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.home_page_tsx; } if (_rewrite.docs_page_tsx) { files['src/app/docs/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.docs_page_tsx; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/docs/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.docs_page_tsx; } if (_rewrite.pricing_page_tsx) { files['src/app/pricing/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.pricing_page_tsx; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/pricing/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.pricing_page_tsx; } if (_rewrite.about_page_tsx) { files['src/app/about/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.about_page_tsx; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/about/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.about_page_tsx; } if (_rewrite.services_page_tsx) { files['src/app/services/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.services_page_tsx; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/services/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.services_page_tsx; } if (_rewrite.contact_page_tsx) { files['src/app/contact/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.contact_page_tsx; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/contact/page.tsx'] = _rewrite.contact_page_tsx; } } catch {} if (_pass === _qualityMaxPasses) { const _finalMisses = Object.entries(_dimensionFloors) .filter(([k, min]) => Number(_dimensionScores?.[k] ?? 0) < min) .map(([k, min]) => `${k}<${min}`) .join(', ') || 'none'; const _msg = `After ${_qualityMaxPasses} passes: score ${_qualityScore}/100 (soft target ${_qualityThreshold}), dimension notes: ${_finalMisses}. Build continues — tighten copy in-repo if you want a higher bar. (Strict halt removed; optional skip: localStorage dynasty_skip_authority_quality_gate=1)`; setP('quality-gate', 'warn', _msg); try { window._authorityQualityGateLast = { score: _qualityScore, threshold: _qualityThreshold, misses: _finalMisses, pass: _qualityMaxPasses }; } catch {} break; } } } } else { addP('quality-gate', 'Authority quality gate', 'Recovered from saved session'); setP('quality-gate', 'ok', 'Skipped re-run — using checkpointed pages'); } } // ── Fix auth links: /auth/login → /sign-in, /auth/signup → /sign-up ── for (const [fp, content] of Object.entries(files)) { if (typeof content === 'string' && (content.includes('/auth/login') || content.includes('/auth/signup'))) { files[fp] = content.replace(/\/auth\/login/g, '/sign-in').replace(/\/auth\/signup/g, '/sign-up'); } } // ── Generate /docs page if not present ── if (isNextJs && !files['src/app/docs/page.tsx']) { const docsPage = `import Link from 'next/link';\n\nexport const metadata = { title: '${(inf.name||'App').replace(/'/g,"\\'")} Documentation', description: 'Learn how to use ${(inf.name||'the platform').replace(/'/g,"\\'")}.' };\n\nexport default function DocsPage() {\n return (\n
\n

Documentation

\n

Everything you need to get started with ${inf.name||'the platform'}.

\n
\n

Getting Started

Create an account and explore the dashboard to begin.

\n

API Reference

Full API documentation is available in the repository. Check the dashboard for your API keys.

\n
\n
\n );\n}\n`; files['src/app/docs/page.tsx'] = docsPage; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/docs/page.tsx'] = docsPage; } // ── Generate /pricing page if not present ── if (isNextJs && !files['src/app/pricing/page.tsx']) { const price = inf.price || '$49/month'; const pricingPage = `import Link from 'next/link';\n\nexport const metadata = { title: 'Pricing - ${(inf.name||'App').replace(/'/g,"\\'")}', description: 'Simple, transparent pricing.' };\n\nexport default function PricingPage() {\n return (\n
\n

Pricing

Start free, scale as you grow.

\n
\n

Free

$0/mo
  • ✓ Basic access
  • ✓ Community support
Get Started
\n
POPULAR

Pro

${price.replace(/\/.*/, '')}/mo
  • ✓ Everything in Free
  • ✓ Priority support
  • ✓ Advanced features
Start Trial
\n

Enterprise

Custom
  • ✓ Everything in Pro
  • ✓ SLA guarantee
  • ✓ Dedicated support
Contact Sales
\n
\n
\n );\n}\n`; files['src/app/pricing/page.tsx'] = pricingPage; files['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/pricing/page.tsx'] = pricingPage; } // ── Generate .env.example ── if (isNextJs) { const sn = (inf.name||'App').replace(/'/g, ''); files['.env.example'] = `# ${sn} — Environment Variables # Copy this file: cp .env.example .env.local # Then fill in each value from the service dashboards listed below. # NEVER commit .env.local to git. # ─── Authentication (Clerk) ───────────────────────────────────────────── # Dashboard: https://dashboard.clerk.com → Create application → API Keys NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_REPLACE_ME CLERK_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_REPLACE_ME NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL=/en/sign-in NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_UP_URL=/en/sign-up NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_IN_URL=/en/dashboard NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_UP_URL=/en/dashboard # ─── Payments (Stripe) ────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Dashboard: https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_REPLACE_ME STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_REPLACE_ME # Webhook: https://dashboard.stripe.com/webhooks → Add endpoint → your-domain/api/webhooks/stripe STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_REPLACE_ME BILLING_PLAN_ENV=test # ─── Database (Neon Postgres) ─────────────────────────────────────────── # Dashboard: https://console.neon.tech → Project → Connection Details # Use the pooled connection string (ending in -pooler.neon.tech) DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@host-pooler.neon.tech/dbname?sslmode=require # ─── App Configuration ────────────────────────────────────────────────── NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=https://${inf.repo||'your-app'}.vercel.app NODE_ENV=production # ─── Monitoring (Optional) ────────────────────────────────────────────── # Sentry: https://sentry.io → Create Project → Client Keys # SENTRY_DSN=https://...@sentry.io/... # PostHog: https://app.posthog.com → Project Settings → Project API Key # NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=phc_... # NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=https://app.posthog.com `; } // Global find-and-replace across ALL files — catch anything the above missed for (const [fpath, content] of Object.entries(files)) { if (typeof content !== 'string') continue; let c = content; if (c.includes('nextjs-boilerplate.com') || c.includes('SaaS Boilerplate') || c.includes('SaaS Template') || c.includes('Ixartz')) { c = c.replace(/https?:\/\/[^\s"')\]]*nextjs-boilerplate[^\s"')\]]*/g, '#'); c = c.replace(/SaaS Boilerplate/g, inf.name || 'Platform'); c = c.replace(/SaaS Template/g, inf.name || 'Platform'); c = c.replace(/@?Ixartz/g, inf.name || 'Platform'); c = c.replace(/Demo of SaaS[^"']*/g, inf.name || 'Platform'); files[fpath] = c; } } })(); try { dynastyScheduleCheckpointSave(desc, inf, { gateCompleted: true }); } catch {} // ── g8 Day-1 Kit: Parallelized (TurboQuant compression) ───────────── // Group B: tests + seed + api (code-heavy, independent) — run in parallel { addP('g8ts', 'Generating automated test suite', '', '⬡ Testing'); setP('g8ts', 'run'); addP('g8sd', 'Generating seed data and demo content', '', '⬡ Seed Data'); setP('g8sd', 'run'); addP('g8api', 'Generating API collection', '', '⬡ API Docs'); setP('g8api', 'run'); const apiContracts = files['docs/API-CONTRACTS.md'] || ''; const dataModel = files['docs/DATA-MODEL.md'] || ''; const specDoc = files['SPEC.md'] || ''; const generatedRoutes = Object.keys(files).filter(f => f.includes('/api/') || f.includes('route.ts') || f.includes('route.js')); const generatedPages = Object.keys(files).filter(f => f.includes('/app/') && (f.includes('page.ts') || f.includes('page.js'))); const [_g8tsR, _g8sdR, _g8apiR] = await Promise.allSettled([ // ── g8_tests ── aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive test suite for "${inf.name}" (Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript). PROJECT CONTEXT: ${cx(desc, inf)} GENERATED API ROUTES: ${generatedRoutes.join(', ') || 'src/app/api/v1/route.ts, src/app/api/data/route.ts'} GENERATED PAGES: ${generatedPages.join(', ') || 'src/app/page.tsx, src/app/dashboard/page.tsx, src/app/pricing/page.tsx'} DATA MODEL SUMMARY: ${dataModel.slice(0, 1500)} API CONTRACTS SUMMARY: ${apiContracts.slice(0, 1500)} Generate these files in delimiter format: ===FILE: src/__tests__/api.test.ts=== [Vitest tests for API routes. Test each endpoint: status codes, response shape, error handling. Use fetch. Mock auth.] ===FILE: src/__tests__/pages.test.tsx=== [React Testing Library tests for pages. Test key elements render: headings, navigation, CTAs.] ===FILE: src/__tests__/schema.test.ts=== [Validation tests for data model. Test required fields, type constraints using Zod or manual checks.] ===FILE: e2e/smoke.spec.ts=== [Playwright e2e smoke tests. Homepage loads, nav links work, sign-in redirects, dashboard gated.] ===FILE: playwright.config.ts=== [Playwright config: localhost:3000, chromium only, screenshot on failure.] ===FILE: vitest.config.ts=== [Vitest config: jsdom, path aliases, coverage thresholds 70%.] Rules: Use Vitest (not Jest), @testing-library/react, Playwright. All TypeScript. No real API keys. Return ONLY delimited content.`, 12000, 'standard'), // ── g8_seed ── aiRaw(`Generate seed data and a seeder script for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). PROJECT: Business type: ${inf.type_label}, Revenue: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}, Stack: ${(inf.stack||[]).join(', ')}, Accent: ${inf.accent} DATA MODEL: ${dataModel.slice(0, 2000)} Generate in delimiter format: ===FILE: src/data/seed/users.json=== [8 fictional users: id (uuid), name, email (@example.com), role (admin/user/manager), avatar URL, createdAt, plan tier.] ===FILE: src/data/seed/content.json=== [12 sample content items matching the business model. id, title, description, status, createdAt, authorId.] ===FILE: src/data/seed/transactions.json=== [15 sample transactions for charts. id, amount (cents), currency, status, customerId, description, createdAt (last 90 days).] ===FILE: src/scripts/seed.ts=== [TypeScript seeder: reads JSON files, detects ORM (Prisma/Drizzle/raw pg), upserts records. Runs as: npx tsx src/scripts/seed.ts. Supports --clean flag. Logs progress.] Rules: Fictional data only, @example.com emails, amounts in cents, UUIDs, ISO dates. Return ONLY delimited content.`, 6000, 'standard'), // ── g8_api ── aiRaw(`Generate an OpenAPI 3.0 spec and Postman collection for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). PROJECT: App name: ${inf.name}, Base URL: \${baseUrl} (default: ${inf.url || 'http://localhost:3000'}), Auth: Bearer token. API CONTRACTS: ${apiContracts.slice(0, 2500)} DATA MODEL: ${dataModel.slice(0, 1500)} Generate in delimiter format: ===FILE: docs/openapi.json=== [OpenAPI 3.0.3 JSON: info, servers, paths (every route with method, params, requestBody, responses 200/400/401/404/500), components/schemas, securitySchemes (bearerAuth).] ===FILE: docs/postman-collection.json=== [Postman v2.1 JSON: variable (baseUrl, token), auth (bearer), item folders (Auth, Users, Core, Admin), each request with method, url, headers, body, examples. Importable into Postman/Bruno/Hoppscotch.] Rules: Valid JSON only, realistic examples, error responses, auth flows. Return ONLY delimited content.`, 12000, 'standard'), ]); // Process g8_tests result const testRaw = _g8tsR?.status === 'fulfilled' ? _g8tsR.value : ''; let testFilesCount = 0; if (testRaw) { const testSections = testRaw.split(/===FILE:\s*/); for (const section of testSections) { if (!section.trim()) continue; const match = section.match(/^([^\n=]+)===([\s\S]*)/); if (match) { const fpath = match[1].trim(); let content = match[2].trim().replace(/^\`\`\`\w*\s*\n?/gm, '').replace(/\n?\`\`\`\s*$/gm, '').trim(); if (fpath && content.length > 50) { files[fpath] = content; testFilesCount++; } } } } if (testFilesCount > 0) { try { const pkg = JSON.parse(files['package.json'] || '{}'); if (!pkg.devDependencies) pkg.devDependencies = {}; if (!pkg.scripts) pkg.scripts = {}; pkg.devDependencies['vitest'] = '^3.0.0'; pkg.devDependencies['@vitest/coverage-v8'] = '^3.0.0'; pkg.devDependencies['@testing-library/react'] = '^16.0.0'; pkg.devDependencies['@testing-library/jest-dom'] = '^6.6.0'; pkg.devDependencies['@playwright/test'] = '^1.48.0'; pkg.devDependencies['jsdom'] = '^25.0.0'; pkg.scripts['test'] = 'vitest run'; pkg.scripts['test:watch'] = 'vitest'; pkg.scripts['test:coverage'] = 'vitest run --coverage'; pkg.scripts['test:e2e'] = 'playwright test'; files['package.json'] = JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2); } catch (_pkgErr) {} setP('g8ts', 'ok', `${testFilesCount} test files generated`); } else { setP('g8ts', 'sk', 'No test files parsed'); } // Process g8_seed result const seedRaw = _g8sdR?.status === 'fulfilled' ? _g8sdR.value : ''; let seedFilesCount = 0; if (seedRaw) { const seedSections = seedRaw.split(/===FILE:\s*/); for (const section of seedSections) { if (!section.trim()) continue; const match = section.match(/^([^\n=]+)===([\s\S]*)/); if (match) { const fpath = match[1].trim(); let content = match[2].trim().replace(/^\`\`\`\w*\s*\n?/gm, '').replace(/\n?\`\`\`\s*$/gm, '').trim(); if (fpath && content.length > 30) { files[fpath] = content; seedFilesCount++; } } } } if (seedFilesCount > 0) { try { const pkg = JSON.parse(files['package.json'] || '{}'); if (!pkg.scripts) pkg.scripts = {}; pkg.scripts['db:seed'] = 'tsx src/scripts/seed.ts'; pkg.scripts['db:seed:clean'] = 'tsx src/scripts/seed.ts --clean'; if (!pkg.devDependencies) pkg.devDependencies = {}; if (!pkg.devDependencies['tsx']) pkg.devDependencies['tsx'] = '^4.19.0'; files['package.json'] = JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2); } catch (_pkgErr) {} setP('g8sd', 'ok', `${seedFilesCount} seed files generated`); } else { setP('g8sd', 'sk', 'No seed files parsed'); } // Process g8_api result const apiColRaw = _g8apiR?.status === 'fulfilled' ? _g8apiR.value : ''; let apiFilesCount = 0; if (apiColRaw) { const apiSections = apiColRaw.split(/===FILE:\s*/); for (const section of apiSections) { if (!section.trim()) continue; const match = section.match(/^([^\n=]+)===([\s\S]*)/); if (match) { const fpath = match[1].trim(); let content = match[2].trim().replace(/^\`\`\`\w*\s*\n?/gm, '').replace(/\n?\`\`\`\s*$/gm, '').trim(); if (fpath && content.length > 50) { files[fpath] = content; apiFilesCount++; } } } } if (apiFilesCount > 0) { setP('g8api', 'ok', `${apiFilesCount} API collection files`); } else { setP('g8api', 'sk', 'No API files parsed'); } } // Group A: onboard + playbook (doc-heavy, independent) — run in parallel { addP('g8ob', 'Generating interactive onboarding dashboard', '', '⬡ Onboarding'); setP('g8ob', 'run'); addP('g8pb', 'Generating 90-day launch playbook', '', '⬡ Launch Plan'); setP('g8pb', 'run'); const tier = getUserTier(); const tierConfig = V3_TIERS[tier] || V3_TIERS.foundation; const moduleResults = window._dynastyModuleResults || {}; const provisionedServices = Object.entries(moduleResults).filter(([, v]) => v && v.ok).map(([k]) => k); const deferredServices = Object.entries(moduleResults).filter(([, v]) => v && !v.ok).map(([k]) => k); const envVars = files['.env.example'] || ''; const manualActions = files['MANUAL-ACTIONS.md'] || ''; const deliverables = Object.keys(files).slice(0, 40); const [_g8obR, _g8pbR] = await Promise.allSettled([ // ── g8_onboard ── aiRaw(`Generate a self-contained HTML onboarding dashboard for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). CONTEXT: Tier: ${tier}, Accent: ${inf.accent}, URL: ${inf.url || 'https://your-app.vercel.app'}, Provisioned: ${provisionedServices.join(', ') || 'none yet'}, Deferred: ${deferredServices.join(', ') || 'none'}, Env vars: ${envVars.split('\n').filter(l => l.includes('=')).map(l => l.split('=')[0].trim()).slice(0, 20).join(', ')} REQUIREMENTS: Complete self-contained HTML (inline CSS + JS, no external deps). Include: 1. Branded header with accent color ${inf.accent} 2. Progress bar updating as items checked 3. Checklist sections (CRITICAL: Clerk/Stripe/DB, IMPORTANT: domain/email/analytics, OPTIONAL: social/SEO/chatbot) 4. Each item: checkbox (localStorage "onboard_${inf.repo}"), service name, expandable instructions, copy-to-clipboard for env vars, vendor dashboard links, status indicator 5. Verify Environment section 6. Responsive + dark mode 7. Footer linking to MANUAL-ACTIONS.md and .env.example Style: Clean, modern, card-based. System font stack. Return ONLY HTML.`, 6000, 'standard'), // ── g8_playbook ── aiRaw(`Generate a 90-day launch playbook for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). CONTEXT: Tier: ${tier} (${tierConfig.label}), Revenue: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price}, Provisioned: ${provisionedServices.join(', ') || 'none (Foundation — all manual)'}, Deferred: ${deferredServices.join(', ') || 'standard integrations'}, Key files: ${deliverables.join(', ')}, Has seed data: ${files['src/scripts/seed.ts'] ? 'yes' : 'no'}, Has tests: ${files['src/__tests__/api.test.ts'] ? 'yes' : 'no'}, Has onboard: ${files['public/onboard.html'] ? 'yes' : 'no'} Structure: # 90-Day Launch Playbook ## Week 1: Foundation (Days 1-7) [Day-by-day: onboard.html, seed script, test suite, branding, domain, email, soft launch] ## Weeks 2-4: Validation (Days 8-28) [First users, social calendar, analytics, feedback. ${tier === 'foundation' ? 'DIY integrations from MANUAL-ACTIONS.md' : 'Verify auto-provisioned integrations'}] ## Weeks 5-8: Growth (Days 29-56) [First revenue, activate deferred modules, A/B test messaging, social proof, SEO] ## Weeks 9-12: Scale (Days 57-90) [Revenue targets, hiring signals, automation, tier upgrade triggers, retrospective] ## Key Files Reference [Table: action → specific repo file] ## Upgrade Triggers [Signals to upgrade tier] Rules: Every item references a specific file/URL. Be specific. Realistic time estimates. Label as "suggested playbook" not advice. Return ONLY markdown.`, 6000, 'standard'), ]); // Process onboard result const onboardHtml = _g8obR?.status === 'fulfilled' ? _g8obR.value : ''; if (onboardHtml && (onboardHtml.includes(' 500) { files['LAUNCH-PLAYBOOK.md'] = playbookMd; setP('g8pb', 'ok', 'LAUNCH-PLAYBOOK.md'); } else { setP('g8pb', 'sk', 'Generation insufficient'); } } // Authority sites: content files pushed by authority_deploy in provision.js // Strategy docs still need to be pushed separately if (isAuthority) { const strategyDocs = Object.entries(files).filter(([p]) => p.endsWith('.md') || p === '.env.example' || p === '.gitignore' || p.startsWith('docs/') || p.startsWith('.github/') ); if (strategyDocs.length > 0) { addP('auth-docs', `Pushing ${strategyDocs.length} strategy docs`); setP('auth-docs', 'run'); for (let i = 0; i < strategyDocs.length; i++) { const [p, c] = strategyDocs[i]; if (!c || c.includes('[Generation incomplete]')) continue; try { await pushF(`${ORG}/${inf.repo}`, p, c, `docs: dynasty strategy — ${p}`); } catch {} setP('auth-docs', 'run', `${i+1}/${strategyDocs.length} — ${p}`); } setP('auth-docs', 'ok', `${strategyDocs.length} strategy docs pushed`); } } // Non-authority types: push all files to standalone repo if (!isAuthority) { // For backend-only builds, generate a landing page so Vercel serves something if (V2_DEPLOY_TARGET !== 'fullstack' && V2_DEPLOY_TARGET !== 'authority' && !files['index.html']) { if (V2_DEPLOY_TARGET === 'static') { // Generate a proper lead-capture landing page via AI addP('static-page', 'Generating static landing page', '', '⬡ Landing Page'); setP('static-page', 'run'); try { const lpHtml = await aiRaw(`Generate a complete, production-ready single-page HTML landing page. PROJECT: ${inf.name} DESCRIPTION: ${desc} ACCENT COLOR: ${inf.accent} REVENUE MODEL: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price} Requirements: - Self-contained single HTML file (inline CSS, no external dependencies except Google Fonts) - Dark theme with accent color ${inf.accent} - Sections: Hero with headline + subheadline + CTA button, 3 Benefits/Features, How It Works (3 steps), Pricing, FAQ (4 items), Email signup form, Footer - Modern design: subtle gradients, card-based layout, responsive (mobile-first) - Email form should use formsubmit.co or just a mailto: link - Include meta tags for SEO (title, description, og:image placeholder) - Include the PostHog analytics snippet at the bottom: ${getPostHogSnippet().replace(/.`, 8000); if (lpHtml && (lpHtml.includes(' ${inf.name}
Dynasty Empire

${inf.name}

Backend-only deployment. Clone the repo and start building with Claude Code or Pi Agent.

View on GitHub README SPEC
gh repo clone ${ORG}/${inf.repo} && cd ${inf.repo} && claude or pi
${getPostHogSnippet()}`; } } if (getBuildProfile().plainLanguage) { addP('plain', 'Plain-language companion docs', 'docs/plain/ — owner-friendly summaries'); setP('plain', 'run'); const _plainTargets = [ ['BUSINESS-SYSTEM.md', 'Business system'], ['REVENUE-MODEL.md', 'Revenue model'], ['GTM-PLAYBOOK.md', 'Go-to-market'], ['SPEC.md', 'Product spec'], ['FAILURE-MODES.md', 'Risk scenarios'], ['BANK-FUNDING-PLAYBOOK.md', 'Bank-ready funding'], ]; for (const [_pf, _plabel] of _plainTargets) { if (!files[_pf]) continue; try { const _pout = await aiRaw(`You rewrite technical strategy content for a small business owner who is NOT an MBA. Rules: - 8th–10th grade reading level - Define acronyms on first use - Add a "What this means for you" bullet list at the end - Keep all numbers and facts; do not invent data Source (${_plabel}): ${files[_pf].slice(0, 14000)}`, 6000, 'budget'); if (_pout && _pout.length > 200) { files['docs/plain/' + _pf] = `# ${_plabel} — Plain language companion > Technical original: \`/${_pf}\` (repo root) ${_pout}`; } } catch (_e) {} } files['docs/plain/START-HERE.md'] = `# Start here (plain language) Technical originals live at the repo root (e.g. BUSINESS-SYSTEM.md). This folder explains the same ideas in simpler language. For banks or investors, share the technical originals. - [Business system](./BUSINESS-SYSTEM.md) - [Revenue model](./REVENUE-MODEL.md) - [Go-to-market](./GTM-PLAYBOOK.md) `; qaPlainLanguageHeuristic(files); setP('plain', 'ok', 'docs/plain/*'); } try { dynastyScheduleCheckpointSave(desc, inf, { gateCompleted: true, prePush: true }); } catch {} const fl = Object.entries(files); // File review — show what's about to be pushed addP('review', `📋 ${fl.length} files ready for push`); setP('review', 'run', fl.filter(([,c])=>c&&!c.includes('[Generation incomplete]')).map(([p,c])=>{ const words = Math.round((c||'').split(/\s+/).length); return `${p} (${words.toLocaleString()} words)`; }).slice(0,8).join(' · ') + (fl.length > 8 ? ` · +${fl.length-8} more` : '')); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1500)); // Brief pause to show review setP('review', 'ok', `${fl.filter(([,c])=>c&&!c.includes('[Generation incomplete]')).length} files verified · ${Math.round(fl.reduce((sum,[,c])=>(c||'').length+sum,0)/5).toLocaleString()} words total`); updateEduCard('pf'); addP('pf',`Pushing ${fl.length} files`);setP('pf','run',`batching into 1 commit…`); updateGlobalProgress(8, 12, 'Phase 8 of 14 \u00b7 Pushing Files'); // Strategy: push ALL files in ONE git-tree commit. 10-20x faster than // per-file content API and dodges GitHub's secondary rate limits. // On batch failure, fall back to per-file pushF with retries so we // still land as much as possible, then report which critical files // are missing. let pushFails = 0; const pushedPaths = new Set(); const failedPaths = []; const batchFileMap = {}; for (const [p, c] of fl) { if (c && !c.includes('[Generation incomplete]')) batchFileMap[p] = c; } let batchOk = false; try { const batchResult = await pushFiles(`${ORG}/${inf.repo}`, batchFileMap, `feat: dynasty scaffold — ${Object.keys(batchFileMap).length} files`); if (batchResult.ok && batchResult.file_count > 0) { batchOk = true; for (const p of Object.keys(batchFileMap)) pushedPaths.add(p); setP('pf', 'ok', `${batchResult.file_count} files live · 1 commit (${(batchResult.commit_sha||'').slice(0,7)})`); } } catch (batchErr) { console.warn(`[pushFiles] batch commit failed, falling back to per-file: ${batchErr.message}`); setP('pf', 'run', 'Batch failed, falling back to per-file…'); } if (!batchOk) { // Fallback: per-file pushF with retries (pushF already wraps withRetry) for (let i = 0; i < fl.length; i++) { const [p, c] = fl[i]; if (!c || c.includes('[Generation incomplete]')) continue; try { await pushF(`${ORG}/${inf.repo}`, p, c, `feat: dynasty scaffold — ${p}`); pushedPaths.add(p); } catch (pushErr) { pushFails++; failedPaths.push(p); console.warn(`Push failed for ${p}: ${pushErr.message}`); } setP('pf', 'run', `${i+1}/${fl.length} — ${p}`); } setP('pf', pushFails > 0 ? 'sk' : 'ok', pushFails > 0 ? `${fl.length - pushFails} files pushed · ${pushFails} failed (fallback)` : `${fl.length} files live (fallback)`); } // ── HARD-FAIL if any critical file is missing ────────────────────── // Without package.json / src/app/page.tsx / src/app/globals.css the // Next.js build cannot succeed, so surface this as a real error rather // than silently proceeding and letting the verification gate bypass it. const missingCritical = failedPaths.filter(isCriticalPath); // Also check paths that weren't in the generated set at all const expectedCritical = CRITICAL_FILE_PATHS.filter(p => batchFileMap[p]); const hasAnyNextConfig = CRITICAL_CONFIG_FILES.some(p => pushedPaths.has(p) || batchFileMap[p]); if (!hasAnyNextConfig) { missingCritical.push('next.config.{js,ts,mjs} (none generated)'); } for (const p of expectedCritical) { if (!pushedPaths.has(p) && !missingCritical.includes(p)) missingCritical.push(p); } if (missingCritical.length > 0 && V2_DEPLOY_TARGET === 'fullstack') { setP('pf', 'er', `CRITICAL: missing ${missingCritical.join(', ')} — build will fail`); throw new Error(`Critical files missing from repo: ${missingCritical.join(', ')}. Aborting build — fix pushF errors or re-run.`); } try { if (getBuildProfile().archetype === 'demo_express') { window._v4Phase8DoneAt = Date.now(); if (window._buildFiles) { const _t0 = window._V4_DEMO_T0 || BUILD_START_TIME || Date.now(); const _p8 = window._v4Phase8DoneAt; const _sec = ((_p8 - _t0) / 1000).toFixed(1); const _met = (_p8 - _t0) / 1000 <= 300; window._buildFiles['docs/V4-DEMO-SLA.md'] = '# Demo / Express — SLA telemetry\n\n**Target:** ≤5 minutes from **start of code pipeline** (`proceedToBuild`) to **Git push complete** (Vercel build follows).\n\n| Metric | Value |\n|--------|-------|\n| Pipeline start (epoch ms) | ' + _t0 + ' |\n| Phase 8 complete (epoch ms) | ' + _p8 + ' |\n| Seconds to push | ' + _sec + ' |\n| ≤300s | ' + (_met ? 'Met' : 'Not met') + ' |\n\nDeferred modules: `BUILD-MANIFEST.json`.\n'; try { await pushF(`${ORG}/${inf.repo}`, 'docs/V4-DEMO-SLA.md', window._buildFiles['docs/V4-DEMO-SLA.md'], 'docs: V4 demo SLA telemetry'); } catch {} } } } catch {} // ── Push shareable report HTML to repo ──────────────────────────── try { const reportTitle = inf.name; const reportDate = new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', { year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric' }); const sc = V2_SCORECARD; const scoreHtml = sc ? `
${sc.verdict} — Score: ${sc.composite.toFixed(1)}/10
` : ''; const filesTable = Object.keys(window._buildFiles || {}).map(f => `
  • ${f}
    • `).join(''); // Build module results section for report const moduleReport = (() => { if (!provRes?.modules) return ''; const mods = provRes.modules; const okMods = Object.entries(mods).filter(([,v]) => v?.ok); const failMods = Object.entries(mods).filter(([,v]) => v && !v.ok && v.error); const skipMods = Object.entries(mods).filter(([,v]) => v && !v.ok && !v.error); let html = `

    Provisioned Services (${okMods.length} of ${Object.keys(mods).length})

    `; if (okMods.length) { html += '
    ' + okMods.map(([k,v]) => { const details = v.details || {}; const info = details.domain || details.product_id || details.list_id || details.phone_number || details.workspace_url || details.chatbot_id || ''; return `
    ✓ ${k}
    ${info || v.service || 'Provisioned'}
    `; }).join('') + '
    '; } if (failMods.length) { html += `

    Failed (${failMods.length})

    `; html += failMods.map(([k,v]) => `
    ✗ ${k}
    ${v.fallback || v.error}
    `).join(''); } if (skipMods.length) { html += `

    Skipped (${skipMods.length})

    `; html += '
    ' + skipMods.map(([k]) => k).join(', ') + '
    '; } // Infrastructure summary if (provRes.results) { html += '

    Infrastructure

    '; for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(provRes.results)) { const status = v.ok || v.package_id || v.price_id ? '#22C55E' : v.manual ? '#FBBF24' : '#F87171'; html += `
    ${k}
    `; } html += '
    '; } return html; })(); const reportHtml = `${reportTitle} — Build Report

    ${reportTitle}

    ${inf.type_label} · ${inf.revenue_model} · ${inf.price} · ${reportDate}

    ${scoreHtml}${moduleReport}

    Generated Files (${Object.keys(window._buildFiles||{}).length})

      ${filesTable}

    Quick Start

    gh repo clone ${ORG}/${inf.repo} && cd ${inf.repo} && claude

    Documentation

    MANUAL-ACTIONS.md
    Setup & configuration guide
    .env.example
    Environment variables template

    ${isWhiteLabel() ? '' : 'Built with Your Deputy V3 · '}${reportDate}

    `; await pushF(`${ORG}/${inf.repo}`, 'REPORT.html', reportHtml, 'docs: shareable build report'); } catch {} } // ── Live tool provisioning ──────────────────────────────────────────── // ── Live tool provisioning (type-aware: 20i/Neon/Vercel/Stripe/Acumbamail) ─ provRes=null; const provisionSvcs=['stripe','acumbamail','n8n','comms']; { const isWP=inf?.type_id==='wp-theme'||inf?.type_id==='wordpress'||V2_DEPLOY_TARGET==='wordpress'; const isStaticType=['static','portfolio','landing'].includes(inf?.type_id||'')||V2_DEPLOY_TARGET==='static'; const provLabel=isWP?'Provisioning WordPress hosting':isStaticType?'Provisioning static hosting':'Provisioning cloud infrastructure'; const provNote=isWP||isStaticType?'Hosting + payments + email marketing':'Hosting, database, payments, email marketing'; updateEduCard('prov'); addP('prov',provLabel,provNote);setP('prov','run'); updateGlobalProgress(9, 12, 'Phase 9 of 14 \u00b7 Provisioning'); try{ const pr=await fetch('/api/provision?action=provision',{method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders()}, body:JSON.stringify({ inf: {...inf, deploy_target: V2_DEPLOY_TARGET}, svcs:provisionSvcs, custom_domain: localStorage.getItem('dynasty_custom_domain') || null, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() })}); const pd=await pr.json(); provRes=pd; if(pd.ok){ const done=Object.entries(pd.results||{}) .filter(([,v])=>v.ok||v.package_id||v.price_id||v.queued) .map(([k])=>k); const manualCount=(pd.manual_steps||[]).length; setP('prov','ok',`${done.join(', ')||'setup complete'}${manualCount?' ('+manualCount+' manual)':''}`); }else{setP('prov','sk','Partial — check manual_steps in API response');} }catch{setP('prov','sk','Provision API offline — check dashboard and retry');} } // ── Direct Vercel deploy (no Flint needed) ────────────────────────────── if(isAuthority){ updateEduCard('deploy'); addP('deploy','Deploying authority site to Vercel','Forking template → AI content generation → Google directory → auto-build');setP('deploy','run'); updateGlobalProgress(10, 12, 'Phase 10 of 14 \u00b7 Deploying'); try{ const deployResp=await fetch('/api/provision',{method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders()}, body:JSON.stringify({ action:'authority_deploy', project_slug:inf.repo, niche_name:inf.name, niche_config:nc?.niche_config||'', theme_css:null, accent_hex:inf.accent, domain:inf.domain||`${inf.repo}.vercel.app`, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() })}); const dd=await deployResp.json(); if(dd.ok){ setP('deploy','ok',`Live in ~90s → ${dd.vercel_url}`); LAUNCH_URL=dd.vercel_url; // Carry authority_deploy's Vercel project ID into provRes so verification loop can use it if (!provRes) provRes = { results: {} }; if (!provRes.results) provRes.results = {}; provRes.results.vercel = { ok: true, url: dd.vercel_url, project_id: dd.vercel_project_id }; }else{ setP('deploy','warn',dd.error||'Deploy triggered — check hosting dashboard'); } }catch(e){ setP('deploy','warn','Deploy queued — check https://vercel.com/~/projects'); } } // Quality validation — check generated files for placeholders const criticalFiles = { 'DESIGN.md': df?.design_md || '', 'SPEC.md': dc?.spec_md || '', 'BUSINESS-SYSTEM.md': bz?.biz_system || '', 'backend/main.py': be?.main_py || '', }; let qualityOk = true; try { const vr = await fetch('/api/validate', { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({ files: criticalFiles, projectName: inf.name || '', category: typeof V2_CATEGORY !== 'undefined' ? V2_CATEGORY : inf.type_id, }) }); const vd = await vr.json(); if (!vd.ok) { qualityOk = false; const warn = Object.entries(vd.issues).map(([f,issues]) => `${f}: ${issues.length} issue(s)`).join(' | '); addP('qv', `Quality check: ${vd.total_issues} placeholder(s) found`, warn, '⬡ Quality'); setP('qv', 'sk', warn); } } catch {} // ── V3 MODULE PROVISIONING ──────────────────────────────────────────── // After deployment succeeds, provision all enabled V3 integration modules const v3ModulesRaw = getEnabledModules(); const _bpV3 = getBuildProfile(); if (_bpV3.verticalTool) v3ModulesRaw.vertical_tool = true; // Client-side tier filtering — only send modules allowed by user's tier const userTier = getUserTier(); const tierAllowed = V3_TIERS[userTier]?.modules || []; const v3Modules = {}; for (const [mod, on] of Object.entries(v3ModulesRaw)) { // Free/Foundation tiers (empty modules array) = NO modules. Only Professional+ get modules. v3Modules[mod] = on && tierAllowed.length > 0 && tierAllowed.includes(mod); } const anyModulesEnabled = Object.values(v3Modules).some(v => v); let v3Results = null; if (anyModulesEnabled) { const liveUrl = LAUNCH_URL || provRes?.results?.vercel?.url || `https://${inf.repo}.vercel.app`; const v3Project = getV3Project(inf); const moduleNames = { hosting: 'Domain + Email', billing: 'Stripe Billing', email: 'Email Marketing', phone: 'Phone + Voice', sms: 'SMS Campaigns', chatbot: 'Website Chatbot', seo: 'SEO Content', video: 'Explainer Video', design: 'Design Assets', analytics: 'Analytics', leads: 'Lead Intelligence', automation: 'Automation Workflows', docs: 'Legal PDFs', crm: 'CRM Portal', directory: 'Directory', wordpress: 'WordPress', social: 'Social Scheduling', vertical_tool: 'Niche vertical tool scaffold' }; const enabledList = Object.entries(v3Modules).filter(([,v]) => v).map(([k]) => moduleNames[k] || k); addP('v3-start', `V3 Modules — ${enabledList.length} integrations`, enabledList.join(', ')); setP('v3-start', 'run', 'Provisioning external services...'); if ((localStorage.getItem('dynasty_automation_mode') || 'zero_cost') === 'zero_cost') { addP('v3-mode', 'Zero-cost autopilot'); setP('v3-mode', 'sk', 'Paid integrations auto-skip in this mode to keep marginal cost near zero.'); } updateGlobalProgress(10, 14, 'Phase 10 of 14 · V3 Module Provisioning'); try { const v3Resp = await fetch('/api/provision?action=provision_modules', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ project: v3Project, liveUrl, modules_enabled: v3Modules, ...getProvisionAuthPayload(), build_profile: getBuildProfile(), }) }); v3Results = await v3Resp.json(); if (v3Results.ok) { // Show per-module results for (const [mod, res] of Object.entries(v3Results.modules || {})) { const label = moduleNames[mod] || mod; if (res.ok) { const detail = Object.entries(res.details || {}).filter(([k]) => !k.includes('error') && !k.includes('snippet') && !k.includes('script')).slice(0, 2).map(([k, v]) => `${k}: ${typeof v === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(v).slice(0, 40) : String(v).slice(0, 40)}`).join(' · '); addP(`v3-${mod}`, label); setP(`v3-${mod}`, 'ok', detail || 'Provisioned'); } else if (res.error) { addP(`v3-${mod}`, label); setP(`v3-${mod}`, 'sk', res.fallback || res.error); } } setP('v3-start', 'ok', `${v3Results.summary?.succeeded || 0}/${v3Results.summary?.total || 0} modules provisioned`); // Show gated modules warning if (v3Results.gated?.modules?.length) { addP('v3-gated', `Tier upgrade available`); setP('v3-gated', 'sk', v3Results.gated.message); } // Add V3 module costs to build total if (v3Results.totalCost) V2_BUILD_COST += v3Results.totalCost; // Store module results for done screen if (provRes) provRes.modules = v3Results.modules; else provRes = { results: {}, modules: v3Results.modules }; } else { setP('v3-start', 'sk', v3Results.error || 'Module provisioning failed'); } } catch (e) { setP('v3-start', 'sk', 'Module API error — skipped in automation-only mode'); } } // ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ // ── PHASE 11-12: MULTI-PASS AUDIT-FIX-RECHECK VERIFICATION ────────────── // Pass 1 AUDIT → pass 2 FIX → passes 3–5 RECHECK/stabilize (live + deploy can lag). // UI must not claim "live / complete" unless completion gate + quality agree. // ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ const VERIFY_MAX_PASS = 5; addP('verify', `Deployment Verification — ${VERIFY_MAX_PASS}-pass audit`, 'Audit → Fix → Multi recheck for each system'); setP('verify', 'run'); updateGlobalProgress(11, 14, 'Phase 11 of 14 · Multi-pass verification'); const vercelUrl = provRes?.results?.vercel?.url; const vercelProjectId = provRes?.results?.vercel?.project_id; const verifyResults = { github: false, vercel: false, live: false, route_coverage: false, issues: [], fixes_applied: [] }; for (let pass = 1; pass <= VERIFY_MAX_PASS; pass++) { const passLabel = pass === 1 ? 'Audit' : pass === 2 ? 'Fix' : `Recheck (${pass - 2}/${VERIFY_MAX_PASS - 2})`; setP('verify', 'run', `Pass ${pass}/${VERIFY_MAX_PASS}: ${passLabel}...`); addP(`verify-pass-${pass}`, `${passLabel} pass`); setP(`verify-pass-${pass}`, 'run'); // ── CHECK 1: GitHub repo has critical Next.js files ───────────────── // Old check just counted repo-root entries >= 5 — trivially passes on // a bare template fork. Instead verify each critical path exists. if (!verifyResults.github) { try { const ghPlan = buildGithubVerifyPlan(); const missing = []; for (const p of ghPlan.required) { try { const r = await gh(`/repos/${ORG}/${inf.repo}/contents/${encodeGithubContentPath(p)}`); if (!r.ok) missing.push(p); } catch { missing.push(p); } } for (const group of ghPlan.oneOfGroups) { let anyOk = false; for (const p of group) { try { const r = await gh(`/repos/${ORG}/${inf.repo}/contents/${encodeGithubContentPath(p)}`); if (r.ok) { anyOk = true; break; } } catch {} } if (!anyOk) missing.push(`one of: ${group.join(' | ')}`); } if (ghPlan.needNextConfig) { let hasConfig = false; for (const cfg of ['next.config.js', 'next.config.ts', 'next.config.mjs']) { try { const r = await gh(`/repos/${ORG}/${inf.repo}/contents/${cfg}`); if (r.ok) { hasConfig = true; break; } } catch {} } if (!hasConfig) missing.push('next.config.{js,ts,mjs}'); } if (missing.length === 0) { verifyResults.github = true; } else if (pass === 2) { // FIX pass: attempt to re-push the critical files from the in-memory // build. If any are missing in memory too, record and continue. verifyResults.fixes_applied.push(`Re-pushing ${missing.length} missing critical files`); for (const p of missing) { const content = (window._buildFiles || {})[p]; if (content) { try { await pushF(`${ORG}/${inf.repo}`, p, content, `fix: re-push missing ${p}`); } catch {} } } await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 3000)); } else { verifyResults.issues.push(`GitHub missing (pass ${pass}): ${missing.join(', ')}`); } } catch (e) { if (pass < VERIFY_MAX_PASS) await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 5000)); // wait and retry } } // ── CHECK 2: Vercel deployment is READY ───────────────────────────── if (!verifyResults.vercel && vercelProjectId) { try { const depCheck = await fetch(`/api/provision?action=verify_deploy`, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ project_id: vercelProjectId, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); const depData = await depCheck.json(); if (depData.state === 'READY') { verifyResults.vercel = true; } else if (pass === 2) { // FIX: take corrective action based on failure type if (depData.state === 'ERROR') { verifyResults.fixes_applied.push('Vercel deploy ERROR — triggering retry with framework fix'); setP(`verify-pass-${pass}`, 'run', 'Deploy failed — retrying...'); await fetch('/api/provision?action=retry_deploy', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ project_id: vercelProjectId, repo: inf.repo, org: ORG, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 20000)); // wait for retry to build } else if (depData.state === 'NOT_FOUND' || !depData.state) { verifyResults.fixes_applied.push('No deployment found — triggering fresh deploy'); setP(`verify-pass-${pass}`, 'run', 'No deployment — triggering...'); await fetch('/api/provision?action=retry_deploy', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ project_id: vercelProjectId, repo: inf.repo, org: ORG, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 20000)); } else { // BUILDING or QUEUED — just wait setP(`verify-pass-${pass}`, 'run', `Vercel: ${depData.state} — waiting...`); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 30000)); } } else if (pass >= 3 && pass <= VERIFY_MAX_PASS && !verifyResults.vercel) { // RECHECK: poll up to 60 more seconds for (let poll = 0; poll < 4; poll++) { await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 15000)); try { const rc = await fetch(`/api/provision?action=verify_deploy`, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ project_id: vercelProjectId, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); const rd = await rc.json(); setP(`verify-pass-${pass}`, 'run', `Recheck: Vercel ${rd.state} (${(poll+1)*15}s)`); if (rd.state === 'READY') { verifyResults.vercel = true; break; } if (rd.state === 'ERROR') { verifyResults.issues.push('Vercel build failed after retry — check build logs'); break; } } catch {} } } } catch {} } else if (!vercelProjectId && vercelUrl) { verifyResults.vercel = true; // no project ID but URL exists (manual deploy) } // ── CHECK 3: Live URL serves real, project-specific content ──────── // Strict criteria: must be 200, must contain the project name, and // must NOT contain template-branding leaks. Removes the previous // "200 but thin content → still live" bypass that let bare template // pages pass as "live". if (!verifyResults.live && vercelUrl && verifyResults.vercel) { try { if (pass > 1) await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 8000)); // DNS propagation const liveCheck = await fetch(`/api/provision?action=verify_live`, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ url: vercelUrl, project_name: inf.name, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); const liveData = await liveCheck.json(); const strictOk = liveData.ok && liveData.status === 200 && liveData.has_content && liveData.has_project_name && !liveData.has_template_branding; let routesOk = true; const routeFailures = []; if (strictOk) { const bpNow = getBuildProfile(); const expectedRoutePaths = ['/docs', '/pricing', '/about']; if (['starter_5p', 'growth', 'authority_site', 'enterprise_full'].includes(bpNow?.archetype || '')) { expectedRoutePaths.push('/about', '/services', '/contact'); } for (const path of expectedRoutePaths) { try { const routeCheck = await fetch(`/api/provision?action=verify_live`, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ url: `${String(vercelUrl).replace(/\/$/, '')}${path}`, project_name: inf.name, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); const routeData = await routeCheck.json(); const routePass = routeData.ok && routeData.status === 200 && routeData.has_content && !routeData.has_template_branding; if (!routePass) { routesOk = false; routeFailures.push(`${path} (${routeData.status || 0})`); } } catch { routesOk = false; routeFailures.push(`${path} (check failed)`); } } } if (strictOk && routesOk) { verifyResults.live = true; verifyResults.content_verified = true; verifyResults.route_coverage = true; } else if (liveData.ok && liveData.status === 200) { // 200 but failing strict criteria — do NOT mark live. Record why. const whyFailed = []; if (!liveData.has_content) whyFailed.push('thin body'); if (!liveData.has_project_name) whyFailed.push('project name missing from homepage'); if (liveData.has_template_branding) whyFailed.push('template branding leaked (SaaS Template / Ixartz / nextjs-boilerplate)'); if (routeFailures.length) whyFailed.push(`route coverage failed: ${routeFailures.join(', ')}`); verifyResults.issues.push(`Live: 200 but ${whyFailed.join(', ')}`); if (pass === 2 && liveData.has_template_branding) { // FIX: trigger a retry deploy — may pick up newer files verifyResults.fixes_applied.push('Template branding leaked — triggering retry_deploy'); if (vercelProjectId) { try { await fetch('/api/provision?action=retry_deploy', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ project_id: vercelProjectId, repo: inf.repo, org: ORG, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 25000)); } catch {} } } else if (pass === 2 && routeFailures.length) { verifyResults.fixes_applied.push('Route coverage failed — triggering retry_deploy'); if (vercelProjectId) { try { await fetch('/api/provision?action=retry_deploy', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ project_id: vercelProjectId, repo: inf.repo, org: ORG, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 25000)); } catch {} } } } else if (pass === 2) { // FIX: check if it's a 500 (code error) vs 404 (not deployed yet) if (liveData.status >= 500) { verifyResults.fixes_applied.push(`Live URL returns ${liveData.status} — server error in deployed code`); verifyResults.issues.push(`Live: HTTP ${liveData.status} — app has a runtime error`); } else if (liveData.status === 404 || liveData.status === 0) { verifyResults.fixes_applied.push('Live URL not responding — DNS may still be propagating'); // Nothing to fix — just needs time } } else if (pass >= 3 && pass <= VERIFY_MAX_PASS) { // Stabilization passes — wait longer each round, then strict homepage recheck await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 12000 + (pass - 3) * 8000)); try { const rc = await fetch(`/api/provision?action=verify_live`, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...getProvisionAuthHeaders() }, body: JSON.stringify({ url: vercelUrl, project_name: inf.name, ...getProvisionAuthPayload() }) }); const rd = await rc.json(); if (rd.ok && rd.status === 200 && rd.has_content && rd.has_project_name && !rd.has_template_branding) { verifyResults.live = true; } } catch {} } } catch {} } // ── Pass summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── const passChecks = [ verifyResults.github ? '✓ GitHub' : '✗ GitHub', (verifyResults.vercel || !vercelProjectId) ? '✓ Vercel' : '✗ Vercel', (verifyResults.live || !vercelUrl) ? '✓ Live' : '✗ Live', (verifyResults.route_coverage || !vercelUrl) ? '✓ Routes' : '✗ Routes' ]; const allPassed = verifyResults.github && (verifyResults.vercel || !vercelProjectId) && (verifyResults.live || !vercelUrl) && (verifyResults.route_coverage || !vercelUrl); if (allPassed) { setP(`verify-pass-${pass}`, 'ok', passChecks.join(' · ')); // All checks passed — skip remaining passes if (pass < VERIFY_MAX_PASS) { for (let skip = pass + 1; skip <= VERIFY_MAX_PASS; skip++) { addP(`verify-pass-${skip}`, skip === 2 ? 'Fix pass' : 'Recheck pass'); setP(`verify-pass-${skip}`, 'ok', 'Skipped — all checks already passed'); } } break; } else { const fixNote = pass === 2 && verifyResults.fixes_applied.length ? ` · Fixes: ${verifyResults.fixes_applied.length}` : ''; setP(`verify-pass-${pass}`, pass === VERIFY_MAX_PASS ? 'er' : 'sk', passChecks.join(' · ') + fixNote); } // Brief pause between passes if (pass < VERIFY_MAX_PASS && !allPassed) await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 3000)); } // ── Final verification summary ──────────────────────────────────────── const finalGood = verifyResults.github && (verifyResults.vercel || !vercelProjectId) && (verifyResults.live || !vercelUrl) && (verifyResults.route_coverage || !vercelUrl); if (finalGood) { setP('verify', 'ok', `${VERIFY_MAX_PASS}-pass verification complete — all systems confirmed live`); } else { const stillFailing = []; if (!verifyResults.github) stillFailing.push('GitHub'); if (!verifyResults.vercel && vercelProjectId) stillFailing.push('Vercel'); if (!verifyResults.live && vercelUrl) stillFailing.push('Live URL'); if (!verifyResults.route_coverage && vercelUrl) stillFailing.push('Route coverage'); setP('verify', 'er', `Verification incomplete after ${VERIFY_MAX_PASS} passes — ${stillFailing.join(', ')} failed`); if (verifyResults.fixes_applied.length) { addP('verify-fixes', `${verifyResults.fixes_applied.length} fixes attempted`); setP('verify-fixes', 'sk', verifyResults.fixes_applied.join(' · ')); } } // ── PHASE 12: Definition of Done Summary ───────────────────────────────── // HARD FAIL if any critical check is false for a project that expected // Vercel deployment. Previous behavior: skip checks for missing Vercel URL // and silently showed a "complete" UI even when the homepage was broken. addP('done-gate', 'Build Completion Check'); const expectsLiveSite = !!vercelProjectId && !!vercelUrl; const doneChecks = { github: verifyResults.github, // If a Vercel project was created, it MUST reach READY — no "skip if none" vercel: expectsLiveSite ? verifyResults.vercel : true, // If a live URL exists, it MUST serve project-specific content live: expectsLiveSite ? verifyResults.live : true, routes: expectsLiveSite ? verifyResults.route_coverage : true, files: Object.keys(files).length >= 10, quality: qualityOk, }; const doneCount = Object.values(doneChecks).filter(Boolean).length; const doneTotal = Object.keys(doneChecks).length; const failed = Object.entries(doneChecks).filter(([,v]) => !v).map(([k]) => k); if (doneCount === doneTotal) { setP('done-gate', 'ok', `${doneCount}/${doneTotal} checks passed — all completion gates satisfied`); } else { setP('done-gate', 'er', `${doneCount}/${doneTotal} passed · Failed: ${failed.join(', ')}`); } updateGlobalProgress(12, 12, 'Complete!'); document.getElementById('global-progress').style.display = 'none'; // If the build actually failed, render the done-screen as usual so the // user can still inspect what DID generate, then prepend a red error // banner with actionable next steps (err-box lives inside prog-screen // which showDone hides, so we inject directly into done-screen). const customerDeployReady = !expectsLiveSite || finalGood; const customerFullReady = doneCount === doneTotal && customerDeployReady && qualityOk; window._buildOutcome = { expectsLiveSite, finalGood, doneCount, doneTotal, doneChecks, failed, qualityOk, customerFullReady, customerDeployReady, }; showDone(inf,repo,isAuthority ? 0 : Object.keys(files).length,inf.manual_svcs||[]); if (doneCount < doneTotal) { const nextSteps = []; if (!doneChecks.github) nextSteps.push('Missing critical files in GitHub repo — inspect Vercel build logs or re-run the build.'); if (!doneChecks.vercel) nextSteps.push(`Vercel deployment did not reach READY. Check https://vercel.com/~/projects/${inf.repo}/deployments for the build error.`); if (!doneChecks.live) nextSteps.push(`Live URL ${vercelUrl || ''} did not return project-specific content. Common causes: build error, template branding leak, or DNS still propagating.`); if (!doneChecks.routes) nextSteps.push('Required routes failed content checks (e.g., /docs or /pricing or package routes).'); if (!doneChecks.quality) nextSteps.push('Quality gate flagged placeholders or invalid patterns in critical docs/code — expand the Quality step in the build log, fix in-repo, then redeploy if needed.'); if (verifyResults.issues?.length) nextSteps.push('Issues detected: ' + verifyResults.issues.join(' · ')); const banner = document.createElement('div'); banner.style.cssText = 'background:rgba(229,83,75,0.08);border:1px solid #E5534B;border-radius:var(--rl,8px);padding:1rem 1.25rem;margin-bottom:1.25rem;color:#EEEDE8'; banner.innerHTML = `
    ⚠️ Build did not complete successfully (${failed.join(', ')} failed)
    ` + '
      ' + nextSteps.map(s => `
    • ${s}
      • `).join('') + '
    '; const doneScreen = document.getElementById('done-screen'); if (doneScreen) doneScreen.insertBefore(banner, doneScreen.firstChild); } } async function build(desc,inf,buildOpts){ buildOpts = buildOpts || {}; const repo=`${ORG}/${inf.repo}`; try{ if (buildOpts.resume) { const ck = await dynastyLoadBuildCheckpoint(); if (!ck || ck.v !== DYNASTY_CKPT_VER) throw new Error('No valid saved session. Run a new build to create a checkpoint.'); if (ck.inf && inf && ck.inf.repo !== inf.repo) throw new Error('Saved session is for a different repo (' + ck.inf.repo + '). Open that project or discard the checkpoint.'); desc = ck.desc || desc; if (ck.inf) Object.assign(inf, ck.inf); INFERRED = inf; const files = ck.files; window._buildFiles = files; window._socialCalendarMd = ck.socialCalendarMd || ''; if (ck.nicheConfig) window._nicheConfig = ck.nicheConfig; isAuthority = !!ck.isAuthority; if (ck.v2DeployTarget) V2_DEPLOY_TARGET = ck.v2DeployTarget; if (ck.v2Category) V2_CATEGORY = ck.v2Category; if (ck.prgs && ck.prgs.length) { PRGS = ck.prgs.map(p => ({ id: p.id, l: p.l, n: p.n, s: p.s, g: p.g })); renderProg(); } dynastyEnsureCheckpointLoop(desc, inf); await dynastyContinueBuildFromPostMerge(desc, inf, files, { resume: true, gateCompleted: !!ck.gateCompleted, prePush: !!ck.prePush }); return; } updateEduCard('g1'); addP('g1','Generating DESIGN.md + CLAUDE.md','','⬡ Design System');setP('g1','run'); updateGlobalProgress(1, 12, 'Phase 1 of 14 \u00b7 Design System'); let df;try{ df=await ai(`GENERATE DYNASTY EMPIRE DESIGN FILES. No preamble. Return ONLY in the delimiter format below. ${cx(desc,inf)} Master tokens: dynasty-black #0A0A0A, dynasty-gold #C9A84C, 8px spacing, shadow-border rgba(0,0,0,0.08) 0px 0px 0px 1px, WCAG AA required. Generate following the awesome-design-md format (VoltAgent/awesome-design-md). Each section must be specific, evocative, and technically precise — not generic. Include exact hex values, rgba shadows, font weights, letter-spacing values, and border-radius tokens. The Visual Theme section should read like a design critic's review of the product — specific enough that an AI agent can recreate the aesthetic from description alone. IMPORTANT DESIGN SYSTEM RULES (from awesome-design-md best practices): - Shadow colors should use brand-tinted rgba values (like Stripe's rgba(50,50,93,0.25)), not generic black - Headlines should use negative letter-spacing at display sizes (progressive tightening) - Border philosophy should be explicitly stated (whisper-weight like Notion, or pronounced like IBM) - Every interactive component needs 6 states: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading - Color roles must distinguish between: page background, card surface, primary text, secondary text, muted text, primary accent, accent hover, accent light, borders, shadows, focus ring - Status system must define: verified/active, pending, expired/error, draft, admin — each with bg/text/border hex ---BEGIN:design_md--- # DESIGN.md — ${inf.name} > Inherits from: DYNASTY-DESIGN.md ## 1. Visual Theme & Atmosphere [3 evocative paragraphs with precise technical descriptions — like a design critic's review] Key Characteristics: - [8 precise technical bullets with exact values] ## 2. Color Palette & Roles | Color | Hex | Role | [Complete table: page background, card surface, primary text, secondary text, muted text, primary accent ${inf.accent}, accent hover, accent light, borders, shadows (brand-tinted rgba), focus ring] [Status system table: verified/pending/expired/draft/admin with bg/text/border hex for each] [Dynasty Gold #C9A84C — premium tier markers only] ## 3. Typography Rules Font Stack: | Role | Font | Fallback | [Font stack table with OpenType features] Hierarchy: | Role | Font | Size | Weight | Line Height | Letter Spacing | Features | Use | [14+ rows — display sizes use negative letter-spacing (progressive tightening)] ## 4. Component Stylings [Exact specs for: Primary button (all 6 states), Secondary button, Destructive button, Standard card, Featured card, Status badges (always pill 9999px + text label), Form inputs (default/hover/focus/active/disabled/loading), Navigation] ## 5. Layout Principles [Page structure, spacing scale (8px base), grid system, whitespace philosophy] ## 6. Depth & Elevation | Level | Shadow Value | Use | [6 levels from flat to modal with exact brand-tinted rgba shadow values] ## 7. Do's and Don'ts Do: [12 specific rules for this product] Don't: [12 specific anti-patterns] ## 8. Responsive Behavior | Breakpoint | Width | Key Changes | [4 breakpoints + collapsing strategy] ## 9. Agent Prompt Guide Quick Color Reference: [table] Example Component Prompts: [5 copy-paste-ready prompts] Iteration Guide: [7-point guide] ---END:design_md--- ---BEGIN:claude_md--- # CLAUDE.md — ${inf.name} Read DYNASTY-DESIGN.md then DESIGN.md before any UI code. ## Project Overview Stack: ${(inf.stack||[]).join(', ')} Repo: ${ORG}/${inf.repo} Domain: [to be configured] ## Design Rules MANDATORY [10 critical rules with exact hex values, border technique, focus ring color, font rules, status badge requirement, what NOT to use from other Dynasty products] ## Required States Every interactive component must implement: default / hover / focus / active / disabled / loading ## Architecture Rules [File conventions for ${(inf.stack||[]).slice(0,3).join('/')}. Database rules. Auth pattern. Third-party service conventions.] ## Environment Variables [All likely vars for this stack, grouped by service with comments] ## Git Rules Always run before committing: git config user.email "you@example.com" git config user.name "your-username" ## Do Not [14 specific prohibitions — include colors from other Dynasty products, and any anti-patterns specific to this stack] ---END:claude_md--- `, ['design_md','claude_md'], 8000); setP('g1','ok',`${((df.design_md?.length||0)/1000).toFixed(1)}k chars`); // Context Compaction — compress design decisions for downstream phases await compactPhase('design', df); }catch(e){setP('g1','er',e.message);throw e;} updateEduCard('g2'); addP('g2','Generating SPEC + ROADMAP + README + MANUAL-ACTIONS + .env','','⬡ Product Docs');setP('g2','run'); updateGlobalProgress(2, 12, 'Phase 2 of 14 \u00b7 Product Docs'); let dc;try{ dc=await ai(`You are a senior product strategist at a world-class consulting firm. Generate board-ready product documentation. Write with precision and authority. Use numbered sections, professional tables, and specific measurable criteria. No AI clichés. No hedging. Every feature has acceptance criteria. Every metric has a target. Cross-reference other Dynasty documents. Return ONLY in delimiter format. No preamble. ${cx(desc,inf)} ---BEGIN:spec_md--- # Product Requirements Document — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire Product Specification | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', {year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric'})} --- ## Executive Summary [3-4 sentences: What this product is, the specific market gap it fills, the target revenue within 12 months, and why now is the right time to build it. Reference competitive landscape from BUSINESS-SYSTEM.md.] --- ## 1. Product Vision & Strategy ### 1.1 Vision Statement [1 crisp paragraph: The world this product creates. Specific, not aspirational — "Reduces [X process] from [Y hours] to [Z minutes] for [target segment]"] ### 1.2 Strategic Context [1 paragraph: How this product fits within Dynasty Empire's portfolio. Reference market research data if available.] --- ## 2. Target Users ### 2.1 Primary Persona: [Name, Title] | Attribute | Detail | |---|---| | **Demographics** | [Age range, job title, company size, industry] | | **Goals** | [Top 3 professional goals] | | **Frustrations** | [Top 3 pain points with current solutions] | | **Quote** | "[A sentence this persona would say about their problem]" | | **Success Metric** | [How they measure if a tool is working — e.g., "time saved per week"] | ### 2.2 Secondary Persona: [Name, Title] [Same table format] ### 2.3 Anti-Persona (Who This Is NOT For) [1 paragraph: Explicitly define who should NOT use this product and why. Prevents scope creep.] --- ## 3. Feature Specification ### 3.1 P0 — MVP (${inf.mvp}) | # | Feature | User Story | Acceptance Criteria | Effort | Dependencies | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | 1 | [Name] | As a [persona], I want [action] so that [outcome] | Given [context], When [action], Then [result] | [S/M/L/XL] | [None / Feature #] | | 2 | | | | | | | 3 | | | | | | | 4 | | | | | | | 5 | | | | | | | 6 | | | | | | [6-8 features] ### 3.2 P1 — Growth Phase | # | Feature | Value Proposition | Estimated Effort | Target Release | |---|---|---|---|---| [4-5 features with business justification] ### 3.3 P2 — Scale Phase [3-4 future features with strategic rationale] ### 3.4 Explicitly Out of Scope | Item | Reason for Exclusion | Reconsider When | |---|---|---| [4-5 items explicitly cut, with reasoning and trigger for reconsideration] --- ## 4. Non-Functional Requirements | Requirement | Target | Measurement | |---|---|---| | Page Load Time | <2 seconds (p95) | Lighthouse / Web Vitals | | API Response Time | <500ms (p95) | Application monitoring | | Uptime | 99.5% | Pulsetic | | Accessibility | WCAG 2.1 AA | axe-core audit | | Browser Support | Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest 2) | Manual + Playwright | | Mobile Responsive | Full functionality at 375px+ | Visual regression tests | | Data Encryption | AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit | Security audit | --- ## 5. Data Model Overview [Key entities described in plain language with relationships — "A User belongs to one Organization. An Organization has many Projects. Each Project contains Tasks..." Reference docs/DATA-MODEL.md for full schema.] --- ## 6. Integration Map | Service | Purpose | Priority | API Type | Env Var | |---|---|---|---|---| [All integrations with clear role and configuration reference] --- ## 7. Success Metrics | KPI | Target (3mo) | Target (12mo) | Measurement | Owner | |---|---|---|---|---| | [Activation metric] | [X] | [Y] | [How measured] | Product | | [Retention metric] | [X]% | [Y]% | | Product | | [Revenue metric] | $[X] | $[Y] | Stripe | Business | | [Engagement metric] | [X] | [Y] | PostHog | Product | | [Quality metric] | [X] | [Y] | | Engineering | ---END:spec_md--- ---BEGIN:roadmap_md--- # Development Roadmap — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire Engineering Plan | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', {year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric'})} --- ## Phase 1: MVP — ${inf.mvp} **Goal:** Ship core value proposition to first 10 customers **Done when:** [Binary success gate] | # | Task | Description | Effort | Depends On | Owner | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | 1.1 | [Task] | [Specific deliverable] | [S/M/L/XL] | — | Engineering | [12 tasks with dependencies mapped] **Phase 1 Exit Criteria:** - [ ] All P0 features pass acceptance criteria - [ ] CI/CD pipeline green - [ ] Production deployment verified - [ ] First customer onboarded --- ## Phase 2: Core Features (Weeks 3-6) **Goal:** [Specific goal with metric] **Gate:** Phase 1 exit criteria met + [X] active users | # | Task | Description | Effort | Business Impact | |---|---|---|---|---| [10 tasks with business justification] --- ## Phase 3: Growth & Polish (Weeks 7-10) **Goal:** Production hardening + growth features **Gate:** Phase 2 complete + $[X] MRR | # | Task | Category | Effort | |---|---|---|---| [8 tasks — reliability, performance, analytics, GTM integration] --- ## Phase 4: Scale (Post-launch) **Goal:** 10x growth infrastructure **Gate:** $[X] MRR + [N] active customers | # | Initiative | Expected Impact | Investment | |---|---|---|---| [6 strategic initiatives] --- ## Resource Requirements | Phase | Engineering | Design | Business | Duration | |---|---|---|---|---| | Phase 1 | [X] FTE | [X] FTE | [X] FTE | [X] weeks | [Per phase] ## Technology Decision Log | Decision | Options Considered | Chosen | Rationale | |---|---|---|---| [5-8 key technical decisions made during architecture phase — reference Architecture Blueprint] ---END:roadmap_md--- ---BEGIN:readme_md--- # ${inf.name} > [One compelling line — benefit-driven, specific] **👉 New here? Start with [START-HERE.md](./START-HERE.md) for setup instructions.** [![Deploy](https://img.shields.io/badge/Deployed-Vercel-black?logo=vercel)](https://${inf.repo}.vercel.app) [![Built with Dynasty](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built%20with-Dynasty%20Launcher-C9A84C)](https://yourdeputy.com) --- ## The Problem [2-3 sentences: The specific pain this solves. Use numbers — "X professionals spend Y hours/week on Z"] ## The Solution [2-3 sentences: What this product does differently.
