Specific outcome — "Reduces X from Y hours to Z minutes"] ## Architecture [Text-based architecture description:] \`\`\` Client (Next.js) → API Routes → Business Logic → Database (Neon Postgres) ↓ ↓ Clerk Auth Stripe Payments ↓ ↓ Dashboard ←────── Analytics (PostHog) \`\`\` ## Quick Start \`\`\`bash # Clone and setup gh repo clone ${ORG}/${inf.repo} cd ${inf.repo} cp .env.example .env.local # Fill in environment variables (see .env.example for descriptions) # Start backend docker-compose up -d # Start frontend npm install && npm run dev \`\`\` **Or use an AI coding agent:** \`\`\`bash gh repo clone ${ORG}/${inf.repo} && cd ${inf.repo} && claude # Claude Code gh repo clone ${ORG}/${inf.repo} && cd ${inf.repo} && pi # Pi Agent \`\`\` ## Key Documents | Document | Purpose | |---|---| | [SPEC.md](./SPEC.md) | Product requirements and feature spec | | [DESIGN.md](./DESIGN.md) | Visual design system and component specs | | [ROADMAP.md](./ROADMAP.md) | Development phases and timeline | | [BUSINESS-SYSTEM.md](./BUSINESS-SYSTEM.md) | Strategic business architecture | | [docs/API-CONTRACTS.md](./docs/API-CONTRACTS.md) | API endpoint specifications | | [docs/DATA-MODEL.md](./docs/DATA-MODEL.md) | Database schema documentation | ## Tech Stack [Concise list: Framework, Auth, Database, Payments, Hosting, UI Library, Monitoring] ## Deployment ### Vercel (Recommended) \`\`\`bash npx vercel --prod \`\`\` Or connect this GitHub repo in the [Vercel Dashboard](https://vercel.com/new) for automatic deploys on every push. ### Environment Variables Add all variables from \`.env.example\` to your Vercel project: **Vercel Dashboard → Settings → Environment Variables** ### Post-Deploy Checklist 1. Set env vars in Vercel (see \`.env.example\`) 2. Configure Clerk auth (see [MANUAL-ACTIONS.md](./MANUAL-ACTIONS.md)) 3. Set up Stripe webhooks pointing to \`your-domain/api/webhooks/stripe\` 4. Run database migrations: \`npx prisma db push\` (if using Prisma) 5. Verify: visit your domain and test sign-up flow ## Contributing See [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) for coding conventions and [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) for AI agent instructions. --- *Built by [Dynasty Empire](https://dynastyempire.com) · Generated by [Your Deputy](https://yourdeputy.com)* ---END:readme_md--- ---BEGIN:manual_md--- # Manual Configuration Guide — ${inf.name} > This guide covers everything you need to go from repository to production. --- ## 0. Deploy to Production \`\`\`bash # Clone your repo gh repo clone ${ORG}/${inf.repo} cd ${inf.repo} # Set up environment cp .env.example .env.local # Fill in values from the service dashboards below # Install and test locally npm install npm run dev # Visit http://localhost:3000 to verify # Deploy to Vercel npx vercel --prod # Or connect this repo at https://vercel.com/new for auto-deploy \`\`\` **After deploying:** Add all .env.local variables to Vercel Dashboard → Settings → Environment Variables. --- ${(inf.manual_svcs||[]).length > 0 ? (inf.manual_svcs||[]).map((k, i) => `## ${i + 1}. ${k.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + k.slice(1)} Configuration **Why needed:** [Specific reason this service requires manual setup for ${inf.name}] **Time required:** ~[X] minutes ### Steps: | # | Action | URL / Location | Expected Result | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | [Navigate to...] | [Exact URL] | [What you should see] | | 2 | [Click/Configure...] | [Dashboard section] | [Expected outcome] | | 3 | [Copy value to...] | .env.local → [VAR_NAME] | [Verification] | **Verification:** [How to confirm this is working — "Visit /api/health and confirm [service] returns 'connected'"] ---`).join('\n\n') : '✅ No manual actions required for this run. All in-scope services completed through automation for this build profile/tier.'} ---END:manual_md--- ---BEGIN:env_ex--- # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Environment Configuration — ${inf.name} # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Copy this file to .env.local and fill in all values. # Never commit .env.local — it contains secrets. # See MANUAL-ACTIONS.md for where to find each value. # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # ── Database (Neon Postgres) ───────────────────────────────── # Find at: https://console.neon.tech → Project → Connection Details DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:password@host:5432/dbname # POSTGRES_URL is the same — some ORMs prefer this name POSTGRES_URL=postgresql://user:password@host:5432/dbname # ── Authentication ─────────────────────────────────────────── # Find at: https://dashboard.clerk.com → API Keys NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_... CLERK_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_... # ── Payments (Stripe) ──────────────────────────────────────── # Find at: https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_... STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_... STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_... # ── Infrastructure ─────────────────────────────────────────── NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000 NODE_ENV=development # ── Third-party Services ───────────────────────────────────── # [List each service this project needs with dashboard URL comment] # ── Application Secrets ────────────────────────────────────── # Generate: openssl rand -hex 32 SECRET_KEY=generate-me ---END:env_ex--- `, ['spec_md','roadmap_md','readme_md','manual_md','env_ex'], 14000, 'g2'); setP('g2','ok',`${Object.values(dc).filter(v=>v).length} docs ready`); await compactPhase('spec', dc); }catch(e){setP('g2','er',e.message);throw e;} updateEduCard('g3'); addP('g3','Generating DATA-MODEL + API-CONTRACTS + KB-OUTLINES','','⬡ Supporting Docs');setP('g3','run'); updateGlobalProgress(3, 12, 'Phase 3 of 14 \u00b7 Technical Docs'); let ds;try{ ds=await ai(`You are a senior software architect writing technical documentation for an engineering team. Every table must have real column names and types. Every API endpoint must have request/response schemas. No placeholders. Reference the Architecture Blueprint decisions. Return ONLY in delimiter format. No preamble. ${cx(desc,inf)} ---BEGIN:data_model--- # Data Architecture Specification — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire Data Layer | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', {year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric'})} --- ## 1. Architecture Overview [1 paragraph: Database choice rationale (Neon Postgres), ORM (Drizzle), connection pooling strategy, and how the data model supports the business requirements in SPEC.md.] --- ## 2. Entity Specifications [For EACH entity (6-10 entities):] ### 2.[N] [entity_name] Table **Purpose:** [1 sentence — what business concept this represents] | Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Constraints | Description | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | id | uuid | NO | gen_random_uuid() | PRIMARY KEY | Unique identifier | | created_at | timestamptz | NO | now() | | Record creation time | | updated_at | timestamptz | NO | now() | | Last modification time | [All columns with real names, types, and constraints] **Indexes:** - \`idx_[table]_[column]\` on ([column]) — [reason: "frequently queried by this field"] [List all needed indexes with rationale] **Relationships:** - belongs_to [other_entity] via [foreign_key] - has_many [other_entity] (cascade delete: [yes/no]) --- ## 3. Entity Relationship Summary | From | Relationship | To | Cardinality | Cascade | |---|---|---|---|---| [All relationships in one table] --- ## 4. Query Patterns | Query | Frequency | Tables | Index Used | Estimated Latency | |---|---|---|---|---| | [Common query description] | [High/Med/Low] | [Tables touched] | [Index name] | <[X]ms | [5-8 common query patterns the application will need] --- ## 5. Migration Strategy - **Tool:** Drizzle Kit (\`drizzle-kit generate\` + \`drizzle-kit push\`) - **Zero-downtime:** Add columns as nullable first, backfill, then add constraints - **Rollback:** Keep previous migration files; use \`drizzle-kit drop\` if needed - **Seeding:** \`scripts/seed.ts\` for development data --- ## 6. Data Retention & Privacy | Data Type | Retention Period | Deletion Method | GDPR Relevant | |---|---|---|---| | User PII | Until account deletion + 30 days | Hard delete | ✅ | | Transaction records | 7 years (tax compliance) | Anonymize after retention | ✅ | | Session data | 30 days | Auto-expire | ❌ | | Audit logs | 1 year | Archive to cold storage | ❌ | ---END:data_model--- ---BEGIN:api_contracts--- # API Specification — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire API Reference | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', {year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric'})} --- ## 1. Authentication | Method | Type | Location | Format | |---|---|---|---| | Primary | Bearer Token (Clerk JWT) | Authorization header | \`Bearer \` | | API Key | Static key | X-API-Key header | \`CROP-ADMIN-...\` (admin only) | **Token refresh:** Clerk handles automatically via session management. **Unauthorized response:** \`401 { "error": "unauthorized", "message": "..." }\` --- ## 2. Response Format All responses follow this structure: \`\`\`json // Success { "data": {...}, "meta": { "page": 1, "total": 100 } } // Error { "error": "error_code", "message": "Human-readable message", "details": {...} } \`\`\` --- ## 3. Core Endpoints [For EACH resource group (4-6 groups, 3-5 endpoints each):] ### 3.[N] [Resource Name] | Method | Path | Auth | Description | |---|---|---|---| | GET | /api/v1/[resource] | Required | List all (paginated) | | GET | /api/v1/[resource]/:id | Required | Get by ID | | POST | /api/v1/[resource] | Required | Create new | | PUT | /api/v1/[resource]/:id | Required | Update | | DELETE | /api/v1/[resource]/:id | Admin | Soft delete | **Query Parameters (GET list):** | Param | Type | Default | Description | |---|---|---|---| | page | integer | 1 | Page number | | limit | integer | 20 | Items per page (max 100) | | sort | string | created_at | Sort field | | order | string | desc | asc or desc | | search | string | — | Full-text search | **Request Body (POST/PUT):** | Field | Type | Required | Validation | |---|---|---|---| [Fields with types and validation rules] **Response (200):** | Field | Type | Description | |---|---|---| [Response fields] --- ## 4. Webhook Events (if applicable) | Event | Trigger | Payload | |---|---|---| | [event.name] | [When this fires] | [Key fields in payload] | --- ## 5. Rate Limiting | Endpoint Group | Limit | Window | Exceeded Response | |---|---|---|---| | Public endpoints | 60 requests | 1 minute | 429 + Retry-After header | | Authenticated | 300 requests | 1 minute | 429 | | Admin | 1000 requests | 1 minute | 429 | --- ## 6. Versioning - Current version: v1 - Version in URL path: \`/api/v1/...\` - Breaking changes increment version number - Previous versions supported for 6 months after deprecation ---END:api_contracts--- ---BEGIN:kb_outlines--- # Content Strategy & Knowledge Base — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire Content Plan | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', {year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric'})} --- ## Content Strategy Overview [1 paragraph: SEO-first content approach targeting [primary keyword cluster]. Goal: establish topical authority in [niche] within 6 months. Reference NeuronWriter keyword data if available.] --- ## Knowledge Base Articles (25 Outlines) [For EACH of 25 articles:] ### [N]. [Article Title — keyword-optimized] **Target Keyword:** [primary keyword] | **Difficulty:** [Low/Med/High] | **Search Volume:** [est. monthly] **Word Count Target:** [800-2000 depending on topic] **Intent:** [Informational / Commercial / Transactional] **Outline:** 1. [H2: Introduction/Definition — 150 words] 2. [H2: Core concept — 300 words] 3. [H2: How-to / Implementation — 400 words] 4. [H2: Common mistakes / FAQ — 200 words] 5. [H2: Conclusion + CTA — 100 words] **Internal Links:** → [Article #X], [Article #Y] **CTA:** [Specific conversion action relevant to this topic] --- [Group articles into 5 topic clusters of 5 articles each. Each cluster has a pillar page + 4 supporting articles.] ### Topic Cluster Map | Cluster | Pillar Article | Supporting Articles | Business Goal | |---|---|---|---| | [Cluster 1 name] | Article #[N] | #[N], #[N], #[N], #[N] | [Awareness/Conversion/Retention] | [5 clusters] ---END:kb_outlines--- `, ['data_model','api_contracts','kb_outlines'], 8000); setP('g3','ok','Technical docs ready'); }catch(e){setP('g3','er',e.message);throw e;} const scorecardContext = V2_SCORECARD ? ` DYNASTY VIABILITY SCORECARD (embed this at the top of BUSINESS-SYSTEM.md): Composite Score: ${V2_SCORECARD.composite}/10 — ${V2_SCORECARD.verdict} ${Object.entries(V2_SCORECARD.scores).map(([k,v]) => `${k}: ${v.score}/10 — ${v.rationale}`).join('\n')} Risk: ${V2_SCORECARD.risk_summary} Opportunity: ${V2_SCORECARD.opportunity_summary} FRAMEWORK ANALYSES TO INCLUDE: ${Object.entries(V2_SCORECARD.framework_analyses || {}).map(([k,v]) => `## ${k.replace(/_/g,' ').toUpperCase()}\n${v}`).join('\n\n')} ` : ''; updateEduCard('g4a'); addP('g4a','Generating comprehensive business intelligence suite','','⬡ Business Intelligence');setP('g4a','run'); updateGlobalProgress(4, 12, 'Phase 4 of 14 \u00b7 Business Intelligence Suite'); let bz1;try{ bz1=await ai(`You are a senior partner at McKinsey & Company preparing a board-ready strategic analysis that will be delivered to the CEO and investors. This document must be indistinguishable from a $50,000 consulting engagement. QUALITY STANDARDS (non-negotiable): - Every paragraph must contain at least one specific number, percentage, or dollar figure - Every strategic claim must be supported with evidence or structured logic - Use McKinsey's MECE framework (Mutually Exclusive, Collectively Exhaustive) for all categorizations - Include "so what" implications after every data table — don't just present data, interpret it - Financial projections must use bottoms-up methodology with explicit assumptions - Competitor analysis must name real companies and reference real pricing/features - Risk assessment must use probability × impact scoring with specific mitigation plans - Write in the authoritative, direct tone of a senior partner briefing a board of directors - NO hedging language, NO filler, NO AI clichés ("certainly", "it's worth noting", "dive in", "landscape") - EVERY section must be substantial — minimum 3 paragraphs or a detailed table FORMATTING STANDARDS (professional document appearance): - Start with a metadata block: title, date, prepared for, confidentiality notice - Use numbered sections (1. 2. 3.) with clear hierarchy (## for sections, ### for subsections) - Use tables for ALL comparative data, financial projections, and scoring matrices - Use blockquotes (>) for key insights and executive callouts - Use horizontal rules (---) between major sections - Use bold (**text**) for key metrics, findings, and recommendations - Include a Table of Contents at the top with section links - End each section with a "Key Takeaway" callout in blockquote format - End the document with an "Executive Summary" and "Recommended Next Steps" - Format financial figures consistently: $X,XXX for currency, X.X% for percentages - Use bullet lists for action items, numbered lists for sequential steps - Cross-reference other documents by name (REVENUE-MODEL.md, GTM-PLAYBOOK.md, SPEC.md) ${cx(desc,inf)} ${scorecardContext} Return in delimiter format ONLY. No preamble. No JSON. No backticks. ---BEGIN:biz_system--- # Strategic Business Architecture — ${inf.name} > **Prepared by:** Your Deputy Intelligence Engine > **Date:** ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', {year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric'})} > **Confidence Level:** [High/Medium based on data availability] > **Data Sources:** [List: Exa.ai competitive intelligence, NeuronWriter SEO data, Framework analyses, Market research] --- ## Executive Summary **Finding 1:** [Bold, specific insight with a dollar figure or percentage — e.g., "The addressable market for X in Y segment is $Z, growing at N% annually"] **Finding 2:** [Second bold insight about competitive positioning] **Finding 3:** [Third bold insight about unit economics viability] **Strategic Recommendation:** [One decisive sentence — what to build, for whom, priced how] --- ## 1. Strategic Positioning ### 1.1 Market Definition [2 substantial paragraphs defining the specific market segment. Include TAM estimate with methodology. Reference real competitors by name if available from research data. Cite any framework analysis results.] ### 1.2 Competitive Landscape | Competitor | Est. Market Share | Key Strength | Key Weakness | Our Differentiator | |---|---|---|---|---| [3-5 real competitors from research data. If no research data, use informed estimates with "est." prefix] ### 1.3 Positioning Statement > For **[target segment with size estimate]** who **[specific pain point]**, ${inf.name} is a **[category]** that **[primary benefit with measurable outcome]**. Unlike **[primary competitor]**, we **[key differentiator backed by architectural advantage]**. ### 1.4 Competitive Moat Assessment | Moat Type | Strength (1-5) | Timeline to Build | Evidence | |---|---|---|---| [Network effects, switching costs, data advantages, brand, regulatory, etc. — from 7 Powers analysis if available] --- ## 2. Value Architecture ### 2.1 Customer Jobs Analysis | Job Type | Description | Priority | Current Solution | Our Approach | |---|---|---|---|---| | Functional | [Core task they need done] | P0 | [How they do it today] | [How we do it better] | | Social | [How they want to appear to peers] | P1 | | | | Emotional | [How they want to feel] | P2 | | | ### 2.2 Pain-Solution Mapping | Pain Point | Severity (1-5) | Frequency | Current Workaround | Our Solution | Impact | |---|---|---|---|---|---| [5 pain points with measurable impact — "saves X hours/week" or "reduces cost by Y%"] --- ## 3. Revenue Architecture ### 3.1 Pricing Strategy & Rationale [1 paragraph explaining pricing philosophy — value-based, competitor-anchored, or cost-plus. Reference competitor pricing from research data.] | Tier | Name | Monthly | Annual (${'{'}20% off${'}'}) | Target Segment | Key Differentiator | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | Starter | [Name] | $[price] | $[price] | [Segment] | [What they get] | | Professional | [Name] | $[price] | $[price] | [Segment] | [+What] | | Enterprise | [Name] | $[price] | $[price] | [Segment] | [+What] | **Pricing rationale:** "[Tier 2] at $[X]/mo positions us [below/at/above] [competitor] ($[Y]/mo) while delivering [Z additional value]. Willingness-to-pay research suggests the optimal range for this segment is $[A]-$[B]." ### 3.2 12-Month Financial Projection | Month | New Cust. | Total Cust. | MRR | ARR | Gross Margin | EBITDA | Key Event | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | 1 | [N] | [N] | $[X] | $[X] | [%] | -$[X] | MVP launch | | 2 | | | | | | | | | 3 | | | | | | | [Milestone] | [...through month 12 with realistic S-curve growth] ### 3.3 Unit Economics | Metric | Value | Industry Benchmark | Assessment | |---|---|---|---| | Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) | $[X] | Median: $[Y] | [✅ Below / ⚠️ At / ❌ Above] | | Lifetime Value (LTV) | $[X] | | | | LTV:CAC Ratio | [X]:1 | Healthy: >3:1 | [✅/⚠️/❌] | | Payback Period | [X] months | Best-in-class: [Y]mo | | | Monthly Churn | [X]% | SaaS median: 5% | | | Net Revenue Retention | [X]% | Best-in-class: >120% | | | Gross Margin | [X]% | SaaS median: 70% | | ### 3.4 Sensitivity Analysis | Scenario | Monthly Churn | CAC | MRR Month 12 | Break-Even | |---|---|---|---|---| | 🟢 Best Case | [X-1]% | $[X-20%] | $[X+30%] | Month [N] | | 🟡 Base Case | [X]% | $[X] | $[X] | Month [N] | | 🔴 Worst Case | [X+2]% | $[X+40%] | $[X-40%] | Month [N] or never | --- ## 4. MVP Specification (${inf.mvp}) ### Ships (P0 Features) | # | Feature | User Story | Acceptance Criteria | Effort | |---|---|---|---|---| [6-8 features in "As a [user], I want [action] so that [outcome]" format with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria] ### Explicitly Excluded [4 items cut from MVP with reasoning — "Deferred to Phase 2 because X"] ### Done Definition "MVP is complete when [binary, measurable statement]." --- ## 5. Risk Matrix | # | Risk | Probability | Impact | Score | Mitigation | Owner | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | 1 | [Market risk] | [H/M/L] | [H/M/L] | [1-9] | [Specific action] | [Role] | [8-10 risks across market, technical, financial, operational categories. Sorted by score descending.] --- ## 6. Scale Plan | Milestone | Timeline | Revenue | Customers | Key Initiative | Success Gate | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | Launch | Month 1 | $[X] MRR | [N] | MVP + first 10 customers | [Metric] | | Product-Market Fit | Month 3 | $[X] MRR | [N] | [Initiative] | [Metric] | | Growth | Month 6 | $[X] MRR | [N] | [Initiative] | [Metric] | | Scale | Month 12 | $[X] MRR | [N] | [Initiative] | [Metric] | | Expansion | Month 24 | $[X] MRR | [N] | [Initiative] | [Metric] | --- ## Appendix A: Key Assumptions | # | Assumption | Confidence | Impact if Wrong | Validation Method | |---|---|---|---|---| [8 explicit assumptions underlying the model] ## Appendix B: Methodology This analysis was generated using Your Deputy Intelligence Engine applying [N] strategic frameworks ([list applied frameworks]) to [M] real-time data sources. Competitive data sourced via Exa.ai semantic search. SEO demand signals from NeuronWriter. All financial projections use a bottoms-up model validated against industry benchmarks. ---END:biz_system--- ---BEGIN:revenue_model--- # Financial Model & Revenue Projections — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire Financial Analysis | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', {year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric'})} --- ## Investment Thesis [1 compelling paragraph: Why this business generates outsized returns. Reference the specific market gap, pricing power, and unit economics that make this work. This paragraph alone should convince an investor to keep reading.] --- ## 1. Pricing Architecture ### 1.1 Tier Structure | Tier | Name | Monthly | Annual | Target Segment | Included | Excluded | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---| [3 tiers with specific features per tier. Show what upgrades between tiers.] ### 1.2 Pricing Rationale "[Tier 2] is priced at $[X]/mo based on: (1) Competitor [Name] charges $[Y] for comparable features, (2) customer interviews indicate willingness-to-pay of $[A]-$[B], (3) at this price point, LTV:CAC exceeds 3:1 by month [N]." --- ## 2. Revenue Projections ### 2.1 Monthly Forecast (Year 1) | Month | New Customers | Churned | Net New | Total Active | Avg Revenue/Cust | MRR | ARR | Notes | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| [12 rows with realistic acquisition curve, churn applied, and milestone annotations] ### 2.2 Revenue Composition | Stream | % of Revenue | Monthly at Scale | Margin | Notes | |---|---|---|---|---| | Subscription (Tier 1) | [X]% | $[X] | [X]% | | | Subscription (Tier 2) | [X]% | $[X] | [X]% | Highest margin | | Subscription (Tier 3) | [X]% | $[X] | [X]% | | | Add-ons / Usage | [X]% | $[X] | [X]% | | --- ## 3. Unit Economics Deep Dive ### 3.1 Customer Acquisition Cost | Channel | Spend/Month | Leads | Conversion | Customers | CAC | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | ${inf.acquisition} | $[X] | [N] | [X]% | [N] | $[X] | | [Secondary channel] | $[X] | [N] | [X]% | [N] | $[X] | | **Blended** | **$[X]** | **[N]** | **[X]%** | **[N]** | **$[X]** | ### 3.2 Lifetime Value - Average monthly revenue per customer: $[X] - Gross margin: [X]% - Monthly churn rate: [X]% - Average customer lifetime: [X] months (1/churn) - **LTV = $[X] × [X]% × [X] months = $[X]** - **LTV:CAC = [X]:1** [✅ Healthy / ⚠️ Monitor / ❌ Unsustainable] ### 3.3 Payback Period - Monthly gross profit per customer: $[X] - CAC: $[X] - **Payback = [X] months** [Target: <12 months] --- ## 4. Expense Model | Category | Month 1 | Month 6 | Month 12 | Notes | |---|---|---|---|---| | Hosting (Vercel/Neon) | $[X] | $[X] | $[X] | Scales with users | | SaaS Tools | $[X] | $[X] | $[X] | Fixed until [threshold] | | Marketing/Acquisition | $[X] | $[X] | $[X] | [X]% of MRR | | Labor/Contractors | $[X] | $[X] | $[X] | First hire at $[X]K MRR | | **Total Monthly Burn** | **$[X]** | **$[X]** | **$[X]** | | | **EBITDA** | **-$[X]** | **$[X]** | **$[X]** | Break-even: Month [N] | --- ## 5. Milestones & Funding Requirements | Milestone | MRR Target | Timeline | Cash Needed | Use of Funds | |---|---|---|---|---| | $1K MRR | $1,000 | Month [X] | $[X] | Product + first customers | | $5K MRR | $5,000 | Month [X] | $[X] | Scale acquisition channel | | $10K MRR | $10,000 | Month [X] | $[X] | First hire | | $25K MRR | $25,000 | Month [X] | | Self-sustaining | --- ## 6. Key Risks to Financial Model | Risk | Impact on Revenue | Probability | Mitigation | |---|---|---|---| [5 specific financial risks with quantified impact] --- ## Appendix: Assumptions Table | # | Assumption | Value Used | Range | Source | |---|---|---|---|---| [Every numeric assumption listed with its source and sensitivity range] ---END:revenue_model--- ---BEGIN:gtm_playbook--- # Go-to-Market Execution Plan — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire GTM Strategy | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', {year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric'})} --- ## GTM Thesis [1 paragraph: WHY this acquisition channel wins for this specific product. Reference the target audience's behavior, the channel's cost structure, and the expected conversion funnel. Be specific — "LinkedIn outbound wins because [target persona] spends [X] hours/week on LinkedIn and responds to [type of message] at [X]% rate."] --- ## 1. Acquisition Funnel Model | Stage | Volume | Conversion | Source | |---|---|---|---| | Impressions/Reach | [N]/month | — | ${inf.acquisition} | | Clicks/Visits | [N] | [X]% CTR | | | Leads (email/signup) | [N] | [X]% | | | Qualified Leads | [N] | [X]% | | | Trials/Demos | [N] | [X]% | | | Paid Customers | [N] | [X]% | | | **CAC** | **$[X]** | | Monthly spend / customers | --- ## 2. Channel Strategy ### 2.1 Primary: ${inf.acquisition} **Why this channel:** [2-3 sentences with data — audience size, cost benchmarks, competitor usage] **Budget:** $[X]/month **Expected output:** [N] customers/month at $[X] CAC ### 2.2 Secondary Channels | Channel | Role | Budget | Expected CAC | Timeline | |---|---|---|---|---| | [Channel 2] | [Growth/Retention/Brand] | $[X] | $[X] | Month [N] | | [Channel 3] | [Growth/Retention/Brand] | $[X] | $[X] | Month [N] | --- ## 3. 90-Day Sprint Plan ### Week 1: Foundation | Day | Action | Deliverable | Success Metric | |---|---|---|---| | Mon | [Specific action] | [Output] | [Metric] | | Tue | | | | | Wed | | | | | Thu | | | | | Fri | | | | ### Weeks 2-4: Launch Sprint | Week | Focus | Key Activities | Target KPI | |---|---|---|---| | 2 | [Focus area] | [3 activities] | [Metric] | | 3 | | | | | 4 | | | | ### Weeks 5-8: Optimization [Weekly objectives with A/B test plan and iteration criteria] ### Weeks 9-12: Scale [Growth targets with channel expansion plan] --- ## 4. Messaging Framework ### 4.1 Core Narrative **The Problem (10 words):** [Crisp problem statement] **The Solution (10 words):** [Clear value prop] **The Proof (10 words):** [Credibility signal — speed, cost, results] ### 4.2 Copy Assets **Homepage Hero:** > Headline: [8 words max, benefit-driven] > Subheadline: [15 words max, specific outcome] > CTA: [Action verb + outcome — e.g., "Start saving 10 hours/week →"] **Email Subject Lines (5 tested variants):** 1. [Curiosity hook] 2. [Pain point] 3. [Social proof] 4. [Urgency] 5. [Direct benefit] **Cold Outreach Template:** Subject: [Subject] "Hi [Name], [1 sentence personalized observation]. [1 sentence problem statement]. [1 sentence how we solve it differently]. [1 sentence social proof or data]. Worth a 15-minute call? [CTA]" --- ## 5. Sales Playbook ### 5.1 Qualification Framework (BANT) | Criteria | Question | Good Answer | Disqualifier | |---|---|---|---| | Budget | "[Question]" | [Answer] | [Red flag] | | Authority | "[Question]" | | | | Need | "[Question]" | | | | Timeline | "[Question]" | | | ### 5.2 Objection Handling | Objection | Response Strategy | Key Phrase | |---|---|---| | "Too expensive" | [Reframe to ROI] | "[Exact words]" | | "Not the right time" | [Create urgency with data] | "[Exact words]" | | "We already have a solution" | [Differentiate on specific feature] | "[Exact words]" | | "Need to think about it" | [Offer low-commitment next step] | "[Exact words]" | | "Can you send more info?" | [Redirect to meeting] | "[Exact words]" | --- ## 6. Comms Stack Configuration ### CallScaler - **Trigger:** [When to use outbound calls] - **Script:** [30-second opener + 3 discovery questions + pitch] - **Follow-up:** [Voicemail script + SMS follow-up timing] ### Insighto Voice Agent - **Inbound script:** [Auto-answer flow for inquiries] - **Qualification logic:** [Route to human vs self-serve based on criteria] ### SMS-iT Sequences | Message | Timing | Content | Goal | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | Immediate | [Welcome/confirmation] | Set expectation | | 2 | +24 hours | [Value-add content] | Build trust | | 3 | +72 hours | [Soft CTA] | Convert | --- ## 7. KPI Dashboard ### Leading Indicators (Weekly) | KPI | Target | Measurement | Alert Threshold | |---|---|---|---| | [Metric 1] | [Target] | [How measured] | [When to worry] | | [Metric 2] | [Target] | | | ### Lagging Indicators (Monthly) | KPI | Target | Measurement | Benchmark | |---|---|---|---| | MRR | $[X] | Stripe dashboard | [Industry benchmark] | | [Metric 2] | [Target] | | | ### North Star Metric **[Metric]** — because [1 sentence explaining why this metric captures the full health of the business] ---END:gtm_playbook--- `, ['biz_system','revenue_model','gtm_playbook'], 16000, 'g4a'); // Generate INVESTOR-READINESS and M&A documents in parallel (separate AI calls for depth) setP('g4a','run','Generating investor + M&A analysis...'); const [investorDoc, maDoc] = await Promise.allSettled([ aiRaw(`You are a senior investment analyst preparing a comprehensive due diligence package. This must cover everything VCs, SBA loan officers, and Shark Tank investors evaluate. Business: ${inf.name} (${inf.type_label}) Description: ${desc} Revenue: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price} ${scorecardContext} Generate an EXHAUSTIVE investor readiness document covering: # ${inf.name} — Investor & Lender Readiness Package ## SECTION A: VENTURE CAPITAL READINESS ### What VCs Evaluate (address each): 1. Team — founder background, domain expertise, technical capability, gaps 2. Market — TAM/SAM/SOM with bottoms-up methodology, growth rate, timing 3. Product — current stage, differentiation, IP/moat, technology risk 4. Traction — users, revenue, growth rate, engagement, retention 5. Unit Economics — CAC, LTV, LTV:CAC, payback, gross margin, burn rate 6. Business Model — revenue streams, pricing power, scalability 7. Competition — direct/indirect, competitive matrix, sustainable advantage 8. Ask & Use of Funds — how much, allocation breakdown, milestones achieved 9. Return Profile — potential exit size, comparable exits, expected timeline 10. Deal Terms — valuation methodology, cap table, previous rounds ## SECTION B: SBA LOAN ELIGIBILITY ### SBA Requirements (address each): 1. Business Plan Summary — executive summary, company description 2. Market Analysis — industry overview, target market, competition 3. Organization & Management — legal structure, ownership, team bios 4. Products/Services — description, lifecycle, IP, R&D 5. Marketing & Sales Strategy — positioning, channels, pricing 6. Financial Projections — P&L (3 years), cash flow, balance sheet 7. Funding Request — amount, terms, use of funds, repayment plan 8. Collateral — assets, personal guarantees 9. Owner's Equity Injection — % owner investment vs loan amount 10. Industry Risk Assessment — regulatory, market, technology risks ## SECTION C: SHARK TANK / PITCH READINESS ### What Sharks Evaluate: 1. The Hook — 30-second elevator pitch 2. The Problem — specific, quantified pain point 3. The Solution — how it works, demo script 4. The Traction — sales, users, revenue, growth 5. The Numbers — revenue, costs, margins, valuation 6. The Ask — dollar amount, equity offered, valuation justification 7. The Moat — what prevents copying, defensibility 8. Customer Acquisition — how customers find you, CAC, channels 9. Founder Story — why you, personal investment, commitment 10. Use of Funds — exact allocation, milestones ## SECTION D: IDEO HUMAN-CENTERED DESIGN ASSESSMENT ### Desirability-Viability-Feasibility Framework: 1. Desirability — who wants this? Evidence of demand. User research findings. 2. Viability — business model sustainability. Revenue potential vs costs. 3. Feasibility — technical capability to build. Resource requirements. 4. Prototype Plan — what to build first, how to test, success metrics 5. User Journey Map — step-by-step customer experience 6. Empathy Map — what users think, feel, see, hear, say, do ## SECTION E: M&A / EXIT READINESS ### Acquisition Package Requirements: 1. Business Overview — history, operations, key metrics 2. Financial Performance — 3-year P&L, revenue trends, EBITDA 3. Valuation — revenue multiples, EBITDA multiples, comparable transactions 4. Customer Analysis — concentration risk, top 10 customers, retention 5. Technology Assessment — architecture, tech debt, scalability 6. IP Portfolio — patents, trademarks, trade secrets, copyrights 7. Team & Key Person Risk — dependencies, retention plans 8. Legal/Compliance — contracts, liabilities, regulatory status 9. Growth Opportunities — expansion potential, new markets, product extensions 10. Integration Considerations — what an acquirer needs to know Write EVERY section with extreme detail. Minimum 3 paragraphs per sub-section. Use specific numbers, percentages, and dollar figures throughout. This document alone should justify a $10,000+ consulting fee.`, 8000, 'premium'), aiRaw(`Generate a comprehensive SBA Business Plan document for "${inf.name}" (${inf.type_label}). Revenue model: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price} ${scorecardContext} This must follow the EXACT format the SBA requires for loan applications: # ${inf.name} — SBA Business Plan ## 1. Executive Summary (1 page) Company name, mission statement, founding date, products/services overview, financial highlights, funding request summary. ## 2. Company Description (2 pages) Legal structure, ownership, history, mission/vision, goals (short-term and long-term), industry overview, competitive advantages. ## 3. Market Analysis (3 pages) Industry description and outlook, target market description with demographics, market size and growth projections, competitive analysis with 5+ competitors, regulatory environment, SWOT analysis. ## 4. Organization & Management (2 pages) Organizational structure, management team bios, board of advisors, staffing plan, legal structure, ownership breakdown. ## 5. Products & Services (2 pages) Detailed description, pricing model, product lifecycle, intellectual property, research and development plans. ## 6. Marketing & Sales Strategy (3 pages) Marketing strategy, sales strategy, pricing strategy, distribution channels, promotional activities, customer retention plan, marketing budget. ## 7. Financial Projections (5 pages) ### 7.1 Revenue Forecast (monthly for Year 1, quarterly for Years 2-3) ### 7.2 Expense Budget (detailed line items) ### 7.3 Profit & Loss Statement (3 years) ### 7.4 Cash Flow Projection (monthly Year 1) ### 7.5 Balance Sheet (3 years) ### 7.6 Break-Even Analysis ### 7.7 Key Financial Ratios and Benchmarks ## 8. Funding Request (1 page) Amount requested, purpose breakdown, terms desired, future funding needs, repayment plan. ## 9. Appendix Key assumptions, supporting data, relevant permits/licenses needed. Be extremely detailed and specific. Use real numbers based on the business model. This must pass SBA review.`, 8000, 'premium') ]); // Add the additional documents to the build files if (investorDoc.status === 'fulfilled' && investorDoc.value?.length > 500) { bz1.investor_readiness = investorDoc.value; } if (maDoc.status === 'fulfilled' && maDoc.value?.length > 500) { bz1.sba_business_plan = maDoc.value; } setP('g4a','ok',`Business intelligence suite complete (${Object.keys(bz1).filter(k => bz1[k]).length} documents)`); await compactPhase('business', bz1); }catch(e){setP('g4a','er',e.message);throw e;} // ── Parallel Execution: Agent System runs concurrently with Business wrap-up ── updateEduCard('g4b'); addP('g4b','Generating AGENT-SYSTEM + FAILURE-MODES','');setP('g4b','run'); updateGlobalProgress(5, 12, 'Phase 5 of 14 \u00b7 Agent System'); let bz2;try{ bz2=await ai(`You are a systems architect presenting to a CTO. Write with technical authority and operational precision. Every component must have measurable success criteria. No filler. No generic descriptions. Return in delimiter format ONLY. No preamble. ${cx(desc,inf)} ---BEGIN:agent_system--- # AI Operations Architecture — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire Automation Blueprint | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString("en-US", {year:"numeric", month:"long", day:"numeric"})} --- ## Automation Philosophy [1 paragraph: What is automated vs human-controlled. The principle guiding decisions. Reference specific Dynasty stack tools.] --- ## 1. Agent Orchestration Map ### System Data Flow User Input → Intake Agent → Idea Generator → Evaluator → [if viable] → Architect → MVP Builder → Proof Engine → GTM Agent → Sales Agent → Financial Model → Deal Structuring → Scale Plan --- ## 2. Agent Specifications [For EACH of the 11 agents provide a full specification table:] ### 2.[N] [Agent Name] Agent | Attribute | Detail | |---|---| | **Purpose** | [1 sentence — specific, measurable outcome] | | **Trigger** | [Exact condition that activates this agent] | | **Input** | [Specific data structures or files consumed] | | **Output** | [Specific deliverables produced] | | **Tool** | [Dynasty tool: CallScaler/Insighto/SMS-iT/SEO Machine/Stripe/n8n/Flint/Claude] | | **Success Metric** | [Quantifiable — "converts >X% of leads" or "generates files in 95% | <90% | PostHog | | Avg processing time | <30s | >60s | Application logs | | Error rate | <2% | >5% | Pulsetic | | Cost per run | <$0.50 | >$1.00 | Stripe usage | ---END:agent_system--- ---BEGIN:failure_modes--- # Risk & Resilience Framework — ${inf.name} > Dynasty Empire Operational Resilience | ${new Date().toLocaleDateString("en-US", {year:"numeric", month:"long", day:"numeric"})} --- ## Resilience Philosophy [1 paragraph: Graceful degradation over hard failure. Blast radius containment. Observability-first. Specific patterns used in this system.] --- ## 1. Severity Classification | Level | Name | Definition | Response SLA | Escalation | |---|---|---|---|---| | P0 | Critical | Service completely unavailable | 15 min | All hands + CTO | | P1 | Major | Core feature degraded | 1 hour | On-call engineer | | P2 | Minor | Non-core feature broken | 4 hours | Next available | | P3 | Low | Cosmetic or edge case | Next sprint | Backlog | --- ## 2. Failure Scenarios & Runbooks [For EACH of 10 failures:] ### 2.[N] [Failure Name] **Severity:** P[0-3] | **Detection:** [Specific monitor/log/metric] | **Blast Radius:** [What breaks] **Runbook:** 1. **Detect:** [What triggers the alert — specific metric + threshold] 2. **Triage:** [First 5 min — what to check, who to page] 3. **Mitigate:** [Immediate action to reduce user impact] 4. **Resolve:** [Root cause fix with specific technical steps] 5. **Verify:** [Health check confirming resolution] 6. **Post-mortem:** What happened → Timeline → Root cause → Action items Scenarios to cover: JWT/Auth failure, Database connection loss, Stripe webhook failure, Rate limiting, Redis unavailable, Third-party API timeout, Payment processing failure, File/storage failure, Email delivery failure, Auth provider outage. --- ## 3. Resilience Patterns | Pattern | Implementation | Applied To | |---|---|---| | Circuit Breaker | Open after 5 failures in 60s, half-open after 30s | External APIs | | Retry + Backoff | Exponential: 1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 30s max | Database, APIs | | Bulkhead | Separate connection pools per service | DB, Redis | | Graceful Degradation | Cached reads when source unavailable | All read paths | | Idempotency | Keys on all mutations | Payments, webhooks | | Health Checks | /health endpoint checking all dependencies | All services | --- ## 4. Monitoring & Alerting | Signal | Tool | Threshold | Action | |---|---|---|---| | Uptime | Pulsetic (5-min) | <99.5% | Page on-call | | Error rate | PostHog | >2% requests | Slack alert | | p95 latency | App metrics | >2000ms | Investigate | | DB connections | Neon dashboard | >80% pool | Scale | | Webhook failures | Stripe dashboard | 3+ consecutive | Page on-call | --- ## 5. Backup Strategy | System | Method | Frequency | Retention | Recovery Time | |---|---|---|---|---| | Database | Neon point-in-time recovery | Continuous | 7 days | <5 minutes | | Redis | RDB snapshots | Hourly | 24 hours | <2 minutes | | Code | Git tagged releases | Every deploy | Infinite | <1 minute (rollback) | | Secrets | Vercel encrypted env vars | On change | Versioned | Immediate | ---END:failure_modes--- `, ['agent_system','failure_modes'], 12000, 'g4b'); setP('g4b','ok',`Agent system + failure modes ready`); }catch(e){setP('g4b','er',e.message);throw e;} const bz={...bz1,...bz2}; // Merge bz1 + bz2 into bz // ── Strategy Pack gate: documents generated, skip code + deploy ── if (getUserTier() === 'strategy_pack' && !localStorage.getItem('dynasty_admin_token')) { updateGlobalProgress(12, 12, 'Strategy Pack complete'); const docCount = Object.keys(window._buildFiles || {}).length; const d = document.getElementById('dynasty-results'); const fileList = Object.keys(window._buildFiles || {}).sort().map(f => `
    Doc${escapeHtml(f)}
    `).join(''); d.innerHTML = `

    Strategy Pack — ${docCount} documents generated

    Your strategy, legal, financial, marketing, and operations documents are ready. Download below or upgrade to Foundation to generate production code and deploy to your domain.

    ${V2_SCORECARD ? '
    Viability: ' + V2_SCORECARD.verdict + ' — ' + V2_SCORECARD.composite.toFixed(1) + '/10
    ' : ''}

    Ready for the full build?

    Upgrade to Foundation ($1,997) and your $697 Strategy Pack fee is credited. Get production code, deployment, and the Day-1 Success Kit.

    Your Documents (${docCount})

    ${fileList}
    `; d.style.display = 'block'; document.getElementById('dynasty-results').scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' }); window._buildInProgress = false; window.onbeforeunload = null; if (window._buildTimerInterval) { clearInterval(window._buildTimerInterval); window._buildTimerInterval = null; } return; } updateEduCard('g5'); addP('g5','Generating FastAPI backend + Dockerfile + docker-compose','','⬡ Backend Code');setP('g5','run'); updateGlobalProgress(6, 12, 'Phase 6 of 14 \u00b7 Backend Code'); let be;try{ be=await ai(`GENERATE PRODUCTION-GRADE FASTAPI BACKEND. This must be deployment-ready code — not a tutorial example. Include: structured error handling with custom exception classes, request ID tracking via middleware, dependency injection for services, environment-based CORS configuration, graceful startup/shutdown with connection pool management, comprehensive health check (DB + Redis + external deps), API versioning prefix, and proper Python type hints throughout. Real Python code only. Delimiter format ONLY. No preamble. Project: ${inf.name} | Type: ${inf.type_label} | Revenue: ${inf.revenue_model} at ${inf.price} Stack: ${(inf.stack||[]).join(', ')} ---BEGIN:main_py--- #!/usr/bin/env python3 # ${inf.name} — FastAPI Backend from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends, Request, status from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware from slowapi import Limiter, _rate_limit_exceeded_handler from slowapi.util import get_remote_address from slowapi.errors import RateLimitExceeded from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, String, DateTime, Boolean, Integer, text from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, Session from jose import JWTError, jwt from passlib.context import CryptContext from loguru import logger import stripe, redis, os, uuid from datetime import datetime, timedelta from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr from dotenv import load_dotenv load_dotenv() # Config DATABASE_URL = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL") REDIS_URL = os.getenv("REDIS_URL", "redis://localhost:6379") SECRET_KEY = os.getenv("SECRET_KEY", "change-me") ALGORITHM = "HS256" ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE = 30 stripe.api_key = os.getenv("STRIPE_SECRET_KEY") # DB setup engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL) SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine) Base = declarative_base() # Redis r = redis.from_url(REDIS_URL, decode_responses=True) # Rate limiter limiter = Limiter(key_func=get_remote_address) # Password hashing pwd_context = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto") # App app = FastAPI(title="${inf.name} API", version="1.0.0") app.state.limiter = limiter app.add_exception_handler(RateLimitExceeded, _rate_limit_exceeded_handler) app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=["*"], allow_methods=["*"], allow_headers=["*"]) # Models class User(Base): __tablename__ = "users" id = Column(String, primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())) email = Column(String, unique=True, index=True) hashed_password = Column(String) is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True) created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow) plan = Column(String, default="free") [Define 2-3 more domain-specific models for ${inf.type_label}] Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine) # DB dependency def get_db(): db = SessionLocal() try: yield db finally: db.close() # Auth helpers def create_access_token(data: dict): expire = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE) return jwt.encode({**data, "exp": expire}, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM) def verify_token(token: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)): try: payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM]) email = payload.get("sub") if not email: raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Invalid token") user = db.query(User).filter(User.email == email).first() if not user: raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="User not found") return user except JWTError: raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Invalid token") # Pydantic schemas class UserCreate(BaseModel): email: EmailStr password: str class Token(BaseModel): access_token: str token_type: str # Logging middleware @app.middleware("http") async def log_requests(request: Request, call_next): logger.info(f"{request.method} {request.url.path}") response = await call_next(request) logger.info(f"Status: {response.status_code}") return response # Health check (comprehensive — checks all dependencies) @app.get("/health") def health(): checks = {"api": "ok", "version": "1.0.0"} try: db = SessionLocal() db.execute(text("SELECT 1")) db.close() checks["database"] = "ok" except Exception as e: checks["database"] = f"error: {str(e)[:50]}" try: r.ping() checks["redis"] = "ok" except Exception: checks["redis"] = "unavailable" checks["status"] = "healthy" if all(v == "ok" for v in checks.values() if v != "1.0.0") else "degraded" return checks # Structured error handler @app.exception_handler(Exception) async def global_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception): logger.error(f"Unhandled error: {exc}") return JSONResponse(status_code=500, content={"error": "internal_error", "message": "An unexpected error occurred", "request_id": getattr(request.state, 'request_id', 'unknown')}) # Request ID middleware import uuid as uuid_lib @app.middleware("http") async def add_request_id(request: Request, call_next): request_id = str(uuid_lib.uuid4())[:8] request.state.request_id = request_id response = await call_next(request) response.headers["X-Request-ID"] = request_id return response # Auth endpoints @app.post("/auth/register", response_model=Token) @limiter.limit("10/minute") async def register(request: Request, user: UserCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)): existing = db.query(User).filter(User.email == user.email).first() if existing: raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Email already registered") db_user = User(email=user.email, hashed_password=pwd_context.hash(user.password)) db.add(db_user) db.commit() token = create_access_token({"sub": user.email}) return {"access_token": token, "token_type": "bearer"} @app.post("/auth/login", response_model=Token) @limiter.limit("10/minute") async def login(request: Request, user: UserCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)): db_user = db.query(User).filter(User.email == user.email).first() if not db_user or not pwd_context.verify(user.password, db_user.hashed_password): raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Invalid credentials") token = create_access_token({"sub": user.email}) return {"access_token": token, "token_type": "bearer"} [Implement 4 core domain endpoints specific to ${inf.type_label} — GET list, GET detail, POST create, PUT update] # Stripe webhook @app.post("/webhooks/stripe") async def stripe_webhook(request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)): payload = await request.body() sig = request.headers.get("stripe-signature") try: event = stripe.Webhook.construct_event(payload, sig, os.getenv("STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET")) except (ValueError, stripe.error.SignatureVerificationError): raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid signature") if event["type"] == "checkout.session.completed": session = event["data"]["object"] user = db.query(User).filter(User.email == session.get("customer_email")).first() if user: user.plan = "pro" db.commit() return {"received": True} # Checkout @app.post("/billing/checkout") @limiter.limit("10/minute") async def create_checkout(request: Request, current_user: User = Depends(verify_token)): session = stripe.checkout.Session.create( payment_method_types=["card"], line_items=[{"price": os.getenv("STRIPE_PRICE_ID"), "quantity": 1}], mode="subscription", customer_email=current_user.email, success_url=os.getenv("NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL") + "/dashboard?upgraded=true", cancel_url=os.getenv("NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL") + "/pricing", ) return {"url": session.url} @app.on_event("startup") async def startup(): logger.info("${inf.name} API starting") @app.on_event("shutdown") async def shutdown(): logger.info("${inf.name} API shutting down") ---END:main_py--- ---BEGIN:requirements--- fastapi>=0.104.1 uvicorn[standard]>=0.24.0 sqlalchemy>=2.0.23 psycopg2-binary>=2.9.9 redis>=5.0.1 slowapi>=0.1.9 stripe>=7.6.0 python-jose[cryptography]>=3.3.0 passlib[bcrypt]>=1.7.4 loguru>=0.7.2 python-dotenv>=1.0.0 alembic>=1.12.1 pydantic[email]>=2.5.0 ---END:requirements--- ---BEGIN:dockerfile--- FROM python:3.11-slim AS builder WORKDIR /app COPY requirements.txt . RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt FROM python:3.11-slim WORKDIR /app COPY --from=builder /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin /usr/local/bin COPY . . EXPOSE 8000 HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s CMD curl -f http://localhost:8000/health || exit 1 CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000", "--workers", "2"] ---END:dockerfile--- ---BEGIN:docker_compose--- version: '3.9' services: app: build: ./backend ports: ["8000:8000"] environment: DATABASE_URL: postgresql://dynasty:\$\{POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-changeme_before_deploy\}@db:5432/${inf.repo?.replace(/-/g,'_')||'dynasty_db'} REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0 SECRET_KEY: ${'$'}{SECRET_KEY:-changeme} STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: ${'$'}{STRIPE_SECRET_KEY} STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: ${'$'}{STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET} depends_on: db: {condition: service_healthy} redis: {condition: service_healthy} restart: unless-stopped db: image: postgres:15-alpine environment: POSTGRES_DB: ${inf.repo?.replace(/-/g,'_')||'dynasty_db'} POSTGRES_USER: dynasty POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${'$'}{POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-changeme_before_deploy} volumes: [postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data] healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U dynasty"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 restart: unless-stopped redis: image: redis:7-alpine volumes: [redis_data:/data] healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 restart: unless-stopped volumes: postgres_data: redis_data: ---END:docker_compose--- `, ['main_py','requirements','dockerfile','docker_compose'], 7000); setP('g5','ok','Production backend code ready'); await compactPhase('backend', be); }catch(e){setP('g5','er',e.message);throw e;} // ── Phase 7: Frontend scaffold (full-stack deploy only) ──────────────── let feFiles = {}; if (V2_DEPLOY_TARGET === 'fullstack') { updateEduCard('g7'); addP('g7', 'Generating Next.js frontend scaffold', '', '⬡ Frontend'); setP('g7', 'run'); try { const fe = await ai(`GENERATE NEXT.JS 14 APP ROUTER FRONTEND SCAFFOLD. Production-ready TypeScript code. Use the design system from the project config. CRITICAL BUILD RULES (violations cause Vercel deploy failure): 1. ICONS: Use lucide-react for ALL icons (NOT @heroicons/react). Import only icons you actually USE. Example: import { ArrowRight, Check, Shield } from 'lucide-react' 2. UNUSED IMPORTS: NEVER import anything you don't use in the component. TypeScript strict mode treats unused imports as build errors. 3. PACKAGE.JSON: Must include ALL imported packages. Required deps: next, react, react-dom, lucide-react, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, typescript, @types/react, @types/node 4. GLOBALS.CSS: Must start with @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities; 5. LAYOUT.TSX: Must import './globals.css' — this file MUST exist 6. Do NOT use 'use client' unless the component has useState/useEffect/onClick handlers ${cx(desc, inf)} Design: surface=${inf.surface}, accent=${inf.accent}, fonts=${inf.fonts} Generate these files using ---BEGIN:key--- / ---END:key--- delimiters: ---BEGIN:layout_tsx--- // src/app/layout.tsx — Root layout with metadata, fonts, global styles // MUST import './globals.css' // Use next/font/google for fonts. Include proper and // Do NOT add 'use client' — layouts should be server components ---END:layout_tsx--- ---BEGIN:page_tsx--- // src/app/page.tsx — Landing page with hero, features, pricing, CTA // Use lucide-react for icons (NOT heroicons). Only import icons you USE. // Professional design matching the accent color and surface mode // Include at least: hero section, 3 feature cards, pricing section, footer ---END:page_tsx--- ---BEGIN:dashboard_tsx--- // src/app/dashboard/page.tsx — Authenticated dashboard // Use lucide-react for icons (NOT heroicons). Only import icons you USE. // Stats cards, recent activity list, quick actions ---END:dashboard_tsx--- ---BEGIN:globals_css--- // src/app/globals.css — MUST start with these 3 lines: // @tailwind base; // @tailwind components; // @tailwind utilities; // Then add CSS custom properties for the color system ---END:globals_css--- ---BEGIN:tailwind_config--- // tailwind.config.ts — Extended with project colors and fonts ---END:tailwind_config--- ---BEGIN:package_json--- // package.json — MUST include lucide-react (NOT @heroicons/react) in dependencies // Required: next, react, react-dom, lucide-react, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer // Required devDeps: typescript, @types/react, @types/node ---END:package_json--- ---BEGIN:next_config--- // next.config.js ---END:next_config--- ---BEGIN:tsconfig--- // tsconfig.json ---END:tsconfig--- `, ['layout_tsx', 'page_tsx', 'dashboard_tsx', 'globals_css', 'tailwind_config', 'package_json', 'next_config', 'tsconfig'], 8000, 'frontend'); feFiles = { 'src/app/layout.tsx': fe.layout_tsx, 'src/app/page.tsx': fe.page_tsx, 'src/app/dashboard/page.tsx': fe.dashboard_tsx, 'src/app/globals.css': fe.globals_css, 'tailwind.config.ts': fe.tailwind_config, 'package.json': fe.package_json, 'next.config.js': fe.next_config, 'tsconfig.json': fe.tsconfig, }; // ── Template compatibility: also write to [locale] paths ────────── // SaaS boilerplate templates use i18n routing: /[locale]/(marketing)/page.tsx // If we only write to src/app/page.tsx, the middleware redirects to the // locale path and serves the template's default content instead. // Writing to BOTH paths ensures Dynasty content appears regardless of routing. if (fe.page_tsx) { // Strip i18n-incompatible imports (getTranslations etc.) — Dynasty pages are self-contained const cleanPage = fe.page_tsx; feFiles['src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/page.tsx'] = cleanPage; } if (fe.dashboard_tsx) { feFiles['src/app/[locale]/(auth)/dashboard/page.tsx'] = fe.dashboard_tsx; } // ── Post-generation build safety checks ────────────────────────────── // globals.css: AI-generated version is unreliable. Overwrite with a known-good // Tailwind v3 stylesheet (package.json below pins tailwindcss ^3.4.1). Without // this file the Next.js build fails with "Cannot find module './globals.css'" // and the whole deploy serves a template or 404s. feFiles['src/app/globals.css'] = `@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities; :root { --background: 0 0% 100%; --foreground: 222.2 84% 4.9%; --accent: ${(inf.accent || '#C9A84C').replace('#', '')}; } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { :root { --background: 222.2 84% 4.9%; --foreground: 210 40% 98%; } } @layer base { * { border-color: hsl(var(--foreground) / 0.1); } body { @apply bg-white text-gray-900 antialiased; font-feature-settings: "rlig" 1, "calt" 1; } h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { @apply font-semibold tracking-tight; } } @layer utilities { .text-balance { text-wrap: balance; } } `; // Fix 3: Replace @heroicons/react with lucide-react in all TSX files for (const fpath of ['src/app/page.tsx', 'src/app/dashboard/page.tsx', 'src/app/layout.tsx', 'src/app/[locale]/(marketing)/page.tsx', 'src/app/[locale]/(auth)/dashboard/page.tsx']) { if (feFiles[fpath] && feFiles[fpath].includes('@heroicons/react')) { // Common heroicons v1→lucide mappings const iconMap = { 'ArrowRightIcon': 'ArrowRight', 'CheckIcon': 'Check', 'CheckCircleIcon': 'CheckCircle', 'ShieldCheckIcon': 'ShieldCheck', 'DatabaseIcon': 'Database', 'LightningBoltIcon': 'Zap', 'BoltIcon': 'Zap', 'CircleStackIcon': 'Database', 'PlusIcon': 'Plus', 'DocumentTextIcon': 'FileText', 'KeyIcon': 'Key', 'ChartBarIcon': 'BarChart3', 'ClockIcon': 'Clock', 'ExclamationCircleIcon': 'AlertCircle', 'XMarkIcon': 'X', 'Bars3Icon': 'Menu', 'UserIcon': 'User', 'CogIcon': 'Settings', 'HomeIcon': 'Home', 'StarIcon': 'Star', 'HeartIcon': 'Heart', 'MagnifyingGlassIcon': 'Search', 'EnvelopeIcon': 'Mail', 'PhoneIcon': 'Phone', 'GlobeAltIcon': 'Globe', 'CreditCardIcon': 'CreditCard', 'BuildingOfficeIcon': 'Building2', 'RocketLaunchIcon': 'Rocket', 'SparklesIcon': 'Sparkles', 'WrenchIcon': 'Wrench' }; let code = feFiles[fpath]; // Extract which icons are imported const heroImportMatch = code.match(/import\s*\{([^}]+)\}\s*from\s*['"]@heroicons\/react[^'"]*['"]/); if (heroImportMatch) { const importedIcons = heroImportMatch[1].split(',').map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean); const lucideIcons = importedIcons.map(icon => iconMap[icon] || icon.replace(/Icon$/, '')); // Replace import line code = code.replace(heroImportMatch[0], `import { ${lucideIcons.join(', ')} } from 'lucide-react'`); // Replace usage in JSX (e.g. → ) for (let i = 0; i < importedIcons.length; i++) { const regex = new RegExp(importedIcons[i].replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&'), 'g'); code = code.replace(regex, lucideIcons[i]); } feFiles[fpath] = code; } } } // Fix 4: Ensure package.json has lucide-react, not heroicons if (feFiles['package.json']) { try { const pkg = JSON.parse(feFiles['package.json']); if (!pkg.dependencies) pkg.dependencies = {}; pkg.dependencies['lucide-react'] = '^0.400.0'; delete pkg.dependencies['@heroicons/react']; // Ensure essential deps if (!pkg.dependencies['next']) pkg.dependencies['next'] = '14.2.35'; if (!pkg.dependencies['react']) pkg.dependencies['react'] = '^18.3.1'; if (!pkg.dependencies['react-dom']) pkg.dependencies['react-dom'] = '^18.3.1'; if (!pkg.dependencies['tailwindcss']) pkg.dependencies['tailwindcss'] = '^3.4.1'; if (!pkg.devDependencies) pkg.devDependencies = {}; if (!pkg.devDependencies['typescript']) pkg.devDependencies['typescript'] = '^5.3.3'; if (!pkg.devDependencies['@types/react']) pkg.devDependencies['@types/react'] = '^18.2.0'; if (!pkg.devDependencies['@types/node']) pkg.devDependencies['@types/node'] = '^20.11.0'; if (!pkg.devDependencies['postcss']) pkg.devDependencies['postcss'] = '^8.4.33'; if (!pkg.devDependencies['autoprefixer']) pkg.devDependencies['autoprefixer'] = '^10.4.17'; feFiles['package.json'] = JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2); } catch {} } // Fix 5: Do NOT modify layout.tsx globals.css import — template handles it // (Tailwind v4 templates import CSS differently) setP('g7', 'ok', `${Object.keys(fe).filter(k => fe[k]).length} frontend files ready`); } catch (e) { setP('g7', 'er', e.message); } // ── Generate template component overrides (project-specific) ────────── // The SaaS boilerplate template has generic components (Hero, Features, etc.) // We MUST override ALL of them with project-specific content. try { addP('g7b', 'Customizing all template components', '', '⬡ Template Override'); setP('g7b', 'run'); const tmplOverride = await ai(`Generate project-specific React components for a Next.js SaaS template. These REPLACE the generic boilerplate components. PROJECT: ${inf.name} DESCRIPTION: ${desc} TYPE: ${inf.type_label} PRICE: ${inf.price || '$49/month'} REVENUE: ${inf.revenue_model || 'subscription'} ACCENT COLOR: ${inf.accent || '#3B82F6'} CRITICAL RULES: 1. Each component must be SELF-CONTAINED — no external template imports 2. Use Tailwind CSS only. No imports from @/templates or @/features 3. Use lucide-react for icons (NOT heroicons) 4. Only import what you USE. TypeScript strict mode is enabled. 5. Every component must export a named const (not default export) 6. All content must be specific to "${inf.name}" — NO generic "SaaS Template" text Generate using ---BEGIN:key--- / ---END:key--- delimiters: ---BEGIN:hero_tsx--- // src/templates/Hero.tsx — Project hero section // Must include: headline about ${inf.name}, value proposition, 2 CTA buttons // Use the project description for subheadline text // Export: export const Hero = () => (...) ---END:hero_tsx--- ---BEGIN:features_tsx--- // src/templates/Features.tsx — 3-6 feature cards specific to ${inf.name} // Each feature: icon (lucide-react), title, description // Export: export const Features = () => (...) ---END:features_tsx--- ---BEGIN:pricing_tsx--- // src/templates/Pricing.tsx — Pricing section with ${inf.price || '2-3 tiers'} // Include feature lists per tier, CTA buttons // Export: export const Pricing = () => (...) ---END:pricing_tsx--- ---BEGIN:faq_tsx--- // src/templates/FAQ.tsx — 4-6 FAQ items specific to ${inf.name} // Accordion-style with question/answer pairs // Export: export const FAQ = () => (...) ---END:faq_tsx--- ---BEGIN:cta_tsx--- // src/templates/CTA.tsx — Call-to-action banner // Headline + subtext + button specific to ${inf.name} // Export: export const CTA = () => (...) ---END:cta_tsx--- ---BEGIN:footer_tsx--- // src/templates/Footer.tsx — Site footer // Company: ${inf.name}, links: Product, Docs, Pricing, Contact // Export: export const Footer = () => (...) ---END:footer_tsx--- ---BEGIN:navbar_tsx--- // src/templates/Navbar.tsx — Navigation bar // Logo: ${inf.name}, links: Features, Pricing, Docs, Sign In, Get Started // Export: export const Navbar = () => (...) ---END:navbar_tsx--- ---BEGIN:en_json--- // src/locales/en.json — Full English translation overrides // Replace ALL "SaaS Template" and "SaaS Boilerplate" text with ${inf.name} // Must be valid JSON with these top-level keys: // Index, Navbar, Hero, Features, Pricing, PricingPlan, FAQ, CTA, Footer, // Dashboard, DashboardIndex, SignIn, SignUp, DashboardLayout ---END:en_json--- `, ['hero_tsx', 'features_tsx', 'pricing_tsx', 'faq_tsx', 'cta_tsx', 'footer_tsx', 'navbar_tsx', 'en_json'], 8000, 'frontend'); // Apply template overrides if (tmplOverride.hero_tsx) feFiles['src/templates/Hero.tsx'] = tmplOverride.hero_tsx; if (tmplOverride.features_tsx) feFiles['src/templates/Features.tsx'] = tmplOverride.features_tsx; if (tmplOverride.pricing_tsx) feFiles['src/templates/Pricing.tsx'] = tmplOverride.pricing_tsx; if (tmplOverride.faq_tsx) feFiles['src/templates/FAQ.tsx'] = tmplOverride.faq_tsx; if (tmplOverride.cta_tsx) feFiles['src/templates/CTA.tsx'] = tmplOverride.cta_tsx; if (tmplOverride.footer_tsx) feFiles['src/templates/Footer.tsx'] = tmplOverride.footer_tsx; if (tmplOverride.navbar_tsx) feFiles['src/templates/Navbar.tsx'] = tmplOverride.navbar_tsx; // Nullify boilerplate components feFiles['src/templates/DemoBanner.tsx'] = 'export const DemoBanner = () => null;\n'; feFiles['src/templates/Sponsors.tsx'] = 'export const Sponsors = () => null;\n'; feFiles['src/templates/Logo.tsx'] = `export const Logo = () => ${inf.name.replace(/'/g, "\\'")};\n`; feFiles['src/components/DemoBadge.tsx'] = 'export const DemoBadge = () => null;\n'; // Nullify sponsor/boilerplate components feFiles['src/features/sponsors/SponsorLogos.tsx'] = 'export const SponsorLogos = () => null;\n'; feFiles['src/features/landing/LogoCloud.tsx'] = `export const LogoCloud = ({ children }: { children?: React.ReactNode; text?: string }) => null;\nimport React from 'react';\n`; feFiles['src/features/landing/StickyBanner.tsx'] = `import React from 'react';\nexport const StickyBanner = ({ children }: { children?: React.ReactNode }) => null;\n`; // Translation override if (tmplOverride.en_json) { feFiles['src/locales/en.json'] = tmplOverride.en_json; try { const frResp = await callAI(`Translate this JSON i18n file from English to French. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences. Keep all keys identical, translate only the string values:\n${tmplOverride.en_json}`, { max_tokens: 4000 }); const frText = frResp.replace(/```[a-z]*\n?/g, '').replace(/```/g, '').trim(); JSON.parse(frText); feFiles['src/locales/fr.json'] = frText; } catch (_) { feFiles['src/locales/fr.json'] = tmplOverride.en_json; } } setP('g7b', 'ok', `${Object.keys(tmplOverride).filter(k => tmplOverride[k]).length + 4} template components customized`); } catch (e) { setP('g7b', 'sk', e.message); } // ── g7c: Backend Integration — DB, API routes, dashboard, billing ──────── // This transforms the template from a visual shell into a working application try { addP('g7c', 'Generating backend integration (DB, API, dashboard)', '', '⬡ Backend'); setP('g7c', 'run'); const backend = await ai(`Generate backend integration files for a Next.js 14 SaaS application using the existing boilerplate template infrastructure. PROJECT: ${inf.name} DESCRIPTION: ${desc} TYPE: ${inf.type_label} PRICE: ${inf.price || '$49/month'} REVENUE: ${inf.revenue_model || 'subscription'} The template already has: Drizzle ORM (pgTable), Clerk auth, Stripe billing, next-intl i18n. You are EXTENDING the existing template — not replacing its infrastructure. CRITICAL RULES: 1. Use drizzle-orm/pg-core for schema (pgTable, text, serial, timestamp, etc.) 2. API routes go in src/app/api/ as route.ts files with NextResponse 3. Dashboard must use real data from API routes, not hardcoded content 4. Use 'use client' only for components with useState/useEffect 5. Import types from drizzle-orm, next/server, @clerk/nextjs as needed 6. Every file must compile with TypeScript strict mode — no unused imports Generate using ---BEGIN:key--- / ---END:key--- delimiters: ---BEGIN:schema_ts--- // src/models/Schema.ts — Drizzle ORM database schema // KEEP the existing organizationSchema and todoSchema from the template // ADD 2-4 new tables specific to ${inf.name} // Use: pgTable, serial, text, timestamp, bigint, boolean, integer from drizzle-orm/pg-core // Include proper indexes and relationships ---END:schema_ts--- ---BEGIN:api_route_ts--- // src/app/api/v1/route.ts — Main API endpoint // GET: returns project status/health // POST: handles the primary action for ${inf.name} // Use NextRequest/NextResponse from next/server // Include proper error handling and JSON responses ---END:api_route_ts--- ---BEGIN:api_data_route_ts--- // src/app/api/data/route.ts — Data CRUD endpoint // GET: fetch records with pagination // POST: create new record // Use NextRequest/NextResponse, return proper JSON ---END:api_data_route_ts--- ---BEGIN:dashboard_page_tsx--- // src/app/[locale]/(auth)/dashboard/page.tsx — Functional dashboard // Must show: stat cards with real numbers, recent activity table, quick action buttons // Use 'use client' and fetch data from /api/data on mount // Use useState and useEffect for data loading // Include loading states and error handling // Import useTranslations from next-intl for the page title ---END:dashboard_page_tsx--- ---BEGIN:appconfig_ts--- // src/utils/AppConfig.ts — App configuration with billing plans // Keep: locales, defaultLocale, localePrefix // Update: name to "${inf.name}" // Add: PLAN_ID enum and billingPlans array with Free/Pro/Enterprise tiers // Include Stripe price IDs as placeholders (price_xxx format) ---END:appconfig_ts--- ---BEGIN:env_ts--- // src/libs/Env.ts — Environment validation (ALL OPTIONAL for initial deploy) // Use @t3-oss/env-nextjs and zod // Make ALL env vars optional so the build succeeds without real keys // This ensures the app deploys immediately — owner adds real keys later via Vercel dashboard // CRITICAL: Every env var must be z.string().optional() or z.string().default('') ---END:env_ts--- ---BEGIN:env_example--- // .env.example — All environment variables needed // Group by: Auth, Billing, Database, Monitoring ---END:env_example--- `, ['schema_ts', 'api_route_ts', 'api_data_route_ts', 'dashboard_page_tsx', 'appconfig_ts', 'env_ts', 'env_example'], 8000, 'frontend'); if (backend.schema_ts) feFiles['src/models/Schema.ts'] = backend.schema_ts; if (backend.api_route_ts) { feFiles['src/app/api/v1/route.ts'] = backend.api_route_ts; } if (backend.api_data_route_ts) { feFiles['src/app/api/data/route.ts'] = backend.api_data_route_ts; } if (backend.dashboard_page_tsx) { feFiles['src/app/[locale]/(auth)/dashboard/page.tsx'] = backend.dashboard_page_tsx; feFiles['src/app/dashboard/page.tsx'] = backend.dashboard_page_tsx; } if (backend.appconfig_ts) feFiles['src/utils/AppConfig.ts'] = backend.appconfig_ts; // Always push a safe Env.ts that makes ALL vars optional (prevents build crashes) feFiles['src/libs/Env.ts'] = backend.env_ts || `import { createEnv } from '@t3-oss/env-nextjs'; import { z } from 'zod'; export const Env = createEnv({ server: { CLERK_SECRET_KEY: z.string().optional().default(''), STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: z.string().optional().default(''), STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: z.string().optional().default(''), DATABASE_URL: z.string().optional().default(''), LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN: z.string().optional().default(''), }, client: { NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: z.string().optional().default(''), NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: z.string().optional().default(''), NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: z.string().optional().default(''), }, runtimeEnv: { CLERK_SECRET_KEY: process.env.CLERK_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, DATABASE_URL: process.env.DATABASE_URL, LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN: process.env.LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN, NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL, }, }); `; // If AI-generated Env.ts has "required" checks, replace them with optional if (feFiles['src/libs/Env.ts'].includes('.min(1)') || feFiles['src/libs/Env.ts'].includes('z.string().min')) { feFiles['src/libs/Env.ts'] = feFiles['src/libs/Env.ts'] .replace(/z\.string\(\)\.min\(\d+\)/g, 'z.string().optional().default(\'\')') .replace(/z\.string\(\)(?!\.optional)/g, 'z.string().optional().default(\'\')'); } if (backend.env_example) feFiles['.env.example'] = backend.env_example; setP('g7c', 'ok', `${Object.keys(backend).filter(k => backend[k]).length} backend files generated`); } catch (e) { setP('g7c', 'sk', e.message); } } addP('dx','Fetching DYNASTY-DESIGN.md from master repo','','⬡ GitHub');setP('dx','run'); let dy=''; try{const r=await gh(`/repos/${ORG}/DYNASTY/contents/DYNASTY-DESIGN.md`);if(r.ok){const d=await r.json();dy=decodeURIComponent(escape(atob(d.content.replace(/\n/g,''))));}setP('dx','ok');} catch{setP('dx','sk','Skipped');} addP('cr',`Creating github.com/${ORG}/${inf.repo}`);setP('cr','run','From dynasty-project-template…'); // Authority site types push into DYNASTY-SITE-FACTORY instead of creating a standalone repo const authorityTypes=['auth-gen','auth-pest','auth-imm','auth-freight','auth-legal','auth-hvac','auth-med','landing']; isAuthority = authorityTypes.includes(inf.type_id||'') || V2_DEPLOY_TARGET === 'authority'; // Preset mapping based on project type const presetMap={ 'auth-pest':'rugged-trustworthy','auth-hvac':'rugged-trustworthy', 'auth-legal':'professional-legal','auth-imm':'immigration-directory', 'auth-freight':'bold-industrial','auth-med':'modern-medical', 'auth-gen':'warm-authority','landing':'warm-authority' }; const designPreset=presetMap[inf.type_id]||'warm-authority'; // Hex to HSL approximation for color overrides function hexToHslStr(hex){ hex=hex.replace('#',''); const r=parseInt(hex.substr(0,2),16)/255,g=parseInt(hex.substr(2,2),16)/255,b=parseInt(hex.substr(4,2),16)/255; const max=Math.max(r,g,b),min=Math.min(r,g,b); let h,s,l=(max+min)/2; if(max===min){h=s=0;}else{ const d=max-min;s=l>0.5?d/(2-max-min):d/(max+min); switch(max){case r:h=(g-b)/d+(gsetTimeout(r,3500)); } setP('cr','ok'); // ── Authority site: generate niche.config.ts ─────────────────────────── let nc=null; if(isAuthority){ updateEduCard('niche'); addP('niche','Generating niche.config.ts for authority site','');setP('niche','run'); try{ nc=await ai(`GENERATE a complete TypeScript niche configuration for an authority site. Return in delimiter format ONLY. No preamble. Project: ${inf.name} Niche: ${desc} Domain: ${inf.domain} Accent color: ${inf.accent} The config object requires these exact fields: site: { name, tagline, description, domain, foundedYear } branding: { primaryColor (HSL format), accentColor (HSL format) } seo: { defaultTitle, titleTemplate, defaultDescription, defaultImage, keywords (12+), author: { name, bio, credentials (array), email } } niche: { name, primaryKeywords (10+), secondaryKeywords (15+), topicClusters (5 clusters each with title/slug/description/keywords/priority) } navigation: { main (5-7 links with label+href), cta: { label, href }, footer (3-4 sections each with title + links array) } social: { twitter?, linkedin?, facebook?, instagram? } features: { newsletter: true, darkMode: true, search: true } directory: { enabled: true, name, description, categories (4-6 with name/slug/icon) } content: { heroTitle, heroSubtitle, heroCTA: { primary: { text, href }, secondary: { text, href } } } ---BEGIN:niche_config--- /** * NICHE CONFIGURATION — ${inf.name} * Generated by Your Deputy * Niche: ${desc.substring(0,100)} */ export const nicheConfig = { site: { name: "${inf.name}", tagline: "[compelling tagline for this niche — 8-12 words]", description: "[one sentence description for meta tags — 150-160 chars]", domain: "${inf.domain}", foundedYear: 2025, } }, branding: { primaryColor: "[HSL value matching ${inf.accent} — e.g. '262.1 83.3% 57.8%']", accentColor: "[HSL value for accent — complement to primary]", }, seo: { defaultTitle: "[Site name + core value prop — 50-60 chars]", titleTemplate: "%s | ${inf.name}", defaultDescription: "[150-160 char meta description with primary keyword]", defaultImage: "/og-image.jpg", keywords: [ "[10+ primary and secondary keywords for this niche]" ], author: { name: "Dynasty Empire", bio: "[2-3 sentence author bio establishing authority in this niche]", credentials: ["[credential 1]", "[credential 2]", "[credential 3]"], email: "info@${inf.domain}", socialLinks: { twitter: "https://twitter.com/dynastyempire", linkedin: "https://linkedin.com/company/dynastyempire" }, }, }, niche: { name: "[Specific niche name — e.g. 'Pennsylvania Business Compliance']", primaryKeywords: [ "[10-12 high-intent primary keywords for this exact niche]" ], secondaryKeywords: [ "[15-20 supporting long-tail keywords]" ], topicClusters: [ { title: "[Cluster 1 topic]", slug: "[slug]", description: "[description]", keywords: ["kw1","kw2","kw3"], priority: "high" }, { title: "[Cluster 2 topic]", slug: "[slug]", description: "[description]", keywords: ["kw1","kw2","kw3"], priority: "high" }, { title: "[Cluster 3 topic]", slug: "[slug]", description: "[description]", keywords: ["kw1","kw2","kw3"], priority: "medium" }, { title: "[Cluster 4 topic]", slug: "[slug]", description: "[description]", keywords: ["kw1","kw2","kw3"], priority: "medium" }, { title: "[Cluster 5 topic]", slug: "[slug]", description: "[description]", keywords: ["kw1","kw2","kw3"], priority: "low" }, ], }, navigation: { main: [ { label: "[Nav 1]", href: "/[path]", description: "[brief description]" }, { label: "[Nav 2]", href: "/[path]", description: "[brief description]" }, { label: "[Nav 3]", href: "/[path]", description: "[brief description]" }, { label: "[Nav 4]", href: "/[path]", description: "[brief description]" }, { label: "[Nav 5]", href: "/[path]", description: "[brief description]" }, ], cta: { label: "[Primary CTA text]", href: "/[path]" }, footer: [ { title: "Resources", links: [{ label: "[link]", href: "/[path]" }, { label: "[link]", href: "/[path]" }] }, { title: "Services", links: [{ label: "[link]", href: "/[path]" }, { label: "[link]", href: "/[path]" }] }, { title: "Company", links: [{ label: "About", href: "/about" }, { label: "Contact", href: "/contact" }] }, ], }, social: { twitter: "https://twitter.com/[handle]", linkedin: "https://linkedin.com/company/[handle]", facebook: "https://facebook.com/[handle]", instagram: "https://instagram.com/[handle]", youtube: "https://youtube.com/@[handle]", }, features: { newsletter: true, darkMode: true, search: true, comments: false, analytics: true, }, content: { heroTitle: "[Compelling H1 — include primary keyword — 6-10 words]", heroSubtitle: "[Supporting subtitle explaining the value prop — 15-25 words]", heroCTA: { primary: { text: "[Primary CTA button text]", href: "/[path]" }, secondary: { text: "[Secondary CTA text]", href: "/[path]" }, }, aboutPage: { title: "About ${inf.name}", content: "[2-3 paragraph about page content establishing authority, explaining the site mission, and building trust]", }, }, }; ---END:niche_config--- `, ['niche_config'], 7000); if(nc.niche_config && nc.niche_config.length > 100){ window._nicheConfig = nc.niche_config; setP('niche','ok','niche.config.ts generated'); } else { setP('niche','sk','Niche config generation incomplete — manual configuration needed'); } }catch(e){setP('niche','sk',e.message);} } const _v4BpFe = getBuildProfile(); if (V2_DEPLOY_TARGET === 'fullstack' && feFiles && feFiles['src/app/page.tsx']) { const _nm = String(inf.name || 'Project').replace(/`/g, '\\`'); if (_v4BpFe.verticalTool) { feFiles['src/app/tool/takeoff/layout.tsx'] = `import type { Metadata } from 'next';\nexport const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Takeoff assist — ${_nm}' };\nexport default function TakeoffLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\n return
    {children}
    ;\n}\n`; feFiles['src/app/tool/takeoff/page.tsx'] = `'use client';\nimport { useCallback, useEffect, useState, type ChangeEvent } from 'react';\nimport { AlertTriangle, FileUp, Plus } from 'lucide-react';\ninterface JobRow { id: string; created_at?: string; status?: string; title?: string }\nexport default function TakeoffAssistPage() {\n const [note, setNote] = useState('');\n const [jobs, setJobs] = useState([]);\n const [uploadMsg, setUploadMsg] = useState('');\n const [err, setErr] = useState('');\n const load = useCallback(async () => {\n setErr('');\n try {\n const r = await fetch('/api/vertical/jobs');\n const j = await r.json();\n if (j.ok && Array.isArray(j.jobs)) setJobs(j.jobs);\n else if (!j.ok) setErr(j.setup || j.error || 'DB not ready — apply docs/VERTICAL-TOOL-SCHEMA.sql');\n } catch { setErr('Could not reach /api/vertical/jobs'); }\n }, []);\n useEffect(() => { load(); }, [load]);\n async function createJob() {\n setErr('');\n try {\n const r = await fetch('/api/vertical/jobs', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ title: note.slice(0, 200) || 'Draft takeoff' }) });\n const j = await r.json();\n if (j.ok) { setNote(''); load(); }\n else setErr(j.setup || j.error || 'Create failed');\n } catch { setErr('Create failed'); }\n }\n async function onFile(e: ChangeEvent) {\n const f = e.target.files?.[0];\n if (!f) return;\n setUploadMsg('Uploading…');\n try {\n const fd = new FormData();\n fd.append('file', f);\n const r = await fetch('/api/vertical/upload', { method: 'POST', body: fd });\n const j = await r.json();\n if (j.ok && j.url) setUploadMsg('Stored: ' + j.url.slice(0, 48) + '…');\n else setUploadMsg(j.error || 'Upload unavailable — set BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN');\n } catch { setUploadMsg('Upload failed'); }\n e.target.value = '';\n }\n return (\n
    \n

    Takeoff assist

    \n

    Not a binding bid, engineered estimate, or code-compliant quantity survey. Output is an assistive workbook; a qualified operator must confirm quantities and pricing before quoting third parties.

    \n

    See docs/templates/VERTICAL-CONCRETE-TAKEOFF.md for niche prompt patterns.

    \n {err ?

    {err}

    : null}\n \n
